❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if
  needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion
  (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

❯ /model

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if
  needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth
   move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Baked for 16m 9s

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

✻ Baked for 16m 9s

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

❯ /model

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Let me load IWSDK planning guidance first, then draft the plan.

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }


  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):


  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

✻ Brewed for 1m 14sI'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

✻ Baked for 16m 9s

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local
  grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's
  local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the
  server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent
  via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all
   clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already
   placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net,
   not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking
   anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it
  is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center
  top of the interface there will be a timer. you can also set
  the time for the round at the top. when you press start round,
  it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round
  starts, some items will come to life, some items will be goals
  to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms
  of items and what not. add collision detection with teh user
  and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting,
  or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also
  want you to make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right
  one a gun. come up with this plan.
