      904 +        wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', key, item: withRole });
      905        }
      906
      907        function clearCell(key) {

      930 +      // ── Round timer ──────────────────────────────────────────
      931 +      // Authoritative timing lives on the server; portal displays a

      933 +      // Local tick is just for the visual clock — it never writes state.
      934 +      const startBtn     = document.getElementById('start-round-btn');
      935 +      const durationEl   = document.getElementById('round-duration');

      937 +
      938 +      let roundEndsAt = 0;          // ms epoch; 0 when not running
      939 +      let tickHandle  = null;
      940 +
      941 +      function fmtSeconds(s) {
      942 +        const safe = Math.max(0, Math.ceil(s));
      943 +        const mm = String(Math.floor(safe / 60)).padStart(2, '0');
      944 +        const ss = String(safe % 60).padStart(2, '0');
      945 +        return `${mm}:${ss}`;
      946 +      }
      947 +
      948 +      function renderTimer() {
      949 +        const remaining = roundEndsAt
      950 +          ? Math.max(0, (roundEndsAt - Date.now()) / 1000)
      951 +          : parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 0;
      952 +        timerDisplay.textContent = fmtSeconds(remaining);
      953 +
      954 +        timerDisplay.classList.remove('running', 'warning', 'critical');
      955 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      956 +          if (remaining <= 5)       timerDisplay.classList.add('critical');
      957 +          else if (remaining <= 10) timerDisplay.classList.add('warning');
      958 +          else                      timerDisplay.classList.add('running');
      959          }
      936 -        const btn = document.getElementById('transmit-btn');
      937 -        btn.textContent = '✓ Synced!';
      938 -        btn.style.background = '#10b981';
      939 -        setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = 'Transmit'; btn.style.background = ''; }, 1500);
      960 +
      961 +        if (roundEndsAt && remaining <= 0) {
      962 +          // Server will broadcast ROUND_END; stop updating locally.
      963 +          stopTick();
      964 +        }
      965 +      }
      966 +
      967 +      function startTick() {
      968 +        if (tickHandle) return;
      969 +        tickHandle = setInterval(renderTimer, 250);
      970 +      }
      971 +      function stopTick() {
      972 +        if (tickHandle) { clearInterval(tickHandle); tickHandle = null; }
      973 +      }
      974 +
      975 +      function setRoundRunning(running) {
      976 +        startBtn.textContent = running ? 'End Round' : 'Start Round';
      977 +        startBtn.classList.toggle('running', running);
      978 +        durationEl.disabled = running;
      979 +      }
      980 +
      981 +      durationEl.addEventListener('input', () => { if (!roundEndsAt) renderTimer(); });
      982 +
      983 +      startBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      984 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      985 +          // Currently running → ask server to end.
      986 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'host-stopped' });
      987 +        } else {
      988 +          const duration = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 30));
      989 +          durationEl.value = duration;
      990 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration });
      991 +        }
      992        });
      993
      994 +      renderTimer();
      995 +
      996        syncGrids();
      997      </script>
      998    </body>

      24  const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3001;
      25
      26  // ── Shared state ────────────────────────────────────────────
      27 -const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label }
      27 +const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label, role }
      28  const users = new Map();     // userId -> { position: {x, z, heading}, spaceId }
      29
      30 +// Round state. endsAt === 0 means no round in progress.
      31 +// Duration is stored for reference (e.g. portals joining mid-round).
      32 +const round = { endsAt: 0, duration: 0, timeout: null };
      33 +
      34 +function endRound(reason) {
      35 +  if (!round.endsAt) return;
      36 +  if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }
      37 +  round.endsAt = 0;
      38 +  round.duration = 0;
      39 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round ended (${reason})`);
      40 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason });
      41 +}
      42 +
      43 +function startRound(duration) {
      44 +  // Clamp to the same range the portal UI allows.
      45 +  const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(duration)));
      46 +  if (round.timeout) clearTimeout(round.timeout);
      47 +  round.duration = d;
      48 +  round.endsAt = Date.now() + d * 1000;
      49 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round started (${d}s, ends at ${new Date(round.endsAt).toISOString()})`);
      50 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration: d, endsAt: round.endsAt });
      51 +  // Server is authoritative: auto-broadcast ROUND_END when the timer fires.
      52 +  round.timeout = setTimeout(() => endRound('timeout'), d * 1000);
      53 +}
      54 +
      55  // ── Server setup ─────────────────────────────────────────────
      56  const server = createServer();
      57  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ server });

⏺ Phase 1 shipped on game-idea branch.

  What's in it

  - Start Round / End Round button (replaces Transmit)
  - Duration input (5–600s, default 30)
  - Live timer in header (green → amber → red-pulse as it expires)
  - Expanded palette — Weapons 🗡️ 🔫 / Goals ⭐💎🎯❤️  / Hazards 🔥🤖👻 / Decor
  - Role auto-tagged on every placement, carried through WS
  - Cells tinted by role: white halo = pickup, red outline = hazard
  - Server-authoritative rounds — absolute endsAt keeps all clients synced, auto-end on timeout

  Test it locally

  - Portal: https://localhost:8081/portal.html
  - Dev server + WS server already running

  ┌───────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Service      │          Account           │           Owner            │                     URL                      │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ GitHub repo       │ jin-dalrae (Rae's account) │ Rae                        │ github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Your GitHub login │ modestapproach             │ You                        │ —                                            │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Vercel            │ modestapproach's projects  │ You                        │ ar-app-jade.vercel.app                       │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Railway           │ donjikong's Projects       │ You (theodoreyd@gmail.com) │ ar-app-ws-production.up.railway.app          │
  └───────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  What this means

  - The code lives in Rae's GitHub repo. You have write access — you push as modestapproach, but commits are authored as theodoreyd@gmail.com (the email we fixed
  earlier).
  - The live websites are yours. Vercel and Railway are both under your personal accounts. Rae can't see or manage those deployments — they're not connected to
  her.
  - When you vercel deploy --prod, it uploads from your local folder to your Vercel account. It has no link to GitHub (that's why the "Connect to GitHub" step
  failed earlier — Vercel's GitHub app isn't installed on the jin-dalrae org).

  So the flow right now is

  1. You edit code locally
  2. git push → goes to Rae's GitHub repo (shared)
  3. vercel --prod → goes to your Vercel (only you see it)
  4. railway up → goes to your Railway (only you see it)

  If Rae wants the same live URLs, she'd need to either:
  - Get added as a member to your Vercel/Railway teams, or
  - Run her own vercel/railway deploys under her accounts (different URLs)

  If you want auto-deploy on push (instead of manually running vercel each time), someone with admin on jin-dalrae org needs to install the Vercel GitHub app on
  that repo. Then every push triggers a Vercel build automatically.

  Want me to set up your own fork so everything's cleanly yours, or leave it as-is (shared repo, your hosting)?

    - /portal.html — grid planner
    - /broadcast.html — broadcast view
  - WebSocket server (Railway): wss://ar-app-ws-production.up.railway.app

✻ Baked for 16m 9s

❯ /model
  ⎿  Set model to Opus 4.6 (1M context) (default)

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net, not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking anything.

  a timer. you can also set the time for the round at the top. when you press start round, it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round starts,
  some items will come to life, some items will be goals to win the round. we will design what the emoji's do in terms of items and what not. add collision
  detection with teh user and the emoji, then we can set this up to be item collecting, or bad guy collisions later, or collecting objectives. i also want you to
  make the left hand joystic a sword, and the right one a gun. come up with this plan.

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }

  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):

  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

  Answer those and I'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

⏺ Updated Plan — Pickups & Obstacles

  Visual distinction (critical for readability)

  Pickups (interactable) — render with a white billboarded disc/halo behind the emoji. Acts as affordance: "this is grabbable."

  Obstacles (dangerous/solid) — no halo, but get a subtle red flicker/glow to warn.

  Decor (cosmetic) — no halo, sprite as-is.

  Implementation: modify the emoji canvas to draw a soft white radial gradient circle before the emoji glyph. Or stack two sprites (cheaper: combine in one
  CanvasTexture).

  // In makeEmojiSprite — pickup variant
  ctx.beginPath();
  ctx.arc(64, 64, 58, 0, Math.PI * 2);
  const grad = ctx.createRadialGradient(64, 64, 10, 64, 64, 58);
  grad.addColorStop(0, 'rgba(255,255,255,0.95)');
  grad.addColorStop(1, 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)');
  ctx.fillStyle = grad;
  ctx.fill();
  // then draw emoji on top

  Revised role taxonomy

  ┌─────────────────┬────────────────┬────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Role       │     Emojis     │       Halo?        │                        Behavior                         │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ weapon-sword    │ 🗡️  ⚔️           │ white              │ Touch → player equips sword (spawns on left hand)       │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ weapon-gun      │ 🔫             │ white              │ Touch → player equips squirt gun (spawns on right hand) │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal            │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀    │ white              │ Touch → score +1, despawn                               │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ powerup         │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡       │ white              │ Touch → heal/boost                                      │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle-damage │ 🔥             │ none (red flicker) │ Pass-through allowed, damages per frame while inside    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle-solid  │ 🧱 🪵          │ none               │ Blocks movement (phase 2 with physics)                  │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy           │ 🤖 👻 💀       │ none (red)         │ Chases, damages on contact, killable                    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor           │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 🪑 │ none               │ Cosmetic only                                           │
  └─────────────────┴────────────────┴────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Weapon system changes

  No weapons on spawn. Empty hands until pickup.

  // On world.globals
  equippedLeft = signal<'sword' | null>(null)
  equippedRight = signal<'gun' | null>(null)

  // On sword pickup → equippedLeft.value = 'sword'
  // WeaponSystem subscribes; spawns/removes weapon mesh on gripSpaces.left

  If you drop your weapons (die, round ends, or a future unequip pickup), they despawn.

  Fire obstacle specifics

  // GameItem component gets damagePerSecond field
  // Fire: damagePerSecond = 20, radius = 0.4m

  // CollisionSystem per frame:
  for (const fire of queries.damageObstacles.entities) {
    const dist2 = headPos.distanceToSquared(firePos);
    if (dist2 < radius²) {
      playerHealth -= damagePerSecond * delta;
      // red screen vignette + damage sound
    }
  }

  Visual: 🔥 with an animated red glow sprite underneath; particles optional later.

  Updated role inference (portal side)

  Portal auto-tags role when placing by icon:

  const ROLE_BY_ICON = {
    '🗡️ ': 'weapon-sword', '⚔️ ': 'weapon-sword',
    '🔫': 'weapon-gun',
    '⭐': 'goal', '💎': 'goal', '🎯': 'goal', '🌀': 'goal',
    '❤️ ': 'powerup', '🍄': 'powerup', '⚡': 'powerup',
    '🔥': 'obstacle-damage',
    '🧱': 'obstacle-solid', '🪵': 'obstacle-solid',
    '🤖': 'enemy', '👻': 'enemy', '💀': 'enemy',
    // everything else → 'decor'
  };

  Palette should gain 🗡️  🔫 ⭐ 🔥 🤖 ❤️  if not already present. I'll add them when I touch the portal.

  Palette groups (updated)

  Pickups:  🗡️   🔫  ⭐  💎  ❤️   🍄
  Hazards:  🔥  🤖  👻
  Decor:    🌱  🪑  🪵  🏮  🖥️   🎵  🌀

  Flow summary

  1. Portal: designer places items (with auto-role).
  2. Portal: sets timer (e.g. 30s), hits Start Round.
  3. VR: items "come to life" — pickups pulse, enemies start AI, fire animates.
  4. VR player: has no weapons; runs to pick up 🗡️  for sword on left hand, 🔫 for squirt gun on right hand.
  5. Avoids 🔥 (or tanks damage), fights 🤖 with sword/gun, collects ⭐.
  6. Round ends: timer hits 0 OR all goals collected OR player dies.
  7. Final score broadcast; portal shows result.

      544 +      // ── Role inference ────────────────────────────────────────
      545 +      // Drives VR behavior: pickups get halos + equipment grant, hazards
      546 +      // damage player, enemies have AI + are killable, decor is inert.
      547 +      const ROLE_BY_ICON = {
      548 +        '🗡️ ': 'weapon-sword',
      549 +        '🔫': 'weapon-gun',
      550 +        '⭐': 'goal', '💎': 'goal', '🎯': 'goal',
      551 +        '❤️ ': 'powerup',
      552 +        '🔥': 'obstacle-damage',
      553 +        '🤖': 'enemy', '👻': 'enemy', '💀': 'enemy',
      554 +        // everything else → 'decor' (falls through)
      555 +      };
      556 +      function roleFor(icon) { return ROLE_BY_ICON[icon] || 'decor'; }
      557 +
      558 +      // Stamp role on each palette item's DOM so CSS tinting works.
      559 +      document.querySelectorAll('.item[data-icon]').forEach(el => {
      560 +        el.dataset.role = roleFor(el.dataset.icon);
      561 +      });

      930 +      // ── Round timer ──────────────────────────────────────────
      931 +      // Authoritative timing lives on the server; portal displays a
      932 +      // client-side countdown derived from the server's `endsAt` (ms epoch).
      933 +      // Local tick is just for the visual clock — it never writes state.
      934 +      const startBtn     = document.getElementById('start-round-btn');
      935 +      const durationEl   = document.getElementById('round-duration');
      936 +      const timerDisplay = document.getElementById('timer-display');
      937 +
      938 +      let roundEndsAt = 0;          // ms epoch; 0 when not running
      939 +      let tickHandle  = null;
      940 +
      941 +      function fmtSeconds(s) {
      942 +        const safe = Math.max(0, Math.ceil(s));
      943 +        const mm = String(Math.floor(safe / 60)).padStart(2, '0');
      944 +        const ss = String(safe % 60).padStart(2, '0');
      945 +        return `${mm}:${ss}`;
      946 +      }
      947 +
      948 +      function renderTimer() {
      949 +        const remaining = roundEndsAt
      950 +          ? Math.max(0, (roundEndsAt - Date.now()) / 1000)
      951 +          : parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 0;
      952 +        timerDisplay.textContent = fmtSeconds(remaining);
      953 +
      954 +        timerDisplay.classList.remove('running', 'warning', 'critical');
      955 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      956 +          if (remaining <= 5)       timerDisplay.classList.add('critical');
      957 +          else if (remaining <= 10) timerDisplay.classList.add('warning');
      958 +          else                      timerDisplay.classList.add('running');
      959          }
      936 -        const btn = document.getElementById('transmit-btn');
      937 -        btn.textContent = '✓ Synced!';
      938 -        btn.style.background = '#10b981';
      939 -        setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = 'Transmit'; btn.style.background = ''; }, 1500);
      960 +
      961 +        if (roundEndsAt && remaining <= 0) {
      962 +          // Server will broadcast ROUND_END; stop updating locally.
      963 +          stopTick();
      964 +        }
      965 +      }
      966 +
      967 +      function startTick() {
      968 +        if (tickHandle) return;
      969 +        tickHandle = setInterval(renderTimer, 250);
      970 +      }
      971 +      function stopTick() {
      972 +        if (tickHandle) { clearInterval(tickHandle); tickHandle = null; }
      973 +      }
      974 +
      975 +      function setRoundRunning(running) {
      976 +        startBtn.textContent = running ? 'End Round' : 'Start Round';
      977 +        startBtn.classList.toggle('running', running);
      978 +        durationEl.disabled = running;
      979 +      }
      980 +
      981 +      durationEl.addEventListener('input', () => { if (!roundEndsAt) renderTimer(); });
      982 +
      983 +      startBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      984 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      985 +          // Currently running → ask server to end.
      986 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'host-stopped' });
      987 +        } else {
      988 +          const duration = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 30));
      989 +          durationEl.value = duration;
      990 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration });
      991 +        }
      992        });
      993
      994 +      renderTimer();
      995 +
      996        syncGrids();
      997      </script>
      998    </body>

⏺ Now update the server to handle round messages:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 26 lines, removed 1 line
      24  const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3001;
      25
      26  // ── Shared state ────────────────────────────────────────────
      27 -const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label }
      27 +const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label, role }
      28  const users = new Map();     // userId -> { position: {x, z, heading}, spaceId }
      29
      30 +// Round state. endsAt === 0 means no round in progress.
      31 +// Duration is stored for reference (e.g. portals joining mid-round).
      32 +const round = { endsAt: 0, duration: 0, timeout: null };
      33 +
      34 +function endRound(reason) {
      35 +  if (!round.endsAt) return;
      36 +  if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }
      37 +  round.endsAt = 0;
      38 +  round.duration = 0;
      39 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round ended (${reason})`);
      40 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason });
      41 +}
      42 +
      43 +function startRound(duration) {
      44 +  // Clamp to the same range the portal UI allows.
      45 +  const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(duration)));
      46 +  if (round.timeout) clearTimeout(round.timeout);
      47 +  round.duration = d;
      48 +  round.endsAt = Date.now() + d * 1000;
      49 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round started (${d}s, ends at ${new Date(round.endsAt).toISOString()})`);
      50 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration: d, endsAt: round.endsAt });
      51 +  // Server is authoritative: auto-broadcast ROUND_END when the timer fires.
      52 +  round.timeout = setTimeout(() => endRound('timeout'), d * 1000);
      53 +}
      54 +
      55  // ── Server setup ─────────────────────────────────────────────
      56  const server = createServer();
      57  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ server });

⏺ Phase 1 shipped on game-idea branch.

  What's in it

  - Start Round / End Round button (replaces Transmit)
  - Duration input (5–600s, default 30)
  - Live timer in header (green → amber → red-pulse as it expires)
  - Expanded palette — Weapons 🗡️ 🔫 / Goals ⭐💎🎯❤️  / Hazards 🔥🤖👻 / Decor
  - Role auto-tagged on every placement, carried through WS
  - Cells tinted by role: white halo = pickup, red outline = hazard
  - Server-authoritative rounds — absolute endsAt keeps all clients synced, auto-end on timeout

  Test it locally

  - Portal: https://localhost:8081/portal.html
  - Dev server + WS server already running
  - Drop some 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 items on the grid → notice the tints
  - Set 10s → hit Start Round → watch the countdown tick down, then auto-end

  What's next (Phase 2 when you give the go-ahead)

  - VR side: listen for ROUND_START, spawn sprites with white halos for pickups
  - Phase 3: weapon pickup → equip on hand, basic collisions

  Branch is pushed — create PR whenever you want: https://github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping/pull/new/game-idea

  └───────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  What this means

  - The code lives in Rae's GitHub repo. You have write access — you push as modestapproach, but commits are authored as theodoreyd@gmail.com (the email we fixed
  earlier).
  - The live websites are yours. Vercel and Railway are both under your personal accounts. Rae can't see or manage those deployments — they're not connected to
  her.
  - When you vercel deploy --prod, it uploads from your local folder to your Vercel account. It has no link to GitHub (that's why the "Connect to GitHub" step
  failed earlier — Vercel's GitHub app isn't installed on the jin-dalrae org).

  So the flow right now is

  1. You edit code locally
  2. git push → goes to Rae's GitHub repo (shared)
  3. vercel --prod → goes to your Vercel (only you see it)
  4. railway up → goes to your Railway (only you see it)

  If Rae wants the same live URLs, she'd need to either:
  - Get added as a member to your Vercel/Railway teams, or
  - Run her own vercel/railway deploys under her accounts (different URLs)

  If you want auto-deploy on push (instead of manually running vercel each time), someone with admin on jin-dalrae org needs to install the Vercel GitHub app on
  that repo. Then every push triggers a Vercel build automatically.

  Want me to set up your own fork so everything's cleanly yours, or leave it as-is (shared repo, your hosting)?

⏺ Now add animation in update. Items "come to life" when round running — pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker. update already handles pos send; add the animation

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 38 lines
      362      this.spawnedEntities.delete(key);
      363    }
      364
      365 +  // Per-frame item animation: pulse pickups, flicker hazards when a round
      366 +  // is active. Idle state leaves items at their static base transform.
      367 +  private animateItems(time: number, roundRunning: boolean) {
      368 +    // Shared phases — one sin per frame, not per item.
      369 +    const pulse = Math.sin(time * 3.5);        // pickup scale oscillation
      370 +    const bob   = Math.sin(time * 1.8) * 0.05; // pickup vertical bob
      371 +    const flick = 0.75 + Math.random() * 0.25; // hazard opacity jitter
      372 +
      373 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      374 +      const s = item.sprite;
      375 +      const mat = s.material as SpriteMaterial;
      376 +
      377 +      if (!roundRunning) {
      378 +        // Snap to neutral state so the idle view stays calm.
      379 +        s.scale.set(item.baseSize, item.baseSize, 1);
      380 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      381 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      382 +        continue;
      383 +      }
      384 +
      385 +      if (isPickup(item.role)) {
      386 +        const scale = item.baseSize * (1 + 0.08 * pulse);
      387 +        s.scale.set(scale, scale, 1);
      388 +        s.position.y = 0.55 + bob;
      389 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      390 +      } else if (isHazard(item.role)) {
      391 +        // Fire/enemies read as "alive" via opacity flicker. Steady scale
      392 +        // keeps the animation from feeling seizure-y.
      393 +        mat.opacity = flick;
      394 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      395 +      }
      396 +    }
      397 +  }
      398 +
      399    update() {
      400 +    const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      401 +    const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      402 +    this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      403 +
      404      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      405      const now = performance.now();
      406      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

  What's new

❯ /model

❯ ok, so how does this transmit button work, is it necessary?

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Transmit button — manually re-sends all items from the local grid to the WebSocket server.

  How it works: Loops through every placed item in the portal's local state and sends a GRID_PLACE message for each to the server.

  Is it necessary? Probably not for normal use:
  - Every time you tap/drag a cell, the item is already auto-sent via wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', ... })
  - The server keeps state in memory and broadcasts updates to all clients

  When it would matter:
  - If the server restarted/crashed and lost its in-memory state
  - If the VR client (re)connected after portal items were already placed
  - Debugging desync issues

  It's a manual "force-push my local state" button — a safety net, not part of the core flow. You could remove it without breaking anything.

❯ ok, we don't need that button, we can change the botton fso it is now called start round. the way it works, is in the center top of the interface there will be
  a timer. you can also set the time for the round at the top. when you press start round, it will start the round for the vr contestant. when the round starts,

⏺ Skill(iwsdk-planner)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

⏺ Round-Based Game Plan

  Phase 1 — Portal UI (round controls)

  Replace Transmit button + add timer controls in top bar:

  [Clear] [30s ▾] [00:30]← center, monospace] [Start Round]

  - <input type="number"> for duration (default 30s, 5–600)
  - <div id="timer"> — big monospace clock in center
  - <button id="start-round-btn"> — sends ROUND_START with duration, then locally runs countdown synced to server

  New WS message types:
  - ROUND_START: { type, duration, startedAt } — broadcast to all clients
  - ROUND_END: { type, reason: 'timeout'|'completed' }
  - ROUND_SCORE: { type, userId, points, reason }

  Phase 2 — Item schema & roles

  Extend grid item to include a role field:

  { type, icon, label, role }

  ┌──────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Role   │   Emojis    │                  Behavior when round active                   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal     │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀 │ Collect by touch → score +1, despawn, play chime              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy    │ 🔥 🤖 👻 💀 │ Chase/patrol; damage player on touch; killable with sword/gun │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ pickup   │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡    │ Powerup (heal/speed) — stretch goal                           │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle │ 🪵 🧱 🪑    │ Static, impassable (later: physics collision)                 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor    │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 │ Neutral, passable, no interaction                             │
  └──────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Portal palette gets role tags via CSS — goals glow gold, enemies red. No UI changes needed to place them; role is derived from icon.

  Phase 3 — VR round state (new ECS components/systems)

  New components:
  GameItem { role, health, points, speed, behavior }
  RoundState  // tag on singleton entity
  Weapon { type: 'sword' | 'gun', damage, cooldown }
  Projectile { velocity, lifetime, damage, ownerEntity }
  Health { current, max }

  World globals (shared state):
  world.globals.roundRunning = signal(false)
  world.globals.roundEndsAt = signal(0)
  world.globals.score = signal(0)
  world.globals.playerHealth = signal(100)

  New systems (register in order):

  ┌─────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     System      │ Priority │                              Role                              │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ RoundSystem     │ 0        │ Listen to WS ROUND_START/ROUND_END, flip globals, manage timer │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ WeaponSystem    │ 1        │ Read gamepad inputs, spawn projectiles on trigger, swing sword │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ EnemyAISystem   │ 2        │ Move enemies (simple bob/chase) — only when round running      │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ CollisionSystem │ 3        │ Player↔item, sword↔item, projectile↔item distance checks       │
  ├─────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ HUDSystem       │ 35       │ Timer + score + health panel follows head                      │
  └─────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Phase 4 — Weapons

  Left = Sword (melee, attached to gripSpaces.left):
  - BoxGeometry(0.03, 0.8, 0.03) hilt-forward, bright blade
  - Squeeze-to-swing (or always active); track tip velocity
  - On tip proximity (< 0.3m) to enemy + velocity > 2 m/s → damage

  Right = Gun (ranged, attached to gripSpaces.right):
  - Simple pistol shape (CylinderGeometry)
  - On trigger press: spawn Projectile entity at barrel, forward velocity 15 m/s, 3s lifetime
  - Haptic feedback on fire

  Why left/right assignment: left is dominant-handed (defensive/reactive), right triggers are easier to pull quickly for aimed shots. Can swap if needed.

  Clarification: "Joystick" in your ask — assuming you mean the controller itself, not the thumbstick. The thumbstick will stay for locomotion (smooth move).

  Phase 5 — Collision (phase-1: distance-based, no physics)

  Simple squared-distance checks per frame, zero-allocation:
  - Player head → each GameItem within 0.5m
  - Sword tip → each enemy within 0.3m
  - Each Projectile → each enemy within 0.2m

  When round starts, VR spawns an entity per grid cell; when round ends or item killed, entity.dispose().

  Later: swap to PhysicsBody + PhysicsShape(Sphere) triggers once stable — avoids per-frame N×M checks.

  Phase 6 — Feedback

  - Audio: AudioSource per role (goal-collect.mp3, enemy-hit.mp3, gun-fire.mp3, round-start.mp3, round-end.mp3)
  - Visual: items pulse/glow when role=goal active; shake/flash when enemy hit
  - HUD: screen-space panel — ⏱ 00:28   ⭐ 3/7   ❤ 87
  - Broadcast page: scoreboard per user

  ---
  Implementation order (suggest one PR per phase)

  1. Portal UI — button rename + timer controls + WS round messages (no VR changes; portal standalone works)
  2. Role metadata — item schema + palette styling
  3. RoundSystem — globals + WS listener + HUD timer (no gameplay yet)
  4. Weapons — sword + gun visible, firing (no hits yet)
  5. Enemies + Collisions — AI + distance collisions + scoring
  6. Polish — audio, haptics, win/lose states, broadcast scoreboard

  ---
  Open questions before coding

  1. Win condition: all goals collected, or time-based score? Both?
  2. Health/damage: is VR player mortal? Round ends if they die?
  3. Shared score: per-user or team total? (The server is already multi-user.)
  4. Enemy AI difficulty: static-bob (easy) vs. chase-player (intense)?
  5. Pause on start: 3-2-1 countdown before items activate?

  Answer those and I'll start with Phase 1 (Portal UI + WS round messages) — that's self-contained and shippable.

  gun emoji item, you don't get a sword unless you get a sword emoji item. other non items do not have backgrounds and stay as they are. make fire an obstacle. it
   has a hitbox and will hurt if you pass through it.

⏺ Updated Plan — Pickups & Obstacles

  Visual distinction (critical for readability)

  Pickups (interactable) — render with a white billboarded disc/halo behind the emoji. Acts as affordance: "this is grabbable."

  Obstacles (dangerous/solid) — no halo, but get a subtle red flicker/glow to warn.

  Decor (cosmetic) — no halo, sprite as-is.

  Implementation: modify the emoji canvas to draw a soft white radial gradient circle before the emoji glyph. Or stack two sprites (cheaper: combine in one
  CanvasTexture).

  // In makeEmojiSprite — pickup variant
  ctx.beginPath();
  ctx.arc(64, 64, 58, 0, Math.PI * 2);
  const grad = ctx.createRadialGradient(64, 64, 10, 64, 64, 58);
  grad.addColorStop(0, 'rgba(255,255,255,0.95)');
  grad.addColorStop(1, 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)');
  ctx.fillStyle = grad;
  ctx.fill();
  // then draw emoji on top

  Revised role taxonomy

  ┌─────────────────┬────────────────┬────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Role       │     Emojis     │       Halo?        │                        Behavior                         │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ weapon-sword    │ 🗡️  ⚔️           │ white              │ Touch → player equips sword (spawns on left hand)       │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ weapon-gun      │ 🔫             │ white              │ Touch → player equips squirt gun (spawns on right hand) │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ goal            │ ⭐ 💎 🎯 🌀    │ white              │ Touch → score +1, despawn                               │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ powerup         │ ❤️  🍄 ⚡       │ white              │ Touch → heal/boost                                      │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle-damage │ 🔥             │ none (red flicker) │ Pass-through allowed, damages per frame while inside    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ obstacle-solid  │ 🧱 🪵          │ none               │ Blocks movement (phase 2 with physics)                  │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ enemy           │ 🤖 👻 💀       │ none (red)         │ Chases, damages on contact, killable                    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────────────┼────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ decor           │ 🌱 🎵 🖥️  🏮 🪑 │ none               │ Cosmetic only                                           │
  └─────────────────┴────────────────┴────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Weapon system changes

  No weapons on spawn. Empty hands until pickup.

  // On world.globals
  equippedLeft = signal<'sword' | null>(null)
  equippedRight = signal<'gun' | null>(null)

  // On sword pickup → equippedLeft.value = 'sword'
  // WeaponSystem subscribes; spawns/removes weapon mesh on gripSpaces.left

  If you drop your weapons (die, round ends, or a future unequip pickup), they despawn.

  Fire obstacle specifics

  // GameItem component gets damagePerSecond field
  // Fire: damagePerSecond = 20, radius = 0.4m

  // CollisionSystem per frame:
  for (const fire of queries.damageObstacles.entities) {
    const dist2 = headPos.distanceToSquared(firePos);
    if (dist2 < radius²) {
      playerHealth -= damagePerSecond * delta;
      // red screen vignette + damage sound
    }
  }

  Visual: 🔥 with an animated red glow sprite underneath; particles optional later.

  Updated role inference (portal side)

  Portal auto-tags role when placing by icon:

  const ROLE_BY_ICON = {
    '🗡️ ': 'weapon-sword', '⚔️ ': 'weapon-sword',
    '🔫': 'weapon-gun',
    '⭐': 'goal', '💎': 'goal', '🎯': 'goal', '🌀': 'goal',
    '❤️ ': 'powerup', '🍄': 'powerup', '⚡': 'powerup',
    '🔥': 'obstacle-damage',
    '🧱': 'obstacle-solid', '🪵': 'obstacle-solid',
    '🤖': 'enemy', '👻': 'enemy', '💀': 'enemy',
    // everything else → 'decor'
  };

  Palette should gain 🗡️  🔫 ⭐ 🔥 🤖 ❤️  if not already present. I'll add them when I touch the portal.

  Palette groups (updated)

  Pickups:  🗡️   🔫  ⭐  💎  ❤️   🍄
  Hazards:  🔥  🤖  👻
  Decor:    🌱  🪑  🪵  🏮  🖥️   🎵  🌀

  Flow summary

  1. Portal: designer places items (with auto-role).
  2. Portal: sets timer (e.g. 30s), hits Start Round.
  3. VR: items "come to life" — pickups pulse, enemies start AI, fire animates.
  4. VR player: has no weapons; runs to pick up 🗡️  for sword on left hand, 🔫 for squirt gun on right hand.
  5. Avoids 🔥 (or tanks damage), fights 🤖 with sword/gun, collects ⭐.
  6. Round ends: timer hits 0 OR all goals collected OR player dies.
  7. Final score broadcast; portal shows result.

  ---
  Ready to start Phase 1 (portal: timer, Start Round button, WS messages, expanded palette with role inference). Want me to proceed, or tweak anything above first?

      544 +      // ── Role inference ────────────────────────────────────────
      545 +      // Drives VR behavior: pickups get halos + equipment grant, hazards
      546 +      // damage player, enemies have AI + are killable, decor is inert.
      547 +      const ROLE_BY_ICON = {
      548 +        '🗡️ ': 'weapon-sword',
      549 +        '🔫': 'weapon-gun',
      550 +        '⭐': 'goal', '💎': 'goal', '🎯': 'goal',
      551 +        '❤️ ': 'powerup',
      552 +        '🔥': 'obstacle-damage',
      553 +        '🤖': 'enemy', '👻': 'enemy', '💀': 'enemy',
      554 +        // everything else → 'decor' (falls through)
      555 +      };
      556 +      function roleFor(icon) { return ROLE_BY_ICON[icon] || 'decor'; }
      557 +
      558 +      // Stamp role on each palette item's DOM so CSS tinting works.
      559 +      document.querySelectorAll('.item[data-icon]').forEach(el => {
      560 +        el.dataset.role = roleFor(el.dataset.icon);
      561 +      });

      930 +      // ── Round timer ──────────────────────────────────────────
      931 +      // Authoritative timing lives on the server; portal displays a
      932 +      // client-side countdown derived from the server's `endsAt` (ms epoch).
      933 +      // Local tick is just for the visual clock — it never writes state.
      934 +      const startBtn     = document.getElementById('start-round-btn');
      935 +      const durationEl   = document.getElementById('round-duration');
      936 +      const timerDisplay = document.getElementById('timer-display');
      937 +
      938 +      let roundEndsAt = 0;          // ms epoch; 0 when not running
      939 +      let tickHandle  = null;
      940 +
      941 +      function fmtSeconds(s) {
      942 +        const safe = Math.max(0, Math.ceil(s));
      943 +        const mm = String(Math.floor(safe / 60)).padStart(2, '0');
      944 +        const ss = String(safe % 60).padStart(2, '0');
      945 +        return `${mm}:${ss}`;
      946 +      }
      947 +
      948 +      function renderTimer() {
      949 +        const remaining = roundEndsAt
      950 +          ? Math.max(0, (roundEndsAt - Date.now()) / 1000)
      951 +          : parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 0;
      952 +        timerDisplay.textContent = fmtSeconds(remaining);
      953 +
      954 +        timerDisplay.classList.remove('running', 'warning', 'critical');
      955 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      956 +          if (remaining <= 5)       timerDisplay.classList.add('critical');
      957 +          else if (remaining <= 10) timerDisplay.classList.add('warning');
      958 +          else                      timerDisplay.classList.add('running');
      959          }
      936 -        const btn = document.getElementById('transmit-btn');
      937 -        btn.textContent = '✓ Synced!';
      938 -        btn.style.background = '#10b981';
      939 -        setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = 'Transmit'; btn.style.background = ''; }, 1500);
      960 +
      961 +        if (roundEndsAt && remaining <= 0) {
      962 +          // Server will broadcast ROUND_END; stop updating locally.
      963 +          stopTick();
      964 +        }
      965 +      }
      966 +
      967 +      function startTick() {
      968 +        if (tickHandle) return;
      969 +        tickHandle = setInterval(renderTimer, 250);
      970 +      }
      971 +      function stopTick() {
      972 +        if (tickHandle) { clearInterval(tickHandle); tickHandle = null; }
      973 +      }
      974 +
      975 +      function setRoundRunning(running) {
      976 +        startBtn.textContent = running ? 'End Round' : 'Start Round';
      977 +        startBtn.classList.toggle('running', running);
      978 +        durationEl.disabled = running;
      979 +      }
      980 +
      981 +      durationEl.addEventListener('input', () => { if (!roundEndsAt) renderTimer(); });
      982 +
      983 +      startBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      984 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      985 +          // Currently running → ask server to end.
      986 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'host-stopped' });
      987 +        } else {
      988 +          const duration = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 30));
      989 +          durationEl.value = duration;
      990 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration });
      991 +        }
      992        });
      993
      994 +      renderTimer();
      995 +
      996        syncGrids();
      997      </script>
      998    </body>

      644 +              // regardless of clock skew between portal tabs.

⏺ Now update the server to handle round messages:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 26 lines, removed 1 line
      24  const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3001;
      25
      26  // ── Shared state ────────────────────────────────────────────
      27 -const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label }
      27 +const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label, role }
      28  const users = new Map();     // userId -> { position: {x, z, heading}, spaceId }
      29
      30 +// Round state. endsAt === 0 means no round in progress.
      31 +// Duration is stored for reference (e.g. portals joining mid-round).
      32 +const round = { endsAt: 0, duration: 0, timeout: null };
      33 +
      34 +function endRound(reason) {
      35 +  if (!round.endsAt) return;
      36 +  if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }
      37 +  round.endsAt = 0;
      38 +  round.duration = 0;
      39 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round ended (${reason})`);
      40 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason });
      41 +}
      42 +
      43 +function startRound(duration) {
      44 +  // Clamp to the same range the portal UI allows.
      45 +  const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(duration)));
      46 +  if (round.timeout) clearTimeout(round.timeout);
      47 +  round.duration = d;
      48 +  round.endsAt = Date.now() + d * 1000;
      49 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round started (${d}s, ends at ${new Date(round.endsAt).toISOString()})`);
      50 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration: d, endsAt: round.endsAt });
      51 +  // Server is authoritative: auto-broadcast ROUND_END when the timer fires.
      52 +  round.timeout = setTimeout(() => endRound('timeout'), d * 1000);
      53 +}
      54 +
      55  // ── Server setup ─────────────────────────────────────────────
      56  const server = createServer();
      57  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ server });

⏺ Phase 1 shipped on game-idea branch.

  What's in it

  - Start Round / End Round button (replaces Transmit)
  - Duration input (5–600s, default 30)
  - Live timer in header (green → amber → red-pulse as it expires)
  - Expanded palette — Weapons 🗡️ 🔫 / Goals ⭐💎🎯❤️  / Hazards 🔥🤖👻 / Decor
  - Role auto-tagged on every placement, carried through WS
  - Cells tinted by role: white halo = pickup, red outline = hazard
  - Server-authoritative rounds — absolute endsAt keeps all clients synced, auto-end on timeout

  Test it locally

  - Portal: https://localhost:8081/portal.html
  - Dev server + WS server already running
  - Drop some 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 items on the grid → notice the tints
  - Set 10s → hit Start Round → watch the countdown tick down, then auto-end

  What's next (Phase 2 when you give the go-ahead)

  - VR side: listen for ROUND_START, spawn sprites with white halos for pickups
  - Phase 3: weapon pickup → equip on hand, basic collisions

  Branch is pushed — create PR whenever you want: https://github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping/pull/new/game-idea

  - The live websites are yours. Vercel and Railway are both under your personal accounts. Rae can't see or manage those deployments — they're not connected to
  her.
  - When you vercel deploy --prod, it uploads from your local folder to your Vercel account. It has no link to GitHub (that's why the "Connect to GitHub" step
  failed earlier — Vercel's GitHub app isn't installed on the jin-dalrae org).

  So the flow right now is

  1. You edit code locally
  2. git push → goes to Rae's GitHub repo (shared)
  3. vercel --prod → goes to your Vercel (only you see it)
  4. railway up → goes to your Railway (only you see it)

  If Rae wants the same live URLs, she'd need to either:
  - Get added as a member to your Vercel/Railway teams, or
  - Run her own vercel/railway deploys under her accounts (different URLs)

  If you want auto-deploy on push (instead of manually running vercel each time), someone with admin on jin-dalrae org needs to install the Vercel GitHub app on
  that repo. Then every push triggers a Vercel build automatically.

  Want me to set up your own fork so everything's cleanly yours, or leave it as-is (shared repo, your hosting)?

⏺ Now add animation in update. Items "come to life" when round running — pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker. update already handles pos send; add the animation

  ⎿  Error editing file

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 38 lines
      362      this.spawnedEntities.delete(key);
      363    }
      364
      365 +  // Per-frame item animation: pulse pickups, flicker hazards when a round
      366 +  // is active. Idle state leaves items at their static base transform.
      367 +  private animateItems(time: number, roundRunning: boolean) {
      368 +    // Shared phases — one sin per frame, not per item.
      369 +    const pulse = Math.sin(time * 3.5);        // pickup scale oscillation
      370 +    const bob   = Math.sin(time * 1.8) * 0.05; // pickup vertical bob
      371 +    const flick = 0.75 + Math.random() * 0.25; // hazard opacity jitter
      372 +
      373 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      374 +      const s = item.sprite;
      375 +      const mat = s.material as SpriteMaterial;
      376 +
      377 +      if (!roundRunning) {
      378 +        // Snap to neutral state so the idle view stays calm.
      379 +        s.scale.set(item.baseSize, item.baseSize, 1);
      380 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      381 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      382 +        continue;
      383 +      }
      384 +
      385 +      if (isPickup(item.role)) {
      386 +        const scale = item.baseSize * (1 + 0.08 * pulse);
      387 +        s.scale.set(scale, scale, 1);
      388 +        s.position.y = 0.55 + bob;
      389 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      390 +      } else if (isHazard(item.role)) {
      391 +        // Fire/enemies read as "alive" via opacity flicker. Steady scale
      392 +        // keeps the animation from feeling seizure-y.
      393 +        mat.opacity = flick;
      394 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      395 +      }
      396 +    }
      397 +  }
      398 +
      399    update() {
      400 +    const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      401 +    const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      402 +    this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      403 +
      404      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      405      const now = performance.now();
      406      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

⏺ Phase 2 shipped.

  What's new

  - Pickups glow — 🗡️ 🔫⭐💎🎯❤️  get a soft white halo baked in, looks grabbable
  - Hazards aura red — 🔥🤖👻 wrapped in a red glow, looks dangerous
  - Decor unchanged — 🪑🌿💡🟦 stay as plain emojis
  - Round state in world.globals — roundRunning / roundEndsAt signals, peekable from any system
  - Items come to life on ROUND_START: pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker opacity
  - Idle = calm — items snap back to static when round ends

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. On portal (/portal.html), drop some 🗡️ ⭐🔥 on the grid
  3. Inside VR you should see weapons/stars with white halos, fire with red aura, all stationary
  4. Hit Start Round on portal → VR items start bobbing/pulsing/flickering
  5. Timer hits 0 → items go still again

  What's next (Phase 3)

  - Actual weapon pickup logic — touch 🗡️  → sword spawns on left hand, despawns pickup
  - Sword mesh on left controller, squirt gun on right
  - Fire damage zone (hitbox → health drain)
  - HUD timer/score panel following head

  Say the word and I'll start Phase 3.

⏺ Now add collision/pickup handling. Insert after animateItems:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 58 lines, removed 1 line
      397      }
      398    }
      399
      400 -  update() {
      400 +  // Locally-authoritative collision: each client checks its own head
      401 +  // against the shared grid items. On pickup we broadcast GRID_CLEAR so
      402 +  // other clients see the item vanish. No server-side validation yet —
      403 +  // trusting clients is fine for a prototype, not a competitive game.
      404 +  private handleCollisions(deltaSeconds: number) {
      405 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      406 +
      407 +    const pickupR2 = PICKUP_RADIUS * PICKUP_RADIUS;
      408 +    const fireR2   = FIRE_RADIUS   * FIRE_RADIUS;
      409 +
      410 +    // Snapshot keys first: despawning during Map iteration is fine, but
      411 +    // we also want deterministic order in case multiple pickups overlap.
      412 +    for (const [key, item] of [...this.spawnedEntities]) {
      413 +      const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(item.sprite.position);
      414 +
      415 +      if (isPickup(item.role) && d2 < pickupR2) {
      416 +        this.applyPickup(item.role);
      417 +        this.despawnItem(key);
      418 +        // Tell the server so portal + broadcast + other VR peers drop it too.
      419 +        if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      420 +          this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      421 +        }
      422 +        continue;
      423 +      }
      424 +
      425 +      if (item.role === 'obstacle-damage' && d2 < fireR2) {
      426 +        const current = this.playerHealth.peek();
      427 +        const next = Math.max(0, current - FIRE_DPS * deltaSeconds);
      428 +        if (next !== current) this.playerHealth.value = next;
      429 +      }
      430 +    }
      431 +  }
      432 +
      433 +  private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      434 +    switch (role) {
      435 +      case 'weapon-sword':
      436 +        this.equippedLeft.value = 'sword';
      437 +        break;
      438 +      case 'weapon-gun':
      439 +        this.equippedRight.value = 'gun';
      440 +        break;
      441 +      case 'goal':
      442 +        this.score.value = this.score.peek() + GOAL_POINTS;
      443 +        break;
      444 +      case 'powerup':
      445 +        this.playerHealth.value = Math.min(
      446 +          MAX_HEALTH,
      447 +          this.playerHealth.peek() + POWERUP_HEAL,
      448 +        );
      449 +        break;
      450 +    }
      451 +  }
      452 +
      453 +  update(delta: number) {
      454      const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      455      const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      456      this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      457
      458 +    // Gameplay interactions only fire while the round is live. Gives the
      459 +    // planner time to place items before the contestant can grab them.
      460 +    if (roundRunning) this.handleCollisions(delta);
      461 +
      462      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      463      const now = performance.now();
      464      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Phase 3 shipped.

  What works now

  - 🗡️  pickup → sword spawns on left grip (metal blade + gold crossguard + brown hilt)
  - 🔫 pickup → squirt gun spawns on right grip (cyan barrel + red body)
  - ⭐💎🎯 → +1 score
  - ❤️  → +25 health (caps at 100)
  - 🔥 within 0.4m → 30 hp/sec drain
  - HUD follows head: 00:24  ⭐ 3  [████▓░░░] 87
    - Timer green → amber → red-pulse as it expires
    - Health bar color matches severity
  - Round start resets everything; round end drops weapons

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/
  2. Enter XR
  3. Place 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 on portal grid
  4. Hit Start Round on portal
  5. Walk up to 🗡️  → sword should appear in your left hand
  6. Walk up to 🔫 → squirt gun in right hand
  7. Walk through ⭐ → HUD score increments, star vanishes
  8. Linger near 🔥 → watch health bar drain

  Known limitations (future phases)

  - No damage to enemies yet — sword/gun are cosmetic for now. Swinging does nothing.
  - No projectiles — gun trigger doesn't fire anything.
  - Enemy AI (🤖👻) doesn't chase or move — just flickers.
  - Death at 0 HP doesn't trigger anything special. Round keeps running.

  Want Phase 4 (sword swing damage + gun projectiles + enemy AI + death ending the round)?

      545 +      // Drives VR behavior: pickups get halos + equipment grant, hazards

      930 +      // ── Round timer ──────────────────────────────────────────
      931 +      // Authoritative timing lives on the server; portal displays a
      932 +      // client-side countdown derived from the server's `endsAt` (ms epoch).
      933 +      // Local tick is just for the visual clock — it never writes state.
      934 +      const startBtn     = document.getElementById('start-round-btn');
      935 +      const durationEl   = document.getElementById('round-duration');
      936 +      const timerDisplay = document.getElementById('timer-display');
      937 +
      938 +      let roundEndsAt = 0;          // ms epoch; 0 when not running
      939 +      let tickHandle  = null;
      940 +
      941 +      function fmtSeconds(s) {
      942 +        const safe = Math.max(0, Math.ceil(s));
      943 +        const mm = String(Math.floor(safe / 60)).padStart(2, '0');
      944 +        const ss = String(safe % 60).padStart(2, '0');
      945 +        return `${mm}:${ss}`;
      946 +      }
      947 +
      948 +      function renderTimer() {
      949 +        const remaining = roundEndsAt
      950 +          ? Math.max(0, (roundEndsAt - Date.now()) / 1000)
      951 +          : parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 0;
      952 +        timerDisplay.textContent = fmtSeconds(remaining);
      953 +
      954 +        timerDisplay.classList.remove('running', 'warning', 'critical');
      955 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      956 +          if (remaining <= 5)       timerDisplay.classList.add('critical');
      957 +          else if (remaining <= 10) timerDisplay.classList.add('warning');
      958 +          else                      timerDisplay.classList.add('running');
      959          }
      936 -        const btn = document.getElementById('transmit-btn');
      937 -        btn.textContent = '✓ Synced!';
      938 -        btn.style.background = '#10b981';
      939 -        setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = 'Transmit'; btn.style.background = ''; }, 1500);
      960 +
      961 +        if (roundEndsAt && remaining <= 0) {
      962 +          // Server will broadcast ROUND_END; stop updating locally.
      963 +          stopTick();
      964 +        }
      965 +      }
      966 +
      967 +      function startTick() {
      968 +        if (tickHandle) return;
      969 +        tickHandle = setInterval(renderTimer, 250);
      970 +      }
      971 +      function stopTick() {
      972 +        if (tickHandle) { clearInterval(tickHandle); tickHandle = null; }
      973 +      }
      974 +
      975 +      function setRoundRunning(running) {
      976 +        startBtn.textContent = running ? 'End Round' : 'Start Round';
      977 +        startBtn.classList.toggle('running', running);
      978 +        durationEl.disabled = running;
      979 +      }
      980 +
      981 +      durationEl.addEventListener('input', () => { if (!roundEndsAt) renderTimer(); });
      982 +
      983 +      startBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
      984 +        if (roundEndsAt) {
      985 +          // Currently running → ask server to end.
      986 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'host-stopped' });
      987 +        } else {
      988 +          const duration = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, parseInt(durationEl.value, 10) || 30));
      989 +          durationEl.value = duration;
      990 +          wsSend({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration });
      991 +        }
      992        });
      993
      994 +      renderTimer();
      995 +
      996        syncGrids();
      997      </script>
      998    </body>

⏺ Update(server.js)

      24  const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3001;
      25
      26  // ── Shared state ────────────────────────────────────────────
      27 -const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label }
      27 +const gridState = new Map(); // "r,c" -> { type, icon, label, role }
      28  const users = new Map();     // userId -> { position: {x, z, heading}, spaceId }
      29
      30 +// Round state. endsAt === 0 means no round in progress.
      31 +// Duration is stored for reference (e.g. portals joining mid-round).
      32 +const round = { endsAt: 0, duration: 0, timeout: null };
      33 +
      34 +function endRound(reason) {
      35 +  if (!round.endsAt) return;
      36 +  if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }
      37 +  round.endsAt = 0;
      38 +  round.duration = 0;
      39 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round ended (${reason})`);
      40 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason });
      41 +}
      42 +
      43 +function startRound(duration) {
      44 +  // Clamp to the same range the portal UI allows.
      45 +  const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(duration)));
      46 +  if (round.timeout) clearTimeout(round.timeout);
      47 +  round.duration = d;
      48 +  round.endsAt = Date.now() + d * 1000;
      49 +  console.log(`[⏱] Round started (${d}s, ends at ${new Date(round.endsAt).toISOString()})`);
      50 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_START', duration: d, endsAt: round.endsAt });
      51 +  // Server is authoritative: auto-broadcast ROUND_END when the timer fires.
      52 +  round.timeout = setTimeout(() => endRound('timeout'), d * 1000);
      53 +}
      54 +
      55  // ── Server setup ─────────────────────────────────────────────
      56  const server = createServer();
      57  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ server });

⏺ Phase 1 shipped on game-idea branch.

  What's in it

  - Start Round / End Round button (replaces Transmit)
  - Duration input (5–600s, default 30)
  - Live timer in header (green → amber → red-pulse as it expires)
  - Expanded palette — Weapons 🗡️ 🔫 / Goals ⭐💎🎯❤️  / Hazards 🔥🤖👻 / Decor
  - Role auto-tagged on every placement, carried through WS
  - Cells tinted by role: white halo = pickup, red outline = hazard
  - Server-authoritative rounds — absolute endsAt keeps all clients synced, auto-end on timeout

  Test it locally

  - Portal: https://localhost:8081/portal.html
  - Dev server + WS server already running
  - Drop some 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 items on the grid → notice the tints
  - Set 10s → hit Start Round → watch the countdown tick down, then auto-end

  What's next (Phase 2 when you give the go-ahead)

  - VR side: listen for ROUND_START, spawn sprites with white halos for pickups
  - Phase 3: weapon pickup → equip on hand, basic collisions

  Branch is pushed — create PR whenever you want: https://github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping/pull/new/game-idea

⏺ Let me map out exactly what's where:

  ┌───────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Service      │          Account           │           Owner            │                     URL                      │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ GitHub repo       │ jin-dalrae (Rae's account) │ Rae                        │ github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Your GitHub login │ modestapproach             │ You                        │ —                                            │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Vercel            │ modestapproach's projects  │ You                        │ ar-app-jade.vercel.app                       │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Railway           │ donjikong's Projects       │ You (theodoreyd@gmail.com) │ ar-app-ws-production.up.railway.app          │
  └───────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  What this means

  - The code lives in Rae's GitHub repo. You have write access — you push as modestapproach, but commits are authored as theodoreyd@gmail.com (the email we fixed
  earlier).
  - The live websites are yours. Vercel and Railway are both under your personal accounts. Rae can't see or manage those deployments — they're not connected to
  her.
  - When you vercel deploy --prod, it uploads from your local folder to your Vercel account. It has no link to GitHub (that's why the "Connect to GitHub" step
  failed earlier — Vercel's GitHub app isn't installed on the jin-dalrae org).

  So the flow right now is

  1. You edit code locally
  2. git push → goes to Rae's GitHub repo (shared)
  3. vercel --prod → goes to your Vercel (only you see it)
  4. railway up → goes to your Railway (only you see it)

  If Rae wants the same live URLs, she'd need to either:
  - Get added as a member to your Vercel/Railway teams, or
  - Run her own vercel/railway deploys under her accounts (different URLs)

  If you want auto-deploy on push (instead of manually running vercel each time), someone with admin on jin-dalrae org needs to install the Vercel GitHub app on
  that repo. Then every push triggers a Vercel build automatically.

  Want me to set up your own fork so everything's cleanly yours, or leave it as-is (shared repo, your hosting)?

⏺ Now add animation in update. Items "come to life" when round running — pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker. update already handles pos send; add the animation
  branch:
  ⎿  Error editing file

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 38 lines
      362      this.spawnedEntities.delete(key);
      363    }
      364
      365 +  // Per-frame item animation: pulse pickups, flicker hazards when a round
      366 +  // is active. Idle state leaves items at their static base transform.
      367 +  private animateItems(time: number, roundRunning: boolean) {
      368 +    // Shared phases — one sin per frame, not per item.
      369 +    const pulse = Math.sin(time * 3.5);        // pickup scale oscillation
      370 +    const bob   = Math.sin(time * 1.8) * 0.05; // pickup vertical bob
      371 +    const flick = 0.75 + Math.random() * 0.25; // hazard opacity jitter
      372 +
      373 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      374 +      const s = item.sprite;
      375 +      const mat = s.material as SpriteMaterial;
      376 +
      377 +      if (!roundRunning) {
      378 +        // Snap to neutral state so the idle view stays calm.
      379 +        s.scale.set(item.baseSize, item.baseSize, 1);
      380 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      381 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      382 +        continue;
      383 +      }
      384 +
      385 +      if (isPickup(item.role)) {
      386 +        const scale = item.baseSize * (1 + 0.08 * pulse);
      387 +        s.scale.set(scale, scale, 1);
      388 +        s.position.y = 0.55 + bob;
      389 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      390 +      } else if (isHazard(item.role)) {
      391 +        // Fire/enemies read as "alive" via opacity flicker. Steady scale
      392 +        // keeps the animation from feeling seizure-y.
      393 +        mat.opacity = flick;
      394 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      395 +      }
      396 +    }
      397 +  }
      398 +
      399    update() {
      400 +    const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      401 +    const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      402 +    this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      403 +
      404      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      405      const now = performance.now();
      406      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

⏺ Phase 2 shipped.

  What's new

  - Pickups glow — 🗡️ 🔫⭐💎🎯❤️  get a soft white halo baked in, looks grabbable
  - Hazards aura red — 🔥🤖👻 wrapped in a red glow, looks dangerous
  - Decor unchanged — 🪑🌿💡🟦 stay as plain emojis
  - Round state in world.globals — roundRunning / roundEndsAt signals, peekable from any system
  - Items come to life on ROUND_START: pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker opacity
  - Idle = calm — items snap back to static when round ends

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. On portal (/portal.html), drop some 🗡️ ⭐🔥 on the grid
  3. Inside VR you should see weapons/stars with white halos, fire with red aura, all stationary
  4. Hit Start Round on portal → VR items start bobbing/pulsing/flickering
  5. Timer hits 0 → items go still again

  What's next (Phase 3)

  - Actual weapon pickup logic — touch 🗡️  → sword spawns on left hand, despawns pickup
  - Sword mesh on left controller, squirt gun on right
  - Fire damage zone (hitbox → health drain)
  - HUD timer/score panel following head

  Say the word and I'll start Phase 3.

⏺ Now add collision/pickup handling. Insert after animateItems:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 58 lines, removed 1 line
      397      }
      398    }
      399
      400 -  update() {
      400 +  // Locally-authoritative collision: each client checks its own head
      401 +  // against the shared grid items. On pickup we broadcast GRID_CLEAR so
      402 +  // other clients see the item vanish. No server-side validation yet —
      403 +  // trusting clients is fine for a prototype, not a competitive game.
      404 +  private handleCollisions(deltaSeconds: number) {
      405 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      406 +
      407 +    const pickupR2 = PICKUP_RADIUS * PICKUP_RADIUS;
      408 +    const fireR2   = FIRE_RADIUS   * FIRE_RADIUS;
      409 +
      410 +    // Snapshot keys first: despawning during Map iteration is fine, but
      411 +    // we also want deterministic order in case multiple pickups overlap.
      412 +    for (const [key, item] of [...this.spawnedEntities]) {
      413 +      const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(item.sprite.position);
      414 +
      415 +      if (isPickup(item.role) && d2 < pickupR2) {
      416 +        this.applyPickup(item.role);
      417 +        this.despawnItem(key);
      418 +        // Tell the server so portal + broadcast + other VR peers drop it too.
      419 +        if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      420 +          this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      421 +        }
      422 +        continue;
      423 +      }
      424 +
      425 +      if (item.role === 'obstacle-damage' && d2 < fireR2) {
      426 +        const current = this.playerHealth.peek();
      427 +        const next = Math.max(0, current - FIRE_DPS * deltaSeconds);
      428 +        if (next !== current) this.playerHealth.value = next;
      429 +      }
      430 +    }
      431 +  }
      432 +
      433 +  private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      434 +    switch (role) {
      435 +      case 'weapon-sword':
      436 +        this.equippedLeft.value = 'sword';
      437 +        break;
      438 +      case 'weapon-gun':
      439 +        this.equippedRight.value = 'gun';
      440 +        break;
      441 +      case 'goal':
      442 +        this.score.value = this.score.peek() + GOAL_POINTS;
      443 +        break;
      444 +      case 'powerup':
      445 +        this.playerHealth.value = Math.min(
      446 +          MAX_HEALTH,
      447 +          this.playerHealth.peek() + POWERUP_HEAL,
      448 +        );
      449 +        break;
      450 +    }
      451 +  }
      452 +
      453 +  update(delta: number) {
      454      const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      455      const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      456      this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      457
      458 +    // Gameplay interactions only fire while the round is live. Gives the
      459 +    // planner time to place items before the contestant can grab them.
      460 +    if (roundRunning) this.handleCollisions(delta);
      461 +
      462      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      463      const now = performance.now();
      464      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;
