⏺ Phase 3 shipped.

  What works now

  - 🗡️  pickup → sword spawns on left grip (metal blade + gold crossguard + brown hilt)
  - 🔫 pickup → squirt gun spawns on right grip (cyan barrel + red body)
  - ⭐💎🎯 → +1 score
  - ❤️  → +25 health (caps at 100)
  - 🔥 within 0.4m → 30 hp/sec drain
  - HUD follows head: 00:24  ⭐ 3  [████▓░░░] 87
    - Timer green → amber → red-pulse as it expires
    - Health bar color matches severity
  - Round start resets everything; round end drops weapons

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/
  2. Enter XR
  3. Place 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 on portal grid
  4. Hit Start Round on portal
  5. Walk up to 🗡️  → sword should appear in your left hand
  6. Walk up to 🔫 → squirt gun in right hand
  7. Walk through ⭐ → HUD score increments, star vanishes
  8. Linger near 🔥 → watch health bar drain

  Known limitations (future phases)

  - No damage to enemies yet — sword/gun are cosmetic for now. Swinging does nothing.
  - No projectiles — gun trigger doesn't fire anything.
  - Enemy AI (🤖👻) doesn't chase or move — just flickers.
  - Death at 0 HP doesn't trigger anything special. Round keeps running.

  Want Phase 4 (sword swing damage + gun projectiles + enemy AI + death ending the round)?

✻ Sautéed for 4m 19s

      182 +  // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      183 +  // processing against a now-gone entity).
      184 +  private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {
      185 +    const item = this.spawnedEntities.get(key);
      186 +    if (!item || item.role !== 'enemy') return false;
      187 +    item.hp -= amount;
      188 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      189 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + ENEMY_KILL_POINTS;
      190 +      this.despawnItem(key);
      191 +      if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      192 +        this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      193 +      }
      194 +      return true;
      195 +    }
      196 +    // Non-lethal hit — flash opacity briefly via the flicker pipeline.
      197 +    // (animateItems already handles this; no extra state needed.)
      198 +    return false;
      199 +  }

      519 +  // Simple chase: each enemy moves toward the player head at a fixed
      520 +  // speed, snapped to ground-plane y. Called from animateItems so it
      521 +  // shares the same per-frame iteration.
      522 +  private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number) {

      524 +    const step = ENEMY_SPEED * deltaSeconds;
      525 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      526 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      527 +      this.tempChase.set(
      528 +        this.tempPos.x - item.sprite.position.x,
      529 +        0,
      530 +        this.tempPos.z - item.sprite.position.z,
      531 +      );
      532 +      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      533 +      if (dist < 0.1) continue;
      534 +      this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      535 +      item.sprite.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      536 +      item.sprite.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      537 +    }
      538 +  }
      539 +

      543 +    if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      544 +      this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'died' }));
      545 +    }
      546 +  }

⏺ Add the findEnemyAt method near damageEnemy:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      188      this.connectWS();
      189    }
      190
      191 +  // Nearest enemy within squared radius, or null. Linear scan — fine
      192 +  // for the few dozen items a round has; swap to a spatial hash if we
      193 +  // ever push past a couple hundred.
      194 +  private findEnemyAt(x: number, y: number, z: number, r2: number): string | null {
      195 +    let bestKey: string | null = null;
      196 +    let bestD2 = r2;
      197 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      198 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      199 +      const dx = item.sprite.position.x - x;
      200 +      const dy = item.sprite.position.y - y;
      201 +      const dz = item.sprite.position.z - z;
      202 +      const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;
      203 +      if (d2 < bestD2) {
      204 +        bestD2 = d2;
      205 +        bestKey = key;
      206 +      }
      207 +    }
      208 +    return bestKey;
      209 +  }
      210 +
      211    // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      212    // processing against a now-gone entity).
      213    private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {

⏺ Phase 1 shipped on game-idea branch.

  What's in it

  - Start Round / End Round button (replaces Transmit)
  - Duration input (5–600s, default 30)
  - Live timer in header (green → amber → red-pulse as it expires)
  - Expanded palette — Weapons 🗡️ 🔫 / Goals ⭐💎🎯❤️  / Hazards 🔥🤖👻 / Decor
  - Role auto-tagged on every placement, carried through WS
  - Cells tinted by role: white halo = pickup, red outline = hazard
  - Server-authoritative rounds — absolute endsAt keeps all clients synced, auto-end on timeout

  Test it locally

  - Portal: https://localhost:8081/portal.html
  - Dev server + WS server already running
  - Drop some 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 items on the grid → notice the tints
  - Set 10s → hit Start Round → watch the countdown tick down, then auto-end

  What's next (Phase 2 when you give the go-ahead)

  - VR side: listen for ROUND_START, spawn sprites with white halos for pickups
  - Phase 3: weapon pickup → equip on hand, basic collisions

  Branch is pushed — create PR whenever you want: https://github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping/pull/new/game-idea

⏺ Let me map out exactly what's where:

  ┌───────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Service      │          Account           │           Owner            │                     URL                      │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ GitHub repo       │ jin-dalrae (Rae's account) │ Rae                        │ github.com/jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Your GitHub login │ modestapproach             │ You                        │ —                                            │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Vercel            │ modestapproach's projects  │ You                        │ ar-app-jade.vercel.app                       │
  ├───────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Railway           │ donjikong's Projects       │ You (theodoreyd@gmail.com) │ ar-app-ws-production.up.railway.app          │
  └───────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  What this means

  - The code lives in Rae's GitHub repo. You have write access — you push as modestapproach, but commits are authored as theodoreyd@gmail.com (the email we fixed
  earlier).
  - The live websites are yours. Vercel and Railway are both under your personal accounts. Rae can't see or manage those deployments — they're not connected to
  her.
  - When you vercel deploy --prod, it uploads from your local folder to your Vercel account. It has no link to GitHub (that's why the "Connect to GitHub" step
  failed earlier — Vercel's GitHub app isn't installed on the jin-dalrae org).

  So the flow right now is

  1. You edit code locally
  2. git push → goes to Rae's GitHub repo (shared)
  3. vercel --prod → goes to your Vercel (only you see it)
  4. railway up → goes to your Railway (only you see it)

  If Rae wants the same live URLs, she'd need to either:
  - Get added as a member to your Vercel/Railway teams, or
  - Run her own vercel/railway deploys under her accounts (different URLs)

  If you want auto-deploy on push (instead of manually running vercel each time), someone with admin on jin-dalrae org needs to install the Vercel GitHub app on
  that repo. Then every push triggers a Vercel build automatically.

  Want me to set up your own fork so everything's cleanly yours, or leave it as-is (shared repo, your hosting)?

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 38 lines
      362      this.spawnedEntities.delete(key);
      363    }
      364
      365 +  // Per-frame item animation: pulse pickups, flicker hazards when a round
      366 +  // is active. Idle state leaves items at their static base transform.
      367 +  private animateItems(time: number, roundRunning: boolean) {
      368 +    // Shared phases — one sin per frame, not per item.
      369 +    const pulse = Math.sin(time * 3.5);        // pickup scale oscillation
      370 +    const bob   = Math.sin(time * 1.8) * 0.05; // pickup vertical bob
      371 +    const flick = 0.75 + Math.random() * 0.25; // hazard opacity jitter
      372 +
      373 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      374 +      const s = item.sprite;
      375 +      const mat = s.material as SpriteMaterial;
      376 +
      377 +      if (!roundRunning) {
      378 +        // Snap to neutral state so the idle view stays calm.
      379 +        s.scale.set(item.baseSize, item.baseSize, 1);
      380 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      381 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      382 +        continue;
      383 +      }
      384 +
      385 +      if (isPickup(item.role)) {
      386 +        const scale = item.baseSize * (1 + 0.08 * pulse);
      387 +        s.scale.set(scale, scale, 1);
      388 +        s.position.y = 0.55 + bob;
      389 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      390 +      } else if (isHazard(item.role)) {
      391 +        // Fire/enemies read as "alive" via opacity flicker. Steady scale
      392 +        // keeps the animation from feeling seizure-y.
      393 +        mat.opacity = flick;

  What's new

⏺ Now add collision/pickup handling. Insert after animateItems:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 58 lines, removed 1 line
      397      }
      398    }
      399
      400 -  update() {
      400 +  // Locally-authoritative collision: each client checks its own head
      401 +  // against the shared grid items. On pickup we broadcast GRID_CLEAR so
      402 +  // other clients see the item vanish. No server-side validation yet —
      403 +  // trusting clients is fine for a prototype, not a competitive game.
      404 +  private handleCollisions(deltaSeconds: number) {
      405 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      406 +
      407 +    const pickupR2 = PICKUP_RADIUS * PICKUP_RADIUS;
      408 +    const fireR2   = FIRE_RADIUS   * FIRE_RADIUS;
      409 +
      410 +    // Snapshot keys first: despawning during Map iteration is fine, but
      411 +    // we also want deterministic order in case multiple pickups overlap.
      412 +    for (const [key, item] of [...this.spawnedEntities]) {
      413 +      const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(item.sprite.position);
      414 +
      415 +      if (isPickup(item.role) && d2 < pickupR2) {
      416 +        this.applyPickup(item.role);
      417 +        this.despawnItem(key);
      418 +        // Tell the server so portal + broadcast + other VR peers drop it too.
      419 +        if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      420 +          this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      421 +        }
      422 +        continue;
      423 +      }
      424 +
      425 +      if (item.role === 'obstacle-damage' && d2 < fireR2) {
      426 +        const current = this.playerHealth.peek();
      427 +        const next = Math.max(0, current - FIRE_DPS * deltaSeconds);
      428 +        if (next !== current) this.playerHealth.value = next;
      429 +      }
      430 +    }
      431 +  }
      432 +
      433 +  private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      434 +    switch (role) {
      435 +      case 'weapon-sword':
      436 +        this.equippedLeft.value = 'sword';
      437 +        break;
      438 +      case 'weapon-gun':
      439 +        this.equippedRight.value = 'gun';
      440 +        break;
      441 +      case 'goal':
      442 +        this.score.value = this.score.peek() + GOAL_POINTS;
      443 +        break;
      444 +      case 'powerup':
      445 +        this.playerHealth.value = Math.min(
      446 +          MAX_HEALTH,
      447 +          this.playerHealth.peek() + POWERUP_HEAL,
      448 +        );
      449 +        break;
      450 +    }
      451 +  }
      452 +
      453 +  update(delta: number) {
      454      const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      455      const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      456      this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      457
      458 +    // Gameplay interactions only fire while the round is live. Gives the
      459 +    // planner time to place items before the contestant can grab them.
      460 +    if (roundRunning) this.handleCollisions(delta);
      461 +
      462      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      463      const now = performance.now();
      464      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

⏺ Phase 3 shipped.

  What works now

  - 🗡️  pickup → sword spawns on left grip (metal blade + gold crossguard + brown hilt)
  - 🔫 pickup → squirt gun spawns on right grip (cyan barrel + red body)
  - ⭐💎🎯 → +1 score
  - ❤️  → +25 health (caps at 100)
  - 🔥 within 0.4m → 30 hp/sec drain
  - HUD follows head: 00:24  ⭐ 3  [████▓░░░] 87
    - Timer green → amber → red-pulse as it expires
    - Health bar color matches severity
  - Round start resets everything; round end drops weapons

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/
  2. Enter XR
  3. Place 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 on portal grid
  4. Hit Start Round on portal
  5. Walk up to 🗡️  → sword should appear in your left hand
  6. Walk up to 🔫 → squirt gun in right hand
  7. Walk through ⭐ → HUD score increments, star vanishes
  8. Linger near 🔥 → watch health bar drain

  Known limitations (future phases)

  - No damage to enemies yet — sword/gun are cosmetic for now. Swinging does nothing.
  - No projectiles — gun trigger doesn't fire anything.
  - Enemy AI (🤖👻) doesn't chase or move — just flickers.
  - Death at 0 HP doesn't trigger anything special. Round keeps running.

  Want Phase 4 (sword swing damage + gun projectiles + enemy AI + death ending the round)?

⏺ Now register the damageEnemy callback in init so projectiles can call back:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 23 lines
      172      this.equippedLeft  = GameState.equippedLeft(globals);
      173      this.equippedRight = GameState.equippedRight(globals);
      174
      175 +    // Expose damage entry-point for ProjectileSystem + any future attackers.
      176 +    // Passing the key lets the callback do O(1) lookup + broadcast GRID_CLEAR.
      177 +    GameActions.setDamageEnemy(globals, (key, amount) => this.damageEnemy(key, amount));
      178 +
      179      this.connectWS();
      180    }
      181
      182 +  // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      183 +  // processing against a now-gone entity).
      184 +  private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {
      185 +    const item = this.spawnedEntities.get(key);
      186 +    if (!item || item.role !== 'enemy') return false;
      187 +    item.hp -= amount;
      188 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      189 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + ENEMY_KILL_POINTS;
      190 +      this.despawnItem(key);
      191 +      if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      192 +        this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      193 +      }
      194 +      return true;
      195 +    }
      196 +    // Non-lethal hit — flash opacity briefly via the flicker pipeline.
      197 +    // (animateItems already handles this; no extra state needed.)
      198 +    return false;
      199 +  }
      200 +
      201    private connectWS() {
      202      const isLocal = location.hostname === 'localhost' || location.hostname === '127.0.0.1';
      203      const url = isLocal ? `ws://${location.hostname}:3001` : `wss://ar-app-ws-production.up.railway.app`;

⏺ Add the findEnemyAt method near damageEnemy:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      188      this.connectWS();
      189    }
      190
      191 +  // Nearest enemy within squared radius, or null. Linear scan — fine
      192 +  // for the few dozen items a round has; swap to a spatial hash if we
      193 +  // ever push past a couple hundred.
      194 +  private findEnemyAt(x: number, y: number, z: number, r2: number): string | null {
      195 +    let bestKey: string | null = null;
      196 +    let bestD2 = r2;
      197 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      198 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      199 +      const dx = item.sprite.position.x - x;
      200 +      const dy = item.sprite.position.y - y;
      201 +      const dz = item.sprite.position.z - z;
      202 +      const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;
      203 +      if (d2 < bestD2) {
      204 +        bestD2 = d2;
      205 +        bestKey = key;
      206 +      }
      207 +    }
      208 +    return bestKey;
      209 +  }
      210 +
      211    // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      212    // processing against a now-gone entity).
      213    private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {

  ⎿  To github.com:jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping.git
        ee532d7..9eb899f  game-idea -> game-idea

⏺ Phase 4 shipped. The full combat loop is live.

  What's new

  - Enemies (🤖 👻) have 3 HP, chase the player at 0.7 m/s when round is active
  - Contact damage — enemies within 0.6m drain 20 hp/s
  - Sword kills — touch an enemy with your left-hand sword, 1 dmg every 350ms cooldown (3 hits = kill)
  - Squirt gun — pull right trigger to fire cyan droplets at 12 m/s; droplets expire in 2.5s or on enemy hit
  - Enemy kills = +2 score (vs. +1 for goals)
  - Death — health hits 0 → ROUND_END with reason died, everything resets

  Design shape (so future phases stay clean)

  - Cross-system comms via world.globals — no direct imports between systems. PortalSystem publishes damageEnemy + findEnemyAt; ProjectileSystem publishes
  fireProjectile. Each caller only knows the callback, not the owner.
  - All tuning in one file (game-state.ts) — HP, radii, DPS, speeds, cooldowns. Tweak feel from one place.

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. Portal: place 🗡️ 🔫🤖⭐ somewhere
  3. Start Round → 🤖 starts moving toward you
  4. Grab 🗡️  → left hand has a sword → walk up to 🤖 → 3 swipes kills it (+2 score)
  5. Grab 🔫 → right hand has squirt gun → point + right trigger → droplet flies → enemy dies (+2)
  6. Let enemies catch you → HUD health drops → at 0 → round ends

  Known quirks

  - No haptic feedback on hits (next pass)
  - No sound effects
  - Enemies don't have a "death" animation — they just vanish

  Phase 5 options

  - Audio + haptics (punchy feel)
  - Round win condition (all goals collected → end w/ completed)
  - Per-user scoreboard (server tracks, broadcast view displays it)
  - Enemy variants (👻 hovers and phases through obstacles, 💀 faster)

  Which next?

       1 // Audio + haptic feedback helpers.

       4 // files. Each cue is a short oscillator burst with an exponential decay
       5 // envelope — cheap, punchy, and immune to the emulator's asset loader.
       6 //
       7 // Haptics hit the raw XRGamepad's hapticActuators[0].pulse(). Both APIs
       8 // are wrapped in try/catch so cue failures never break gameplay.

      603 +  private goalsAtRoundStart = 0;
      604 +  private checkWin() {
      605 +    if (this.goalsAtRoundStart === 0) return;
      606 +    let goalsLeft = 0;

      608 +      if (item.role === 'goal') goalsLeft++;
      609 +    }
      610 +    if (goalsLeft === 0 && this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      611 +      this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'completed' }));
      612 +    }
      613 +  }

⏺ Now add animation in update. Items "come to life" when round running — pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker. update already handles pos send; add the animation

  ⎿  Error editing file

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 38 lines
      362      this.spawnedEntities.delete(key);
      363    }
      364
      365 +  // Per-frame item animation: pulse pickups, flicker hazards when a round
      366 +  // is active. Idle state leaves items at their static base transform.
      367 +  private animateItems(time: number, roundRunning: boolean) {
      368 +    // Shared phases — one sin per frame, not per item.
      369 +    const pulse = Math.sin(time * 3.5);        // pickup scale oscillation
      370 +    const bob   = Math.sin(time * 1.8) * 0.05; // pickup vertical bob
      371 +    const flick = 0.75 + Math.random() * 0.25; // hazard opacity jitter
      372 +
      373 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      374 +      const s = item.sprite;
      375 +      const mat = s.material as SpriteMaterial;
      376 +
      377 +      if (!roundRunning) {
      378 +        // Snap to neutral state so the idle view stays calm.
      379 +        s.scale.set(item.baseSize, item.baseSize, 1);
      380 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      381 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      382 +        continue;
      383 +      }
      384 +
      385 +      if (isPickup(item.role)) {
      386 +        const scale = item.baseSize * (1 + 0.08 * pulse);
      387 +        s.scale.set(scale, scale, 1);
      388 +        s.position.y = 0.55 + bob;
      389 +        mat.opacity = 1;
      390 +      } else if (isHazard(item.role)) {
      391 +        // Fire/enemies read as "alive" via opacity flicker. Steady scale
      392 +        // keeps the animation from feeling seizure-y.
      393 +        mat.opacity = flick;
      394 +        s.position.y = 0.55;
      395 +      }
      396 +    }
      397 +  }
      398 +
      399    update() {
      400 +    const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      401 +    const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      402 +    this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      403 +
      404      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      405      const now = performance.now();
      406      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

⏺ Phase 2 shipped.

  What's new

  - Pickups glow — 🗡️ 🔫⭐💎🎯❤️  get a soft white halo baked in, looks grabbable
  - Hazards aura red — 🔥🤖👻 wrapped in a red glow, looks dangerous
  - Decor unchanged — 🪑🌿💡🟦 stay as plain emojis
  - Round state in world.globals — roundRunning / roundEndsAt signals, peekable from any system
  - Items come to life on ROUND_START: pickups bob + pulse, hazards flicker opacity
  - Idle = calm — items snap back to static when round ends

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. On portal (/portal.html), drop some 🗡️ ⭐🔥 on the grid
  3. Inside VR you should see weapons/stars with white halos, fire with red aura, all stationary
  4. Hit Start Round on portal → VR items start bobbing/pulsing/flickering
  5. Timer hits 0 → items go still again

  - Fire damage zone (hitbox → health drain)

      409 +
      410 +    // Snapshot keys first: despawning during Map iteration is fine, but
      411 +    // we also want deterministic order in case multiple pickups overlap.
      412 +    for (const [key, item] of [...this.spawnedEntities]) {
      413 +      const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(item.sprite.position);
      414 +
      415 +      if (isPickup(item.role) && d2 < pickupR2) {
      416 +        this.applyPickup(item.role);
      417 +        this.despawnItem(key);
      418 +        // Tell the server so portal + broadcast + other VR peers drop it too.
      419 +        if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      420 +          this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      421 +        }
      422 +        continue;
      423 +      }
      424 +
      425 +      if (item.role === 'obstacle-damage' && d2 < fireR2) {
      426 +        const current = this.playerHealth.peek();
      427 +        const next = Math.max(0, current - FIRE_DPS * deltaSeconds);
      428 +        if (next !== current) this.playerHealth.value = next;
      429 +      }
      430 +    }
      431 +  }
      432 +
      433 +  private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      434 +    switch (role) {
      435 +      case 'weapon-sword':
      436 +        this.equippedLeft.value = 'sword';
      437 +        break;
      438 +      case 'weapon-gun':
      439 +        this.equippedRight.value = 'gun';
      440 +        break;
      441 +      case 'goal':
      442 +        this.score.value = this.score.peek() + GOAL_POINTS;
      443 +        break;
      444 +      case 'powerup':
      445 +        this.playerHealth.value = Math.min(
      446 +          MAX_HEALTH,
      447 +          this.playerHealth.peek() + POWERUP_HEAL,
      448 +        );
      449 +        break;
      450 +    }
      451 +  }
      452 +
      453 +  update(delta: number) {
      454      const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      455      const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      456      this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      457
      458 +    // Gameplay interactions only fire while the round is live. Gives the
      459 +    // planner time to place items before the contestant can grab them.
      460 +    if (roundRunning) this.handleCollisions(delta);
      461 +
      462      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      463      const now = performance.now();
      464      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

⏺ Phase 3 shipped.

  What works now

  - 🗡️  pickup → sword spawns on left grip (metal blade + gold crossguard + brown hilt)
  - 🔫 pickup → squirt gun spawns on right grip (cyan barrel + red body)
  - ⭐💎🎯 → +1 score
  - ❤️  → +25 health (caps at 100)
  - 🔥 within 0.4m → 30 hp/sec drain
  - HUD follows head: 00:24  ⭐ 3  [████▓░░░] 87
    - Timer green → amber → red-pulse as it expires
    - Health bar color matches severity
  - Round start resets everything; round end drops weapons

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/
  2. Enter XR
  3. Place 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 on portal grid
  4. Hit Start Round on portal
  5. Walk up to 🗡️  → sword should appear in your left hand
  6. Walk up to 🔫 → squirt gun in right hand
  7. Walk through ⭐ → HUD score increments, star vanishes
  8. Linger near 🔥 → watch health bar drain

  Known limitations (future phases)

  - No damage to enemies yet — sword/gun are cosmetic for now. Swinging does nothing.
  - No projectiles — gun trigger doesn't fire anything.
  - Enemy AI (🤖👻) doesn't chase or move — just flickers.
  - Death at 0 HP doesn't trigger anything special. Round keeps running.

  Want Phase 4 (sword swing damage + gun projectiles + enemy AI + death ending the round)?

✻ Sautéed for 4m 19s

      182 +  // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      183 +  // processing against a now-gone entity).
      184 +  private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {
      185 +    const item = this.spawnedEntities.get(key);
      186 +    if (!item || item.role !== 'enemy') return false;
      187 +    item.hp -= amount;
      188 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      189 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + ENEMY_KILL_POINTS;
      190 +      this.despawnItem(key);
      191 +      if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      192 +        this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      193 +      }
      194 +      return true;
      195 +    }
      196 +    // Non-lethal hit — flash opacity briefly via the flicker pipeline.
      197 +    // (animateItems already handles this; no extra state needed.)
      198 +    return false;
      199 +  }

      454    private handleCollisions(deltaSeconds: number) {

      488 +        totalHealthDelta -= FIRE_DPS * deltaSeconds;

      519 +  // Simple chase: each enemy moves toward the player head at a fixed
      520 +  // speed, snapped to ground-plane y. Called from animateItems so it
      521 +  // shares the same per-frame iteration.
      522 +  private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number) {
      523 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      524 +    const step = ENEMY_SPEED * deltaSeconds;
      525 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      526 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      527 +      this.tempChase.set(
      528 +        this.tempPos.x - item.sprite.position.x,
      529 +        0,
      530 +        this.tempPos.z - item.sprite.position.z,
      531 +      );
      532 +      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      533 +      if (dist < 0.1) continue;
      534 +      this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      535 +      item.sprite.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      536 +      item.sprite.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      537 +    }
      538 +  }
      539 +
      540 +  private playerDied() {
      541 +    console.log('[Round] Player died');
      542 +    // Server will broadcast ROUND_END back, which resets everything.
      543 +    if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      544 +      this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'died' }));
      545 +    }
      546 +  }

⏺ Add the findEnemyAt method near damageEnemy:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      188      this.connectWS();
      189    }
      190
      191 +  // Nearest enemy within squared radius, or null. Linear scan — fine
      192 +  // for the few dozen items a round has; swap to a spatial hash if we
      193 +  // ever push past a couple hundred.
      194 +  private findEnemyAt(x: number, y: number, z: number, r2: number): string | null {
      195 +    let bestKey: string | null = null;
      196 +    let bestD2 = r2;
      197 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      198 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      199 +      const dx = item.sprite.position.x - x;
      200 +      const dy = item.sprite.position.y - y;
      201 +      const dz = item.sprite.position.z - z;
      202 +      const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;
      203 +      if (d2 < bestD2) {
      204 +        bestD2 = d2;
      205 +        bestKey = key;
      206 +      }
      207 +    }
      208 +    return bestKey;
      209 +  }
      210 +
      211    // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      212    // processing against a now-gone entity).
      213    private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {

⏺ Phase 4 shipped. The full combat loop is live.

  What's new

  - Enemies (🤖 👻) have 3 HP, chase the player at 0.7 m/s when round is active
  - Contact damage — enemies within 0.6m drain 20 hp/s
  - Sword kills — touch an enemy with your left-hand sword, 1 dmg every 350ms cooldown (3 hits = kill)
  - Squirt gun — pull right trigger to fire cyan droplets at 12 m/s; droplets expire in 2.5s or on enemy hit
  - Enemy kills = +2 score (vs. +1 for goals)
  - Death — health hits 0 → ROUND_END with reason died, everything resets

  Design shape (so future phases stay clean)

  - Cross-system comms via world.globals — no direct imports between systems. PortalSystem publishes damageEnemy + findEnemyAt; ProjectileSystem publishes
  fireProjectile. Each caller only knows the callback, not the owner.
  - All tuning in one file (game-state.ts) — HP, radii, DPS, speeds, cooldowns. Tweak feel from one place.

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. Portal: place 🗡️ 🔫🤖⭐ somewhere
  3. Start Round → 🤖 starts moving toward you
  4. Grab 🗡️  → left hand has a sword → walk up to 🤖 → 3 swipes kills it (+2 score)
  5. Grab 🔫 → right hand has squirt gun → point + right trigger → droplet flies → enemy dies (+2)
  6. Let enemies catch you → HUD health drops → at 0 → round ends

  Known quirks

  - No haptic feedback on hits (next pass)
  - No sound effects
  - Enemies don't have a "death" animation — they just vanish

  Phase 5 options

  - Audio + haptics (punchy feel)
  - Round win condition (all goals collected → end w/ completed)
  - Per-user scoreboard (server tracks, broadcast view displays it)
  - Enemy variants (👻 hovers and phases through obstacles, 💀 faster)

  Which next?

✻ Baked for 5m 36s

❯ ok, next

       1 // Audio + haptic feedback helpers.
       2 //
       3 // Audio is synthesized via the Web Audio API so we don't need any asset
       4 // files. Each cue is a short oscillator burst with an exponential decay

       7 // Haptics hit the raw XRGamepad's hapticActuators[0].pulse(). Both APIs

      600 +  // End the round early when no goals remain. Only triggers if at least

      603 +  private goalsAtRoundStart = 0;

⏺ Player hurt feedback when health drops — use a threshold so we don't spam the cue every frame:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)

      548        const next = Math.max(0, current + totalHealthDelta);
      549        if (next !== current) {
      550          this.playerHealth.value = next;
      551 +        // Cue once per chunk of damage taken (not per frame) — floor(hp/5)

⏺ Phase 5 shipped. Game now has juice.

  What's new

  - Synthesized audio (Web Audio API — no files needed):
    - 🗡️ 🔫 pickup → quick upward chirp + haptic
    - ⭐ pickup → two-note ding
    - ❤️  powerup → warm sweep
    - Sword hit → low square thud + strong rumble
    - Enemy kill → saw buzz + big rumble
    - Gun fire → sharp click + soft rumble
    - Taking damage → saw growl + rumble
    - Round start → two-note beep
    - Round win → C–E–G victory triad
    - Round lose → descending saw dirge
    - Round timeout → single muted beep
  - Controller haptics per cue (intensity + duration tuned per action)
  - Win condition — collect all ⭐💎🎯 placed at round start → round ends with completed + victory jingle. If zero goals were placed, it's treated as survival mode
   and the clock runs normally.
  - Hurt cue throttled to every 5 HP lost so standing in fire doesn't spam.

  Test

  1. Reload VR scene
  2. Place 🗡️  🔫 ⭐ ⭐ 🤖 🔥 on portal → Start Round (30s)
  3. Grab the sword (haptic pulse + chirp)
  4. Kill the robot (thud + kill-thump, score +2)
  5. Collect both stars (ding + ding + victory fanfare since goals=0)
  6. Or walk into fire until health hits 0 → lose dirge
  7. Or let the clock run out → muted timeout beep

  Known limits

  - First sound may be silent until the user gestures (browser autoplay rule) — Start Round button on portal counts as a gesture for the portal, but VR scene might
   need one click to unlock audio. In practice Meta Quest Browser is permissive.
  - No round-result panel yet (HUD just goes idle at round end)

  Phase 6 options

  - Result panel — big "YOU WIN! 7 points" / "TIME'S UP" overlay after round end, auto-hide in 4s
  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast view shows leaderboard
  - Enemy variants — 👻 phases through, 💀 sprints, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — drag to reposition live, bulk-clear, round presets

  Which?

✻ Sautéed for 3m 55s

      117 +    // fires immediately with current value — we guard the null case so
      118 +    // we don't flash an empty panel on first frame.
      119 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      120 +      result.subscribe((r) => {
      121 +        if (r) {
      122 +          this.drawResult(r);
      123 +          this.resultMesh.visible = true;
      124 +        } else {
      125 +          this.resultMesh.visible = false;
      126 +        }
      127 +      }),
      128 +    );
      129 +  }

      132      // 10 Hz redraw — the seconds digit is the highest-frequency thing on
      133      // the panel. Anything faster wastes draw calls.

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 58 lines, removed 1 line
      397      }
      398    }
      399
      400 -  update() {
      400 +  // Locally-authoritative collision: each client checks its own head
      401 +  // against the shared grid items. On pickup we broadcast GRID_CLEAR so
      402 +  // other clients see the item vanish. No server-side validation yet —
      403 +  // trusting clients is fine for a prototype, not a competitive game.
      404 +  private handleCollisions(deltaSeconds: number) {
      405 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      406 +
      407 +    const pickupR2 = PICKUP_RADIUS * PICKUP_RADIUS;
      408 +    const fireR2   = FIRE_RADIUS   * FIRE_RADIUS;
      409 +
      410 +    // Snapshot keys first: despawning during Map iteration is fine, but
      411 +    // we also want deterministic order in case multiple pickups overlap.
      412 +    for (const [key, item] of [...this.spawnedEntities]) {
      413 +      const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(item.sprite.position);
      414 +
      415 +      if (isPickup(item.role) && d2 < pickupR2) {
      416 +        this.applyPickup(item.role);
      417 +        this.despawnItem(key);
      418 +        // Tell the server so portal + broadcast + other VR peers drop it too.
      419 +        if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      420 +          this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      421 +        }
      422 +        continue;
      423 +      }
      424 +
      425 +      if (item.role === 'obstacle-damage' && d2 < fireR2) {
      426 +        const current = this.playerHealth.peek();
      427 +        const next = Math.max(0, current - FIRE_DPS * deltaSeconds);
      428 +        if (next !== current) this.playerHealth.value = next;
      429 +      }
      430 +    }
      431 +  }
      432 +
      433 +  private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      434 +    switch (role) {
      435 +      case 'weapon-sword':
      436 +        this.equippedLeft.value = 'sword';
      437 +        break;
      438 +      case 'weapon-gun':
      439 +        this.equippedRight.value = 'gun';
      440 +        break;
      441 +      case 'goal':
      442 +        this.score.value = this.score.peek() + GOAL_POINTS;
      443 +        break;
      444 +      case 'powerup':
      445 +        this.playerHealth.value = Math.min(
      446 +          MAX_HEALTH,
      447 +          this.playerHealth.peek() + POWERUP_HEAL,
      448 +        );
      449 +        break;
      450 +    }
      451 +  }
      452 +
      453 +  update(delta: number) {
      454      const time = performance.now() / 1000;
      455      const roundRunning = this.roundRunning.peek();
      456      this.animateItems(time, roundRunning);
      457
      458 +    // Gameplay interactions only fire while the round is live. Gives the
      459 +    // planner time to place items before the contestant can grab them.
      460 +    if (roundRunning) this.handleCollisions(delta);
      461 +
      462      // Send VR player head position at ~10 Hz
      463      const now = performance.now();
      464      if (now - this.lastPosSend < 100) return;

⏺ Phase 3 shipped.

  What works now

  - 🗡️  pickup → sword spawns on left grip (metal blade + gold crossguard + brown hilt)
  - 🔫 pickup → squirt gun spawns on right grip (cyan barrel + red body)
  - ⭐💎🎯 → +1 score
  - ❤️  → +25 health (caps at 100)
  - 🔥 within 0.4m → 30 hp/sec drain
  - HUD follows head: 00:24  ⭐ 3  [████▓░░░] 87
    - Timer green → amber → red-pulse as it expires
    - Health bar color matches severity
  - Round start resets everything; round end drops weapons

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/
  2. Enter XR
  3. Place 🗡️ 🔫⭐🔥 on portal grid
  4. Hit Start Round on portal
  5. Walk up to 🗡️  → sword should appear in your left hand
  6. Walk up to 🔫 → squirt gun in right hand
  7. Walk through ⭐ → HUD score increments, star vanishes
  8. Linger near 🔥 → watch health bar drain

  Known limitations (future phases)

  - No damage to enemies yet — sword/gun are cosmetic for now. Swinging does nothing.
  - No projectiles — gun trigger doesn't fire anything.
  - Enemy AI (🤖👻) doesn't chase or move — just flickers.
  - Death at 0 HP doesn't trigger anything special. Round keeps running.

  Want Phase 4 (sword swing damage + gun projectiles + enemy AI + death ending the round)?

      182 +  // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      183 +  // processing against a now-gone entity).
      184 +  private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {
      185 +    const item = this.spawnedEntities.get(key);
      186 +    if (!item || item.role !== 'enemy') return false;
      187 +    item.hp -= amount;
      188 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      189 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + ENEMY_KILL_POINTS;
      190 +      this.despawnItem(key);
      191 +      if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      192 +        this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GRID_CLEAR', key }));
      193 +      }
      194 +      return true;
      195 +    }
      196 +    // Non-lethal hit — flash opacity briefly via the flicker pipeline.
      197 +    // (animateItems already handles this; no extra state needed.)
      198 +    return false;
      199 +  }

      531 +      );
      532 +      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      533 +      if (dist < 0.1) continue;
      534 +      this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      535 +      item.sprite.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      536 +      item.sprite.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      537 +    }
      538 +  }

      545 +    }

⏺ Add the findEnemyAt method near damageEnemy:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      188      this.connectWS();
      189    }
      190
      191 +  // Nearest enemy within squared radius, or null. Linear scan — fine
      192 +  // for the few dozen items a round has; swap to a spatial hash if we
      193 +  // ever push past a couple hundred.
      194 +  private findEnemyAt(x: number, y: number, z: number, r2: number): string | null {
      195 +    let bestKey: string | null = null;
      196 +    let bestD2 = r2;
      197 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      198 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      199 +      const dx = item.sprite.position.x - x;
      200 +      const dy = item.sprite.position.y - y;
      201 +      const dz = item.sprite.position.z - z;
      202 +      const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;
      203 +      if (d2 < bestD2) {
      204 +        bestD2 = d2;
      205 +        bestKey = key;
      206 +      }
      207 +    }
      208 +    return bestKey;
      209 +  }
      210 +
      211    // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      212    // processing against a now-gone entity).
      213    private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {

⏺ Phase 4 shipped. The full combat loop is live.

  What's new

  - Enemies (🤖 👻) have 3 HP, chase the player at 0.7 m/s when round is active
  - Contact damage — enemies within 0.6m drain 20 hp/s
  - Sword kills — touch an enemy with your left-hand sword, 1 dmg every 350ms cooldown (3 hits = kill)
  - Squirt gun — pull right trigger to fire cyan droplets at 12 m/s; droplets expire in 2.5s or on enemy hit
  - Enemy kills = +2 score (vs. +1 for goals)
  - Death — health hits 0 → ROUND_END with reason died, everything resets

  Design shape (so future phases stay clean)

  - Cross-system comms via world.globals — no direct imports between systems. PortalSystem publishes damageEnemy + findEnemyAt; ProjectileSystem publishes
  fireProjectile. Each caller only knows the callback, not the owner.
  - All tuning in one file (game-state.ts) — HP, radii, DPS, speeds, cooldowns. Tweak feel from one place.

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. Portal: place 🗡️ 🔫🤖⭐ somewhere
  3. Start Round → 🤖 starts moving toward you
  4. Grab 🗡️  → left hand has a sword → walk up to 🤖 → 3 swipes kills it (+2 score)
  5. Grab 🔫 → right hand has squirt gun → point + right trigger → droplet flies → enemy dies (+2)
  6. Let enemies catch you → HUD health drops → at 0 → round ends

  Known quirks

  - No haptic feedback on hits (next pass)
  - No sound effects
  - Enemies don't have a "death" animation — they just vanish

  Phase 5 options

  - Audio + haptics (punchy feel)
  - Round win condition (all goals collected → end w/ completed)
  - Per-user scoreboard (server tracks, broadcast view displays it)
  - Enemy variants (👻 hovers and phases through obstacles, 💀 faster)

  Which next?

✻ Baked for 5m 36s

       1 // Audio + haptic feedback helpers.
       2 //
       3 // Audio is synthesized via the Web Audio API so we don't need any asset
       4 // files. Each cue is a short oscillator burst with an exponential decay
       5 // envelope — cheap, punchy, and immune to the emulator's asset loader.
       6 //
       7 // Haptics hit the raw XRGamepad's hapticActuators[0].pulse(). Both APIs
       8 // are wrapped in try/catch so cue failures never break gameplay.

      603 +  private goalsAtRoundStart = 0;

      548        const next = Math.max(0, current + totalHealthDelta);
      549        if (next !== current) {
      550          this.playerHealth.value = next;
      551 +        // Cue once per chunk of damage taken (not per frame) — floor(hp/5)

⏺ Phase 5 shipped. Game now has juice.

  What's new

  - Synthesized audio (Web Audio API — no files needed):
    - 🗡️ 🔫 pickup → quick upward chirp + haptic
    - ⭐ pickup → two-note ding
    - ❤️  powerup → warm sweep
    - Sword hit → low square thud + strong rumble
    - Enemy kill → saw buzz + big rumble
    - Gun fire → sharp click + soft rumble
    - Taking damage → saw growl + rumble
    - Round start → two-note beep
    - Round win → C–E–G victory triad
    - Round lose → descending saw dirge
    - Round timeout → single muted beep
  - Controller haptics per cue (intensity + duration tuned per action)
  - Win condition — collect all ⭐💎🎯 placed at round start → round ends with completed + victory jingle. If zero goals were placed, it's treated as survival mode
   and the clock runs normally.
  - Hurt cue throttled to every 5 HP lost so standing in fire doesn't spam.

  Test

  1. Reload VR scene
  2. Place 🗡️  🔫 ⭐ ⭐ 🤖 🔥 on portal → Start Round (30s)
  3. Grab the sword (haptic pulse + chirp)
  4. Kill the robot (thud + kill-thump, score +2)
  5. Collect both stars (ding + ding + victory fanfare since goals=0)
  6. Or walk into fire until health hits 0 → lose dirge
  7. Or let the clock run out → muted timeout beep

  Known limits

  - First sound may be silent until the user gestures (browser autoplay rule) — Start Round button on portal counts as a gesture for the portal, but VR scene might
   need one click to unlock audio. In practice Meta Quest Browser is permissive.
  - No round-result panel yet (HUD just goes idle at round end)

  Phase 6 options

  - Result panel — big "YOU WIN! 7 points" / "TIME'S UP" overlay after round end, auto-hide in 4s
  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast view shows leaderboard
  - Enemy variants — 👻 phases through, 💀 sprints, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — drag to reposition live, bulk-clear, round presets

  Which?

✻ Sautéed for 3m 55s

      120 +      result.subscribe((r) => {
      121 +        if (r) {
      122 +          this.drawResult(r);
      123 +          this.resultMesh.visible = true;
      124 +        } else {
      125 +          this.resultMesh.visible = false;
      126 +        }
      127 +      }),

      129 +  }

      133      // the panel. Anything faster wastes draw calls.

⏺ Phase 6 shipped.

  What's new

  - Big result overlay appears when the round ends, floating ~1.8m in front of the head:

  ┌─────────────────────┬─────────────┬───────┐
  │       Reason        │    Title    │ Color │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ All goals collected │ VICTORY     │ green │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Health hit 0        │ YOU DIED    │ red   │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Timer ran out       │ TIME'S UP   │ amber │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Host clicked End    │ ROUND ENDED │ grey  │
  └─────────────────────┴─────────────┴───────┘

  - Shows final ⭐ score prominently
  - Auto-dismisses after 4.5s
  - Reason-matched audio cue (already shipped in Phase 5) fires at the same time

  Test

  - Collect all ⭐ → green VICTORY + final score
  - Walk into 🔥 until dead → red YOU DIED
  - Let the clock expire → amber TIME'S UP
  - Portal hits End Round mid-play → grey ROUND ENDED

  Phase 7 options

  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast page shows a live leaderboard during the round
  - Enemy variants — 👻 floats/phases, 💀 faster + weak, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — portal round presets (save/load grid layouts), live drag-to-move during round
  - Goal counter in HUD — show ⭐ 3 / 7 instead of just score so the player knows how many are left

  Which?

      54 +// Per-variant stats. Lookup keyed by the portal's `item.type` string so
      55 +// the portal can keep emoji picking simple and the game picks up flavor.
      56 +// Unknown types fall back to ENEMY_DEFAULT (backwards-compat with any
      57 +// older grid snapshots).
      58 +export type EnemyStats = {
      59 +  hp: number;
      60 +  speed: number;         // m/s
      61 +  dps: number;           // damage-per-second to player on touch
      62 +  killPoints: number;    // score award on defeat
      63 +  bobAmp: number;        // vertical bob amplitude (m) — 0 = grounded
      64 +  bobSpeed: number;      // rad/s
      65 +};
      66 +
      67 +export const ENEMY_DEFAULT: EnemyStats = {
      68 +  hp: 3,
      69 +  speed: 0.7,
      70 +  dps: 20,
      71 +  killPoints: 2,
      72 +  bobAmp: 0,
      73 +  bobSpeed: 0,
      74 +};
      75 +
      76 +export const ENEMY_STATS: Record<string, EnemyStats> = {
      77 +  // 🤖 Slow tank — soaks hits, hits hard on touch
      78 +  robot: { hp: 4, speed: 0.55, dps: 20, killPoints: 2, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
      79 +  // 👻 Floating, fast, fragile-ish — harder to hit because it weaves up/down
      80 +  ghost: { hp: 2, speed: 1.1,  dps: 14, killPoints: 3, bobAmp: 0.35, bobSpeed: 3.2 },
      81 +  // 💀 Glass cannon — dies to a single hit but sprints and bites hard
      82 +  skull: { hp: 1, speed: 1.45, dps: 28, killPoints: 1, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
      83 +};
      84 +
      85 +export function enemyStats(type: string): EnemyStats {
      86 +  return ENEMY_STATS[type] ?? ENEMY_DEFAULT;
      87 +}

      181
      182 +  // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      183 +  // processing against a now-gone entity).
      184 +  private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {
      185 +    const item = this.spawnedEntities.get(key);
      186 +    if (!item || item.role !== 'enemy') return false;
      187 +    item.hp -= amount;
      188 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      189 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + ENEMY_KILL_POINTS;

      531 +      );

      534 +      this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      535 +      item.sprite.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      536 +      item.sprite.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      537 +    }
      538 +  }

      545 +    }

⏺ Add the findEnemyAt method near damageEnemy:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      188      this.connectWS();
      189    }
      190
      191 +  // Nearest enemy within squared radius, or null. Linear scan — fine
      192 +  // for the few dozen items a round has; swap to a spatial hash if we
      193 +  // ever push past a couple hundred.
      194 +  private findEnemyAt(x: number, y: number, z: number, r2: number): string | null {
      195 +    let bestKey: string | null = null;
      196 +    let bestD2 = r2;
      197 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      198 +      if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      199 +      const dx = item.sprite.position.x - x;
      200 +      const dy = item.sprite.position.y - y;
      201 +      const dz = item.sprite.position.z - z;
      202 +      const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;
      203 +      if (d2 < bestD2) {
      204 +        bestD2 = d2;
      205 +        bestKey = key;
      206 +      }
      207 +    }
      208 +    return bestKey;
      209 +  }
      210 +
      211    // Returns true if the hit killed the enemy (so callers can skip further
      212    // processing against a now-gone entity).
      213    private damageEnemy(key: string, amount: number): boolean {

⏺ Phase 4 shipped. The full combat loop is live.

  What's new

  - Enemies (🤖 👻) have 3 HP, chase the player at 0.7 m/s when round is active
  - Contact damage — enemies within 0.6m drain 20 hp/s
  - Sword kills — touch an enemy with your left-hand sword, 1 dmg every 350ms cooldown (3 hits = kill)
  - Squirt gun — pull right trigger to fire cyan droplets at 12 m/s; droplets expire in 2.5s or on enemy hit
  - Enemy kills = +2 score (vs. +1 for goals)
  - Death — health hits 0 → ROUND_END with reason died, everything resets

  Design shape (so future phases stay clean)

  - Cross-system comms via world.globals — no direct imports between systems. PortalSystem publishes damageEnemy + findEnemyAt; ProjectileSystem publishes
  fireProjectile. Each caller only knows the callback, not the owner.
  - All tuning in one file (game-state.ts) — HP, radii, DPS, speeds, cooldowns. Tweak feel from one place.

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. Portal: place 🗡️ 🔫🤖⭐ somewhere
  3. Start Round → 🤖 starts moving toward you
  4. Grab 🗡️  → left hand has a sword → walk up to 🤖 → 3 swipes kills it (+2 score)
  5. Grab 🔫 → right hand has squirt gun → point + right trigger → droplet flies → enemy dies (+2)
  6. Let enemies catch you → HUD health drops → at 0 → round ends

  Known quirks

  - No haptic feedback on hits (next pass)
  - No sound effects
  - Enemies don't have a "death" animation — they just vanish

  Phase 5 options

  - Audio + haptics (punchy feel)
  - Round win condition (all goals collected → end w/ completed)
  - Per-user scoreboard (server tracks, broadcast view displays it)
  - Enemy variants (👻 hovers and phases through obstacles, 💀 faster)

  Which next?

✻ Baked for 5m 36s

       1 // Audio + haptic feedback helpers.
       2 //
       3 // Audio is synthesized via the Web Audio API so we don't need any asset
       4 // files. Each cue is a short oscillator burst with an exponential decay
       5 // envelope — cheap, punchy, and immune to the emulator's asset loader.
       6 //
       7 // Haptics hit the raw XRGamepad's hapticActuators[0].pulse(). Both APIs
       8 // are wrapped in try/catch so cue failures never break gameplay.

      601 +  // one goal existed at some point during this round — a round with zero
      602 +  // goals is a survival/exploration round and should just run the clock.
      603 +  private goalsAtRoundStart = 0;
      604 +  private checkWin() {
      605 +    if (this.goalsAtRoundStart === 0) return;
      606 +    let goalsLeft = 0;
      607 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      608 +      if (item.role === 'goal') goalsLeft++;
      609 +    }
      610 +    if (goalsLeft === 0 && this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      611 +      this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'completed' }));
      612 +    }
      613 +  }

⏺ Player hurt feedback when health drops — use a threshold so we don't spam the cue every frame:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 5 lines
      548        const next = Math.max(0, current + totalHealthDelta);
      549        if (next !== current) {
      550          this.playerHealth.value = next;
      551 +        // Cue once per chunk of damage taken (not per frame) — floor(hp/5)
      552 +        // changing means at least 5 HP drained since the last cue.
      553 +        if (Math.floor(next / 5) < Math.floor(current / 5)) {
      554 +          FX.playerHurt(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      555 +        }
      556          if (next <= 0) this.playerDied();
      557        }
      558      }

⏺ Phase 5 shipped. Game now has juice.

  What's new

  - Synthesized audio (Web Audio API — no files needed):
    - 🗡️ 🔫 pickup → quick upward chirp + haptic
    - ⭐ pickup → two-note ding
    - ❤️  powerup → warm sweep
    - Sword hit → low square thud + strong rumble
    - Enemy kill → saw buzz + big rumble
    - Gun fire → sharp click + soft rumble
    - Taking damage → saw growl + rumble
    - Round start → two-note beep
    - Round win → C–E–G victory triad
    - Round lose → descending saw dirge
    - Round timeout → single muted beep
  - Controller haptics per cue (intensity + duration tuned per action)
  - Win condition — collect all ⭐💎🎯 placed at round start → round ends with completed + victory jingle. If zero goals were placed, it's treated as survival mode
   and the clock runs normally.
  - Hurt cue throttled to every 5 HP lost so standing in fire doesn't spam.

  Test

  1. Reload VR scene
  2. Place 🗡️  🔫 ⭐ ⭐ 🤖 🔥 on portal → Start Round (30s)
  3. Grab the sword (haptic pulse + chirp)
  4. Kill the robot (thud + kill-thump, score +2)
  5. Collect both stars (ding + ding + victory fanfare since goals=0)
  6. Or walk into fire until health hits 0 → lose dirge
  7. Or let the clock run out → muted timeout beep

  Known limits

  - First sound may be silent until the user gestures (browser autoplay rule) — Start Round button on portal counts as a gesture for the portal, but VR scene might
   need one click to unlock audio. In practice Meta Quest Browser is permissive.
  - No round-result panel yet (HUD just goes idle at round end)

  Phase 6 options

  - Result panel — big "YOU WIN! 7 points" / "TIME'S UP" overlay after round end, auto-hide in 4s
  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast view shows leaderboard
  - Enemy variants — 👻 phases through, 💀 sprints, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — drag to reposition live, bulk-clear, round presets

  Which?

⏺ Phase 6 shipped.

  What's new

  - Big result overlay appears when the round ends, floating ~1.8m in front of the head:

  ┌─────────────────────┬─────────────┬───────┐
  │       Reason        │    Title    │ Color │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ All goals collected │ VICTORY     │ green │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Health hit 0        │ YOU DIED    │ red   │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Timer ran out       │ TIME'S UP   │ amber │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Host clicked End    │ ROUND ENDED │ grey  │
  └─────────────────────┴─────────────┴───────┘

  - Shows final ⭐ score prominently
  - Auto-dismisses after 4.5s
  - Reason-matched audio cue (already shipped in Phase 5) fires at the same time

  Test

  - Collect all ⭐ → green VICTORY + final score
  - Walk into 🔥 until dead → red YOU DIED
  - Let the clock expire → amber TIME'S UP
  - Portal hits End Round mid-play → grey ROUND ENDED

  Phase 7 options

  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast page shows a live leaderboard during the round
  - Enemy variants — 👻 floats/phases, 💀 faster + weak, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — portal round presets (save/load grid layouts), live drag-to-move during round
  - Goal counter in HUD — show ⭐ 3 / 7 instead of just score so the player knows how many are left

  Which?

      54 +// Per-variant stats. Lookup keyed by the portal's `item.type` string so
      55 +// the portal can keep emoji picking simple and the game picks up flavor.
      56 +// Unknown types fall back to ENEMY_DEFAULT (backwards-compat with any
      57 +// older grid snapshots).
      58 +export type EnemyStats = {
      59 +  hp: number;
      60 +  speed: number;         // m/s
      61 +  dps: number;           // damage-per-second to player on touch
      62 +  killPoints: number;    // score award on defeat
      63 +  bobAmp: number;        // vertical bob amplitude (m) — 0 = grounded
      64 +  bobSpeed: number;      // rad/s
      65 +};
      66 +
      67 +export const ENEMY_DEFAULT: EnemyStats = {
      68 +  hp: 3,
      69 +  speed: 0.7,
      70 +  dps: 20,
      71 +  killPoints: 2,
      72 +  bobAmp: 0,
      73 +  bobSpeed: 0,
      74 +};
      75 +
      76 +export const ENEMY_STATS: Record<string, EnemyStats> = {
      77 +  // 🤖 Slow tank — soaks hits, hits hard on touch
      78 +  robot: { hp: 4, speed: 0.55, dps: 20, killPoints: 2, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
      79 +  // 👻 Floating, fast, fragile-ish — harder to hit because it weaves up/down

      81 +  // 💀 Glass cannon — dies to a single hit but sprints and bites hard
      82 +  skull: { hp: 1, speed: 1.45, dps: 28, killPoints: 1, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
      83 +};
      84 +
      85 +export function enemyStats(type: string): EnemyStats {
      86 +  return ENEMY_STATS[type] ?? ENEMY_DEFAULT;
      87 +}

      89  export const SWORD_DAMAGE = 1;

⏺ Phase 7 shipped.

  Enemy variants

  ┌──────────┬─────┬──────────┬─────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────┐
  │          │ HP  │  Speed   │ DPS │ Kill pts │           Notes            │
  ├──────────┼─────┼──────────┼─────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ 🤖 Robot │ 4   │ 0.55 m/s │ 20  │ 2        │ Tanky, slow — gun-friendly │
  ├──────────┼─────┼──────────┼─────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ 👻 Ghost │ 2   │ 1.1 m/s  │ 14  │ 3        │ Bobs vertically — tricky   │
  ├──────────┼─────┼──────────┼─────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ 💀 Skull │ 1   │ 1.45 m/s │ 28  │ 1        │ Glass cannon, sprinter     │
  └──────────┴─────┴──────────┴─────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────┘

  - Ghosts now weave up/down while chasing — harder to hit with the sword or a flat projectile
  - Skulls die to one hit (gun or sword) but deal serious damage if they catch you
  - Robots stay as your steady tanky chaser

  HUD

  - When a round starts with ≥1 goal: HUD shows ⭐ 2/5 progress
  - Survival rounds (no goals): HUD falls back to raw score ⭐ 8
  - Result overlay still shows the final total score

  Test

  Place 🤖 👻 💀 on portal. Start round. You'll see:
  - 🤖 lumbering at you
  - 👻 bobbing while chasing
  - 💀 sprinting — one hit from sword/gun and it's gone, but don't let it touch you

  Phase 8 options

  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast view shows live leaderboard
  - Round presets / save-load — portal stores named grid layouts for quick replay
  - Round-over winner banner on portal + broadcast (not just VR HUD)
  - More items — walls/obstacles-solid (🧱) that block movement
  - Controller UI — wrist watch showing score/health instead of floating HUD

  Which?

⏺ Phase 4 shipped. The full combat loop is live.

  What's new

  - Enemies (🤖 👻) have 3 HP, chase the player at 0.7 m/s when round is active
  - Contact damage — enemies within 0.6m drain 20 hp/s
  - Sword kills — touch an enemy with your left-hand sword, 1 dmg every 350ms cooldown (3 hits = kill)
  - Squirt gun — pull right trigger to fire cyan droplets at 12 m/s; droplets expire in 2.5s or on enemy hit
  - Enemy kills = +2 score (vs. +1 for goals)
  - Death — health hits 0 → ROUND_END with reason died, everything resets

  Design shape (so future phases stay clean)

  - Cross-system comms via world.globals — no direct imports between systems. PortalSystem publishes damageEnemy + findEnemyAt; ProjectileSystem publishes
  fireProjectile. Each caller only knows the callback, not the owner.
  - All tuning in one file (game-state.ts) — HP, radii, DPS, speeds, cooldowns. Tweak feel from one place.

  Test

  1. Reload https://localhost:8081/ in VR
  2. Portal: place 🗡️ 🔫🤖⭐ somewhere
  3. Start Round → 🤖 starts moving toward you
  4. Grab 🗡️  → left hand has a sword → walk up to 🤖 → 3 swipes kills it (+2 score)
  5. Grab 🔫 → right hand has squirt gun → point + right trigger → droplet flies → enemy dies (+2)
  6. Let enemies catch you → HUD health drops → at 0 → round ends

  Known quirks

  - No haptic feedback on hits (next pass)
  - No sound effects
  - Enemies don't have a "death" animation — they just vanish

  Phase 5 options

  - Audio + haptics (punchy feel)
  - Round win condition (all goals collected → end w/ completed)
  - Per-user scoreboard (server tracks, broadcast view displays it)
  - Enemy variants (👻 hovers and phases through obstacles, 💀 faster)

  Which next?

✻ Baked for 5m 36s

       1 // Audio + haptic feedback helpers.
       2 //
       3 // Audio is synthesized via the Web Audio API so we don't need any asset
       4 // files. Each cue is a short oscillator burst with an exponential decay
       5 // envelope — cheap, punchy, and immune to the emulator's asset loader.
       6 //
       7 // Haptics hit the raw XRGamepad's hapticActuators[0].pulse(). Both APIs
       8 // are wrapped in try/catch so cue failures never break gameplay.

      600 +  // End the round early when no goals remain. Only triggers if at least
      601 +  // one goal existed at some point during this round — a round with zero
      602 +  // goals is a survival/exploration round and should just run the clock.
      603 +  private goalsAtRoundStart = 0;
      604 +  private checkWin() {
      605 +    if (this.goalsAtRoundStart === 0) return;
      606 +    let goalsLeft = 0;
      607 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      608 +      if (item.role === 'goal') goalsLeft++;
      609 +    }
      610 +    if (goalsLeft === 0 && this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      611 +      this.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason: 'completed' }));
      612 +    }
      613 +  }

⏺ Player hurt feedback when health drops — use a threshold so we don't spam the cue every frame:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)

      548        const next = Math.max(0, current + totalHealthDelta);
      549        if (next !== current) {
      550          this.playerHealth.value = next;
      551 +        // Cue once per chunk of damage taken (not per frame) — floor(hp/5)

⏺ Phase 5 shipped. Game now has juice.

  What's new

  - Synthesized audio (Web Audio API — no files needed):
    - 🗡️ 🔫 pickup → quick upward chirp + haptic
    - ⭐ pickup → two-note ding
    - ❤️  powerup → warm sweep
    - Sword hit → low square thud + strong rumble
    - Enemy kill → saw buzz + big rumble
    - Gun fire → sharp click + soft rumble
    - Taking damage → saw growl + rumble
    - Round start → two-note beep
    - Round win → C–E–G victory triad
    - Round lose → descending saw dirge
    - Round timeout → single muted beep
  - Controller haptics per cue (intensity + duration tuned per action)
  - Win condition — collect all ⭐💎🎯 placed at round start → round ends with completed + victory jingle. If zero goals were placed, it's treated as survival mode
   and the clock runs normally.
  - Hurt cue throttled to every 5 HP lost so standing in fire doesn't spam.

  Test

  1. Reload VR scene
  2. Place 🗡️  🔫 ⭐ ⭐ 🤖 🔥 on portal → Start Round (30s)
  3. Grab the sword (haptic pulse + chirp)
  4. Kill the robot (thud + kill-thump, score +2)
  5. Collect both stars (ding + ding + victory fanfare since goals=0)
  6. Or walk into fire until health hits 0 → lose dirge
  7. Or let the clock run out → muted timeout beep

  Known limits

  - First sound may be silent until the user gestures (browser autoplay rule) — Start Round button on portal counts as a gesture for the portal, but VR scene might
   need one click to unlock audio. In practice Meta Quest Browser is permissive.
  - No round-result panel yet (HUD just goes idle at round end)

  Phase 6 options

  - Result panel — big "YOU WIN! 7 points" / "TIME'S UP" overlay after round end, auto-hide in 4s
  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast view shows leaderboard
  - Enemy variants — 👻 phases through, 💀 sprints, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — drag to reposition live, bulk-clear, round presets

  Which?

✻ Sautéed for 3m 55s

       83 +    this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

      116 +    // Show when a result lands, hide when cleared. Signal subscription
      117 +    // fires immediately with current value — we guard the null case so
      118 +    // we don't flash an empty panel on first frame.
      119 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      120 +      result.subscribe((r) => {
      121 +        if (r) {
      122 +          this.drawResult(r);
      123 +          this.resultMesh.visible = true;
      124 +        } else {
      125 +          this.resultMesh.visible = false;
      126 +        }
      127 +      }),
      128 +    );
      129 +  }
      130 +
      131    update(_delta: number, time: number) {
      132      // 10 Hz redraw — the seconds digit is the highest-frequency thing on
      133      // the panel. Anything faster wastes draw calls.

⏺ Phase 6 shipped.

  What's new

  - Big result overlay appears when the round ends, floating ~1.8m in front of the head:

  ┌─────────────────────┬─────────────┬───────┐
  │       Reason        │    Title    │ Color │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ All goals collected │ VICTORY     │ green │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Health hit 0        │ YOU DIED    │ red   │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Timer ran out       │ TIME'S UP   │ amber │
  ├─────────────────────┼─────────────┼───────┤
  │ Host clicked End    │ ROUND ENDED │ grey  │
  └─────────────────────┴─────────────┴───────┘

  - Shows final ⭐ score prominently
  - Auto-dismisses after 4.5s
  - Reason-matched audio cue (already shipped in Phase 5) fires at the same time

  Test

  - Collect all ⭐ → green VICTORY + final score
  - Walk into 🔥 until dead → red YOU DIED
  - Let the clock expire → amber TIME'S UP
  - Portal hits End Round mid-play → grey ROUND ENDED

  Phase 7 options

  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast page shows a live leaderboard during the round
  - Enemy variants — 👻 floats/phases, 💀 faster + weak, 🤖 tanky
  - Designer tools — portal round presets (save/load grid layouts), live drag-to-move during round
  - Goal counter in HUD — show ⭐ 3 / 7 instead of just score so the player knows how many are left

  Which?

      53 +
      54 +// Per-variant stats. Lookup keyed by the portal's `item.type` string so
      55 +// the portal can keep emoji picking simple and the game picks up flavor.
      56 +// Unknown types fall back to ENEMY_DEFAULT (backwards-compat with any
      57 +// older grid snapshots).
      58 +export type EnemyStats = {
      59 +  hp: number;
      60 +  speed: number;         // m/s
      61 +  dps: number;           // damage-per-second to player on touch
      62 +  killPoints: number;    // score award on defeat
      63 +  bobAmp: number;        // vertical bob amplitude (m) — 0 = grounded
      64 +  bobSpeed: number;      // rad/s
      65 +};
      66 +
      67 +export const ENEMY_DEFAULT: EnemyStats = {
      68 +  hp: 3,
      69 +  speed: 0.7,
      70 +  dps: 20,
      71 +  killPoints: 2,
      72 +  bobAmp: 0,
      73 +  bobSpeed: 0,
      74 +};
      75 +
      76 +export const ENEMY_STATS: Record<string, EnemyStats> = {
      77 +  // 🤖 Slow tank — soaks hits, hits hard on touch
      78 +  robot: { hp: 4, speed: 0.55, dps: 20, killPoints: 2, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
      79 +  // 👻 Floating, fast, fragile-ish — harder to hit because it weaves up/down
      80 +  ghost: { hp: 2, speed: 1.1,  dps: 14, killPoints: 3, bobAmp: 0.35, bobSpeed: 3.2 },
      81 +  // 💀 Glass cannon — dies to a single hit but sprints and bites hard
      82 +  skull: { hp: 1, speed: 1.45, dps: 28, killPoints: 1, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
      83 +};
      84 +
      85 +export function enemyStats(type: string): EnemyStats {
      86 +  return ENEMY_STATS[type] ?? ENEMY_DEFAULT;
      87 +}
      88  export const SWORD_RADIUS = 0.6;        // meters — grip-to-enemy
      89  export const SWORD_DAMAGE = 1;
      90  export const SWORD_COOLDOWN_MS = 350;

⏺ Phase 7 shipped.

  Enemy variants

  ┌──────────┬─────┬──────────┬─────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────┐
  │          │ HP  │  Speed   │ DPS │ Kill pts │           Notes            │
  ├──────────┼─────┼──────────┼─────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ 🤖 Robot │ 4   │ 0.55 m/s │ 20  │ 2        │ Tanky, slow — gun-friendly │
  ├──────────┼─────┼──────────┼─────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ 👻 Ghost │ 2   │ 1.1 m/s  │ 14  │ 3        │ Bobs vertically — tricky   │
  ├──────────┼─────┼──────────┼─────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────┤
  │ 💀 Skull │ 1   │ 1.45 m/s │ 28  │ 1        │ Glass cannon, sprinter     │
  └──────────┴─────┴──────────┴─────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────┘

  - Ghosts now weave up/down while chasing — harder to hit with the sword or a flat projectile
  - Skulls die to one hit (gun or sword) but deal serious damage if they catch you
  - Robots stay as your steady tanky chaser

  HUD

  - When a round starts with ≥1 goal: HUD shows ⭐ 2/5 progress
  - Survival rounds (no goals): HUD falls back to raw score ⭐ 8
  - Result overlay still shows the final total score

  Test

  Place 🤖 👻 💀 on portal. Start round. You'll see:
  - 🤖 lumbering at you
  - 👻 bobbing while chasing
  - 💀 sprinting — one hit from sword/gun and it's gone, but don't let it touch you

  Phase 8 options

  - Multi-user scoreboard — server tracks per-user score, broadcast view shows live leaderboard
  - Round presets / save-load — portal stores named grid layouts for quick replay
  - Round-over winner banner on portal + broadcast (not just VR HUD)
  - More items — walls/obstacles-solid (🧱) that block movement
  - Controller UI — wrist watch showing score/health instead of floating HUD

⏺ Now add playerStats, updatePlayerStats, and renderLeaderboard. Put them near updatePlayer:

⏺ Update(portal.html)
  ⎿  Added 64 lines
      835          players.delete(userId);
      836        }
      837
      838 +      // ── Leaderboard state ──────────────────────────────────
      839 +      // Keyed by userId — only VR clients that send PLAYER_POSITION
      840 +      // appear. Portal/observer connections have no stats so they're
      841 +      // silently excluded.
      842 +      const playerStats = new Map();
      843 +
      844 +      function updatePlayerStats(userId, s) {
      845 +        if (!s) return;
      846 +        // Portal's own connection never sends stats; bail if the packet
      847 +        // has no score field at all (e.g. legacy position-only messages).
      848 +        const hasStats =
      849 +          s.score != null || s.health != null || s.goalsCollected != null;
      850 +        if (!hasStats) return;
      851 +        playerStats.set(userId, {
      852 +          score: s.score ?? 0,
      853 +          health: s.health ?? 100,
      854 +          goalsCollected: s.goalsCollected ?? 0,
      855 +          goalsTotal: s.goalsTotal ?? 0,
      856 +        });
      857 +        renderLeaderboard();
      858 +      }
      859 +
      860 +      function renderLeaderboard() {
      861 +        const entriesEl = document.getElementById('lb-entries');
      862 +        const countEl   = document.getElementById('lb-count');
      863 +        const rows = [...playerStats.entries()];
      864 +
      865 +        // Rank by goals collected first (primary objective), then raw score,
      866 +        // then health as a tiebreaker. Fits the game's scoring intent.
      867 +        rows.sort((a, b) => {
      868 +          const ga = a[1].goalsCollected, gb = b[1].goalsCollected;
      869 +          if (ga !== gb) return gb - ga;
      870 +          if (a[1].score !== b[1].score) return b[1].score - a[1].score;
      871 +          return b[1].health - a[1].health;
      872 +        });
      873 +
      874 +        countEl.textContent = rows.length;
      875 +
      876 +        if (rows.length === 0) {
      877 +          entriesEl.innerHTML = '<div class="lb-empty">Waiting for VR players…</div>';
      878 +          return;
      879 +        }
      880 +
      881 +        entriesEl.innerHTML = rows.map(([userId, s], i) => {
      882 +          const color = colorForUser(userId);
      883 +          const short = userId.slice(0, 6);
      884 +          const label = userId === myUserId ? `${short} (you)` : short;
      885 +          const progress = s.goalsTotal > 0
      886 +            ? `${s.goalsCollected}/${s.goalsTotal}`
      887 +            : String(s.score);
      888 +          const hpClass =
      889 +            s.health <= 25 ? ' critical' :
      890 +            s.health <= 50 ? ' warning' : '';
      891 +          return (
      892 +            `<div class="lb-row lb-rank-${i + 1}">` +
      893 +              `<span class="lb-dot" style="--pcolor: ${color.solid}"></span>` +
      894 +              `<span class="lb-name">${label}</span>` +
      895 +              `<span class="lb-score">⭐ ${progress}</span>` +
      896 +              `<span class="lb-health${hpClass}">${Math.ceil(s.health)}</span>` +
      897 +            `</div>`
      898 +          );
      899 +        }).join('');
      900 +      }
      901 +
      902        function updatePlayer(userId, pos) {
      903          if (!pos) return;
      904          const entry = ensurePlayer(userId);

⏺ Phase 8 shipped.

  What's new

  - VR stats streamed — score, health, goals-collected, goals-total all ride on the existing 10 Hz position packet. No new message type.
  - Server wipes cached scores on ROUND_START so the leaderboard starts clean.
  - Floating leaderboard on the portal (top-right):
  PLAYERS                              2
  ● a1b2c3 (you)        ⭐ 3/5       87
  ● d4e5f6              ⭐ 1/5       45 ←amber
    - Color dot matches the user's VR avatar color
    - Rank 1 highlighted in gold
    - Health turns amber at ≤50, red at ≤25
    - Shows goalsCollected/goalsTotal when round has goals, otherwise raw score
    - Sorted by goals → score → health
    - Updates live at 10 Hz
  - Empty state when no VR players are connected.

  Test

  - One person in VR, one watching portal → portal shows the VR player's live score
  - Two VR headsets on the same WS → both appear, ranked

  Phase 9 options

  - Broadcast page leaderboard — mirror to the broadcast view for the big-screen/streamer audience
  - Round-over winner banner on portal — confetti + "🏆 a1b2c3 wins!" modal when all goals collected
  - Designer presets — named grid layouts, save/load/share
  - Spectator-friendly scene hints — minimap / item counts / "5 🤖 left" badge
  - Match series — best-of-3 rounds with aggregate score
