⏺ Now rewrite tickEnemyAI with all the new logic:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 104 lines, removed 17 lines
      736      }
      737    }
      738
      739 -  // Per-variant chase: speed + optional vertical bob for flying enemies.
      740 -  // Shared one-sin-per-frame phase keeps costs flat even with many ghosts.
      739 +  // Per-variant chase AI. Three behaviors stack on the same loop:
      740 +  //  - Robot: aggro only within radius, else walk home to origin.
      741 +  //  - Ghost: always chase (slow), passes through walls.
      742 +  //  - Skull: always chase, periodically switches target between
      743 +  //    live players (multiplayer flavor).
      744 +  //
      745 +  // Dead players are filtered out of the target list entirely so the
      746 +  // round can continue without the game turning into a corpse parade.
      747    private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      742 -    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      748 +    const now = performance.now();
      749 +
      750 +    // Gather live player positions. Own player excluded if dead.
      751 +    const targets: { id: string; x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      752 +    if (!this.isDead.peek()) {
      753 +      this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      754 +      targets.push({ id: this.userId ?? 'self', x: this.tempPos.x, z: this.tempPos.z });
      755 +    }
      756 +    for (const [uid, av] of this.avatars) {
      757 +      if (av.dead) continue;
      758 +      targets.push({ id: uid, x: av.root.position.x, z: av.root.position.z });
      759 +    }
      760 +    if (targets.length === 0) return; // nobody to chase — enemies idle
      761 +
      762 +    // Walls are static — gather once per tick.
      763 +    const walls: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      764      for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      765 +      if (item.kind === 'wall') walls.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      766 +    }
      767 +    const blockR = WALL_CELL_HALF + 0.18; // wall half + enemy half
      768 +    const hitsWall = (x: number, z: number): boolean => {
      769 +      for (const w of walls) {
      770 +        if (Math.abs(x - w.x) < blockR && Math.abs(z - w.z) < blockR) return true;
      771 +      }
      772 +      return false;
      773 +    };
      774 +
      775 +    // Skull target selection — rotate through live players periodically.
      776 +    // Pick once per tick; individual skulls share the selection so the
      777 +    // hunt feels coordinated.
      778 +    const skullRetarget = enemyBehavior('skull').retargetMs ?? 4000;
      779 +    let skullTarget = targets[0];
      780 +    if (this.skullTargetUserId) {
      781 +      const t = targets.find((p) => p.id === this.skullTargetUserId);
      782 +      if (t) skullTarget = t;
      783 +    }
      784 +    if (now >= this.skullRetargetAt) {
      785 +      skullTarget = targets[Math.floor(Math.random() * targets.length)];
      786 +      this.skullTargetUserId = skullTarget.id;
      787 +      this.skullRetargetAt = now + skullRetarget;
      788 +    }
      789 +
      790 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      791        if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      792        const stats = enemyStats(item.type);
      793 +      const behav = enemyBehavior(item.type);
      794 +      const pos = item.object3D.position;
      795
      747 -      // Horizontal chase
      748 -      this.tempChase.set(
      749 -        this.tempPos.x - item.object3D.position.x,
      750 -        0,
      751 -        this.tempPos.z - item.object3D.position.z,
      752 -      );
      753 -      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      754 -      if (dist > 0.1) {
      755 -        const step = stats.speed * deltaSeconds;
      756 -        this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      757 -        item.object3D.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      758 -        item.object3D.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      796 +      // Pick a target per variant:
      797 +      //   skull → shared skullTarget
      798 +      //   everyone else → closest live player
      799 +      let target = targets[0];
      800 +      if (item.type === 'skull') {
      801 +        target = skullTarget;
      802 +      } else {
      803 +        let bestD2 = Infinity;
      804 +        for (const p of targets) {
      805 +          const dx = p.x - pos.x;
      806 +          const dz = p.z - pos.z;
      807 +          const d2 = dx * dx + dz * dz;
      808 +          if (d2 < bestD2) { bestD2 = d2; target = p; }
      809 +        }
      810        }
      811
      812 +      // Aggro: robots only chase if target is within radius; otherwise
      813 +      // they return to origin. null radius = always aggroed.
      814 +      let moveToX: number, moveToZ: number;
      815 +      if (behav.aggroRadius === null) {
      816 +        moveToX = target.x;
      817 +        moveToZ = target.z;
      818 +      } else {
      819 +        const dx = target.x - pos.x;
      820 +        const dz = target.z - pos.z;
      821 +        if (dx * dx + dz * dz < behav.aggroRadius * behav.aggroRadius) {
      822 +          moveToX = target.x;
      823 +          moveToZ = target.z;
      824 +        } else {
      825 +          moveToX = item.origin[0];
      826 +          moveToZ = item.origin[2];
      827 +        }
      828 +      }
      829 +
      830 +      // Move toward destination, optionally blocked by walls.
      831 +      const dx = moveToX - pos.x;
      832 +      const dz = moveToZ - pos.z;
      833 +      const dist = Math.hypot(dx, dz);
      834 +      if (dist > 0.05) {
      835 +        const step = Math.min(dist, stats.speed * deltaSeconds);
      836 +        const nx = pos.x + (dx * step) / dist;
      837 +        const nz = pos.z + (dz * step) / dist;
      838 +        if (behav.wallPass || !hitsWall(nx, nz)) {
      839 +          pos.x = nx;
      840 +          pos.z = nz;
      841 +        } else {
      842 +          // Slide along a wall: try each axis independently so enemies
      843 +          // don't just stick helplessly on a corner.
      844 +          if (!hitsWall(nx, pos.z))      pos.x = nx;
      845 +          else if (!hitsWall(pos.x, nz)) pos.z = nz;
      846 +        }
      847 +      }
      848 +
      849        // Vertical bob — ghosts float and weave, ground units skip this.
      850        if (stats.bobAmp > 0) {
      763 -        item.object3D.position.y =
      764 -          item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      851 +        pos.y = item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      852        }
      853      }
      854    }

⏺ Shipped.

  What changed

  - Place 🟦 on the portal → a real 2.2m-tall blue box spawns in that grid cell in VR
  - Bottom sits on the floor, fills the cell with a small gap so neighboring walls don't z-fight
  - LocomotionEnvironment is attached → you can't walk through it (locomotion system treats it as a static collider)
  - Walls skip all the sprite animation logic (no pulse/flicker/bob)
  - Other items (emojis) still render as billboarded sprites

      178 +  // Warp cooldown + exclusion — after a teleport the player keeps drifting
      179 +  // inside the destination warp's radius for a frame or two. We block
      180 +  // re-triggers globally for WARP_COOLDOWN_MS, and additionally exclude
      181 +  // the destination warp until the player physically walks out of it.
      182 +  private warpCooldownUntil = 0;
      183 +  private lockedWarpKey: string | null = null;

⏺ Add a dedicated tickWarps method after tickEnemyAI:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 65 lines
      710      }
      711    }
      712
      713 +  // Warp teleport — active any time, not gated on roundRunning. Walking
      714 +  // into a 🌀 sends the player to a random *other* warp on the grid.
      715 +  //
      716 +  // The re-trigger problem (player lands inside another warp and would
      717 +  // chain instantly) is solved two ways, together:
      718 +  //   1) Global cooldown blocks any warp for WARP_COOLDOWN_MS.
      719 +  //   2) The destination warp is *locked* until the player physically
      720 +  //      leaves its radius — so even after cooldown, you won't bounce
      721 +  //      back unless you move.
      722 +  private tickWarps() {
      723 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      724 +    const r2 = WARP_RADIUS * WARP_RADIUS;
      725 +    const now = performance.now();
      726 +
      727 +    // Collect all warps once — we need both "am I inside one?" and
      728 +    // "give me a random destination" lookups.
      729 +    const warps: { key: string; pos: Vector3 }[] = [];
      730 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      731 +      if (item.role === 'warp') warps.push({ key, pos: item.object3D.position });
      732 +    }
      733 +
      734 +    // Clear the locked warp once the player has stepped out of its radius.
      735 +    if (this.lockedWarpKey) {
      736 +      const locked = this.spawnedEntities.get(this.lockedWarpKey);
      737 +      if (!locked || locked.role !== 'warp') {
      738 +        this.lockedWarpKey = null;
      739 +      } else {
      740 +        const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(locked.object3D.position);
      741 +        if (d2 > r2) this.lockedWarpKey = null;
      742 +      }
      743 +    }
      744 +
      745 +    if (now < this.warpCooldownUntil) return;
      746 +    if (warps.length < 2) return; // need at least two to make a trip
      747 +
      748 +    // Find the warp the player is currently inside (that isn't locked)
      749 +    let entered: { key: string; pos: Vector3 } | null = null;
      750 +    for (const w of warps) {
      751 +      if (w.key === this.lockedWarpKey) continue;
      752 +      if (this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(w.pos) < r2) { entered = w; break; }
      753 +    }
      754 +    if (!entered) return;
      755 +
      756 +    // Pick a random destination that isn't the one we're inside.
      757 +    const candidates = warps.filter((w) => w.key !== entered!.key);
      758 +    const dest = candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];
      759 +
      760 +    // Teleport by moving the XROrigin such that the player's head lands
      761 +    // at the destination. We don't touch Y so the player stays at the
      762 +    // floor level the locomotion system maintained.
      763 +    const headWorld = this.tempPos;             // already set above
      764 +    const originWorld = this.tempChase;         // reuse scratch
      765 +    this.player.getWorldPosition(originWorld);
      766 +    const dx = dest.pos.x - (headWorld.x - originWorld.x);
      767 +    const dz = dest.pos.z - (headWorld.z - originWorld.z);
      768 +    this.player.position.x = dx;
      769 +    this.player.position.z = dz;
      770 +
      771 +    this.warpCooldownUntil = now + WARP_COOLDOWN_MS;
      772 +    this.lockedWarpKey = dest.key;
      773 +    // Either hand works — prefer right if both are connected.
      774 +    FX.warp(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      775 +    console.log(`[Warp] ${entered.key} → ${dest.key}`);
      776 +  }
      777 +
      778    private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      779      const leftPad  = this.input.gamepads.left;
      780      const rightPad = this.input.gamepads.right;

⏺ Shipped.

  What's new

  🌀 Warps
  - Place 2+ Warp items on the portal grid
  - In VR, walk into one → you're teleported to a random other warp
  - Big whoosh sound + strong haptic on arrival
  - Purple aura around the emoji so you can spot them
  - Breathing pulse when a round is live

  Re-trigger guards (both active simultaneously):
  1. 1.5s global cooldown — can't warp at all for 1.5s after a jump
  2. Destination lock — that specific warp won't retrigger until you physically step out of its radius. So even after the
  cooldown expires, you stay put unless you move.

  Naming
  - Palette label Portal → Warp (disambiguates from /portal.html URL)
  - Palette label Cube → Wall (since 🟦 already renders as a real 3D wall)
  - Internal data-type="warp" and new warp role

  Test

  1. Reload portal tab
  2. Place 🌀 🌀 🌀 at opposite corners + a 🟦 wall in the middle
  3. Walk into any 🌀 → whoosh, you're at a different one
  4. If you stay still on arrival → nothing happens (locked until you leave)
  5. Walk out and back in → teleport again

  Also: 🟦 cubes show in the palette as Wall now instead of Cube.

       78  export const ENEMY_STATS: Record<string, EnemyStats> = {
       79 -  // 🤖 Slow tank — soaks hits, hits hard on touch
       80 -  robot: { hp: 4, speed: 0.55, dps: 20, killPoints: 2, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
       81 -  // 👻 Floating, fast, fragile-ish — harder to hit because it weaves up/down

       83 -  // 💀 Glass cannon — dies to a single hit but sprints and bites hard
       84 -  skull: { hp: 1, speed: 1.45, dps: 28, killPoints: 1, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },
       79 +  // 🤖 Territorial guard — only chases when a player is within aggro
       80 +  //     range, otherwise walks home. Hits hard on touch.
       81 +  robot: { hp: 4, speed: 0.8,  dps: 20, killPoints: 2, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },

       87  };
       88
       89 +// Per-variant AI behavior flags
       90 +export type EnemyBehavior = {
       91 +  aggroRadius: number | null;   // null = always aggroed
       92 +  wallPass: boolean;            // if false, blocked by walls
       93 +  retargetMs: number | null;    // null = never switches target
       94 +};
       95 +export const ENEMY_BEHAVIOR: Record<string, EnemyBehavior> = {
       96 +  robot: { aggroRadius: 3.0,  wallPass: false, retargetMs: null  },
       97 +  ghost: { aggroRadius: null, wallPass: true,  retargetMs: null  },
       98 +  skull: { aggroRadius: null, wallPass: false, retargetMs: 4000  },
       99 +};
      100 +export function enemyBehavior(type: string): EnemyBehavior {
      101 +  return ENEMY_BEHAVIOR[type] ?? { aggroRadius: null, wallPass: false, retargetMs: null };
      102 +}
      103 +
      104  export function enemyStats(type: string): EnemyStats {

      738 +  // Teleport math — moves XROrigin so the player's head lands at `target`
      739 +  // on the XZ plane. Y is left alone so floor-level is preserved.
      740 +  private teleportPlayerTo(targetX: number, targetZ: number) {
      741 +    const headWorld = this.tempPos;
      742 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(headWorld);
      743 +    const originWorld = this.tempChase;
      744 +    this.player.getWorldPosition(originWorld);
      745 +    this.player.position.x = targetX - (headWorld.x - originWorld.x);
      746 +    this.player.position.z = targetZ - (headWorld.z - originWorld.z);
      747 +  }
      748 +
      749 +  // Pick a deterministic spawn: hash userId into the spawn list so two
      750 +  // players reliably end up at different chairs when there are enough.
      751 +  // Falls back to origin when no spawns are placed.
      752 +  private teleportToSpawn() {
      753 +    const spawns: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      754 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      755 +      if (item.role === 'spawn') {
      756 +        spawns.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      757 +      }
      758      }
      759 +    if (spawns.length === 0) {
      760 +      this.teleportPlayerTo(0, 0);
      761 +      return;
      762 +    }
      763 +    // Hash userId to a stable index. Portal observers have no userId
      764 +    // here — fallback to random for offline/dev.
      765 +    let h = 0;
      766 +    const id = this.userId ?? String(Math.random());
      767 +    for (let i = 0; i < id.length; i++) h = (h * 31 + id.charCodeAt(i)) | 0;
      768 +    const pick = spawns[Math.abs(h) % spawns.length];
      769 +    this.teleportPlayerTo(pick.x, pick.z);
      770    }

⏺ Now rewrite tickEnemyAI with all the new logic:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 104 lines, removed 17 lines
      736      }
      737    }
      738
      739 -  // Per-variant chase: speed + optional vertical bob for flying enemies.
      740 -  // Shared one-sin-per-frame phase keeps costs flat even with many ghosts.
      739 +  // Per-variant chase AI. Three behaviors stack on the same loop:
      740 +  //  - Robot: aggro only within radius, else walk home to origin.
      741 +  //  - Ghost: always chase (slow), passes through walls.
      742 +  //  - Skull: always chase, periodically switches target between
      743 +  //    live players (multiplayer flavor).
      744 +  //
      745 +  // Dead players are filtered out of the target list entirely so the
      746 +  // round can continue without the game turning into a corpse parade.
      747    private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      742 -    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      748 +    const now = performance.now();
      749 +
      750 +    // Gather live player positions. Own player excluded if dead.
      751 +    const targets: { id: string; x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      752 +    if (!this.isDead.peek()) {
      753 +      this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      754 +      targets.push({ id: this.userId ?? 'self', x: this.tempPos.x, z: this.tempPos.z });
      755 +    }
      756 +    for (const [uid, av] of this.avatars) {
      757 +      if (av.dead) continue;
      758 +      targets.push({ id: uid, x: av.root.position.x, z: av.root.position.z });
      759 +    }
      760 +    if (targets.length === 0) return; // nobody to chase — enemies idle
      761 +
      762 +    // Walls are static — gather once per tick.
      763 +    const walls: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      764      for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      765 +      if (item.kind === 'wall') walls.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      766 +    }
      767 +    const blockR = WALL_CELL_HALF + 0.18; // wall half + enemy half
      768 +    const hitsWall = (x: number, z: number): boolean => {
      769 +      for (const w of walls) {
      770 +        if (Math.abs(x - w.x) < blockR && Math.abs(z - w.z) < blockR) return true;
      771 +      }
      772 +      return false;
      773 +    };
      774 +
      775 +    // Skull target selection — rotate through live players periodically.
      776 +    // Pick once per tick; individual skulls share the selection so the
      777 +    // hunt feels coordinated.
      778 +    const skullRetarget = enemyBehavior('skull').retargetMs ?? 4000;
      779 +    let skullTarget = targets[0];
      780 +    if (this.skullTargetUserId) {
      781 +      const t = targets.find((p) => p.id === this.skullTargetUserId);
      782 +      if (t) skullTarget = t;
      783 +    }
      784 +    if (now >= this.skullRetargetAt) {
      785 +      skullTarget = targets[Math.floor(Math.random() * targets.length)];
      786 +      this.skullTargetUserId = skullTarget.id;
      787 +      this.skullRetargetAt = now + skullRetarget;
      788 +    }
      789 +
      790 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      791        if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      792        const stats = enemyStats(item.type);
      793 +      const behav = enemyBehavior(item.type);
      794 +      const pos = item.object3D.position;
      795
      747 -      // Horizontal chase
      748 -      this.tempChase.set(
      749 -        this.tempPos.x - item.object3D.position.x,
      750 -        0,
      751 -        this.tempPos.z - item.object3D.position.z,
      752 -      );
      753 -      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      754 -      if (dist > 0.1) {
      755 -        const step = stats.speed * deltaSeconds;
      756 -        this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      757 -        item.object3D.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      758 -        item.object3D.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      796 +      // Pick a target per variant:
      797 +      //   skull → shared skullTarget
      798 +      //   everyone else → closest live player
      799 +      let target = targets[0];
      800 +      if (item.type === 'skull') {
      801 +        target = skullTarget;
      802 +      } else {
      803 +        let bestD2 = Infinity;
      804 +        for (const p of targets) {
      805 +          const dx = p.x - pos.x;
      806 +          const dz = p.z - pos.z;
      807 +          const d2 = dx * dx + dz * dz;
      808 +          if (d2 < bestD2) { bestD2 = d2; target = p; }
      809 +        }
      810        }
      811
      812 +      // Aggro: robots only chase if target is within radius; otherwise
      813 +      // they return to origin. null radius = always aggroed.
      814 +      let moveToX: number, moveToZ: number;
      815 +      if (behav.aggroRadius === null) {
      816 +        moveToX = target.x;
      817 +        moveToZ = target.z;
      818 +      } else {
      819 +        const dx = target.x - pos.x;
      820 +        const dz = target.z - pos.z;
      821 +        if (dx * dx + dz * dz < behav.aggroRadius * behav.aggroRadius) {
      822 +          moveToX = target.x;
      823 +          moveToZ = target.z;
      824 +        } else {
      825 +          moveToX = item.origin[0];
      826 +          moveToZ = item.origin[2];
      827 +        }
      828 +      }
      829 +
      830 +      // Move toward destination, optionally blocked by walls.
      831 +      const dx = moveToX - pos.x;
      832 +      const dz = moveToZ - pos.z;
      833 +      const dist = Math.hypot(dx, dz);
      834 +      if (dist > 0.05) {
      835 +        const step = Math.min(dist, stats.speed * deltaSeconds);
      836 +        const nx = pos.x + (dx * step) / dist;
      837 +        const nz = pos.z + (dz * step) / dist;
      838 +        if (behav.wallPass || !hitsWall(nx, nz)) {
      839 +          pos.x = nx;
      840 +          pos.z = nz;
      841 +        } else {
      842 +          // Slide along a wall: try each axis independently so enemies
      843 +          // don't just stick helplessly on a corner.
      844 +          if (!hitsWall(nx, pos.z))      pos.x = nx;
      845 +          else if (!hitsWall(pos.x, nz)) pos.z = nz;
      846 +        }
      847 +      }
      848 +
      849        // Vertical bob — ghosts float and weave, ground units skip this.
      850        if (stats.bobAmp > 0) {
      763 -        item.object3D.position.y =
      764 -          item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      851 +        pos.y = item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      852        }
      853      }
      854    }

⏺ Shipped. All changes in one big pass:

  HUD

  - Now parented to player.head directly — true fixed HUD with zero smoothing lag
  - Smaller (0.44m × 0.11m) and positioned at bottom-center so it's out of your way

  Game-over (dead = spectator)

  - Health hits 0 → no longer ends the round. You see a big red GAME OVER / Spectating banner
  - You keep moving around; AI ignores you, you can't pick up items, your weapons drop
  - Other players see your avatar at 30% opacity
  - Round ends naturally (timer / all goals / everyone dead)

  Grid reset

  - Server snapshots the layout at ROUND_START, restores it at ROUND_END, broadcasts GRID_SYNC
  - Pickup/kills during a round are temporary — portal's design survives across rounds

  Spawn points

  - Palette: Chair → Spawn (🪑) with a green tint on the grid
  - On round start, each VR player teleports to a chair; hash(userId) % chairs.length so two players get different chairs when
   possible; falls back to world origin

  Enemy AI

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │          │                                         Behavior                                          │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 🤖 Robot │ Aggro radius 3m — chases if you're close, else walks home                                 │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 👻 Ghost │ Slow (0.45 m/s), always chasing, phases through walls                                     │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 💀 Skull │ Normal speed, always chasing, retargets to random live player every 4s (multiplayer only) │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  - Non-phasing enemies slide along walls instead of sticking
  - Dead players filtered from target list

  Test

  1. Reload portal (server + VR)
  2. Place 🪑 × 2, 🗡️ , 🔫, ⭐ × 3, 🟦 wall row, 🤖, 👻, 🔥
  3. Start Round → teleport to chair → play
  4. Try dying intentionally (stand in 🔥) → see GAME OVER, round keeps running
  5. Watch 🤖 only approach when you're close, return home when you leave
  6. Watch 👻 walk through a 🟦 wall; robot + skull slide along it
  7. End round → portal items are back

❯ OK, THE CHAIRS AND WARP AREN'T WORKING

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ just curious, maybe i need to rethink the way the chairs work. can you do some resaerch to see if human players will be
  able to sync to the same physical world layout so they are operating in the same space? if so maybe chiar spawning and warp
   will mess with this. what are your thoughts? is it even possible to sync views across mutliple players when we are doing
  htis on a website? maybe not huh

⏺ Good instinct to pause on this — it's a real design/tech question. Let me lay out what's possible and how it affects
  chairs/warps.

  Two very different multiplayer modes

  1. Remote players (each in their own room)

  - Each headset has its own physical space — rooms don't overlap
  - They share a virtual world (what we're doing now)
  - When Ted turns his body, his avatar in Rae's view turns; they aren't physically near each other
  - Chairs + warps make sense here — teleporting your virtual rig doesn't violate physics because your physical room is yours
  alone
  - ✅ Easy on the web. We already do this via WebSocket.

  2. Co-located players (same physical room)

  - Multiple Quest headsets in the same room need a shared coordinate origin so when Rae points at "the table," Ted sees her
  pointing at the same real spot
  - The hard part: calibrating each headset's tracking so (0, 0, 0) means the same physical point
  - Chairs + warps would break this — teleporting your rig decouples your virtual position from your actual body in the room,
  so you'd "appear" across the virtual room from where you're physically standing

  What's available on the web for co-located?

  Very limited:
  - Meta Quest Browser v39+ has an experimental XRSharedReferenceSpace with a shared UUID. Two Quests in the same physical
  room can request the same origin and their world coords match. Your existing server.js already forwards a spaceId for
  exactly this case.
  - No standard WebXR API for cross-browser co-location. Apple, Chrome on Android, etc. don't have an equivalent.
  - Everything else (QR-code calibration, ARKit WorldMap, ARCore shared anchors) is native only — not available to WebXR.

  So on the web today: co-located multiplayer = Meta Quest only, and experimental.

  My take on chairs + warps

  They're fine for the remote mode we're actually running. If you ever go co-located, you'd want to:
  - Disable spawn teleport when multiple players share a spaceId (detect via the field your server already forwards)
  - Disable warps — or re-imagine them as "doorway portals" between virtual rooms where everyone physically walks through
  - Put chairs in the virtual world as physical seating cues — "walk to your chair to start" — rather than teleport targets

  Practical recommendation

  Right now: keep chairs/warps, they're good for the remote / single-player case, which is where the vast majority of testing
  will happen. The code already has spaceId plumbing for later.

  If you want the game to be "same-room co-op" a la Beat Saber party: that's Quest-only and a separate design pass. You'd rip
  out teleports, make the grid represent a real physical table or room footprint, and rely on players walking.

  Web vs native reality check

  - Web / WebXR: fantastic for rapid iteration, zero install, cross-device — but colocation is a Meta-only experimental
  feature.
  - Native Unity/Unreal: full access to shared anchors, QR calibration, platform SDKs — but you lose the "just open the URL"
  magic.

  You're not wrong to doubt it. Colocation on the open web is genuinely bleeding-edge. For a class project / prototype,
  remote-sync (what we have) is almost certainly the right bet.

  Want me to (a) leave things as-is, (b) wire a spaceId-aware skip for teleports, or (c) rethink chairs as physical-walk-to
  points with no teleport?

⏺ Add a dedicated tickWarps method after tickEnemyAI:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 65 lines
      710      }
      711    }
      712
      713 +  // Warp teleport — active any time, not gated on roundRunning. Walking
      714 +  // into a 🌀 sends the player to a random *other* warp on the grid.
      715 +  //
      716 +  // The re-trigger problem (player lands inside another warp and would
      717 +  // chain instantly) is solved two ways, together:
      718 +  //   1) Global cooldown blocks any warp for WARP_COOLDOWN_MS.
      719 +  //   2) The destination warp is *locked* until the player physically
      720 +  //      leaves its radius — so even after cooldown, you won't bounce
      721 +  //      back unless you move.
      722 +  private tickWarps() {
      723 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      724 +    const r2 = WARP_RADIUS * WARP_RADIUS;
      725 +    const now = performance.now();
      726 +
      727 +    // Collect all warps once — we need both "am I inside one?" and
      728 +    // "give me a random destination" lookups.
      729 +    const warps: { key: string; pos: Vector3 }[] = [];
      730 +    for (const [key, item] of this.spawnedEntities) {
      731 +      if (item.role === 'warp') warps.push({ key, pos: item.object3D.position });
      732 +    }
      733 +
      734 +    // Clear the locked warp once the player has stepped out of its radius.
      735 +    if (this.lockedWarpKey) {
      736 +      const locked = this.spawnedEntities.get(this.lockedWarpKey);
      737 +      if (!locked || locked.role !== 'warp') {
      738 +        this.lockedWarpKey = null;
      739 +      } else {
      740 +        const d2 = this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(locked.object3D.position);
      741 +        if (d2 > r2) this.lockedWarpKey = null;
      742 +      }
      743 +    }
      744 +
      745 +    if (now < this.warpCooldownUntil) return;
      746 +    if (warps.length < 2) return; // need at least two to make a trip
      747 +
      748 +    // Find the warp the player is currently inside (that isn't locked)
      749 +    let entered: { key: string; pos: Vector3 } | null = null;
      750 +    for (const w of warps) {
      751 +      if (w.key === this.lockedWarpKey) continue;
      752 +      if (this.tempPos.distanceToSquared(w.pos) < r2) { entered = w; break; }
      753 +    }
      754 +    if (!entered) return;
      755 +
      756 +    // Pick a random destination that isn't the one we're inside.
      757 +    const candidates = warps.filter((w) => w.key !== entered!.key);
      758 +    const dest = candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];
      759 +
      760 +    // Teleport by moving the XROrigin such that the player's head lands
      761 +    // at the destination. We don't touch Y so the player stays at the
      762 +    // floor level the locomotion system maintained.
      763 +    const headWorld = this.tempPos;             // already set above
      764 +    const originWorld = this.tempChase;         // reuse scratch
      765 +    this.player.getWorldPosition(originWorld);
      766 +    const dx = dest.pos.x - (headWorld.x - originWorld.x);
      767 +    const dz = dest.pos.z - (headWorld.z - originWorld.z);
      768 +    this.player.position.x = dx;
      769 +    this.player.position.z = dz;
      770 +
      771 +    this.warpCooldownUntil = now + WARP_COOLDOWN_MS;
      772 +    this.lockedWarpKey = dest.key;
      773 +    // Either hand works — prefer right if both are connected.
      774 +    FX.warp(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      775 +    console.log(`[Warp] ${entered.key} → ${dest.key}`);
      776 +  }
      777 +
      778    private applyPickup(role: ItemRole) {
      779      const leftPad  = this.input.gamepads.left;
      780      const rightPad = this.input.gamepads.right;

⏺ Shipped.

  What's new

  🌀 Warps
  - Place 2+ Warp items on the portal grid
  - In VR, walk into one → you're teleported to a random other warp
  - Big whoosh sound + strong haptic on arrival
  - Purple aura around the emoji so you can spot them
  - Breathing pulse when a round is live

  Re-trigger guards (both active simultaneously):
  1. 1.5s global cooldown — can't warp at all for 1.5s after a jump
  2. Destination lock — that specific warp won't retrigger until you physically step out of its radius. So even after the
  cooldown expires, you stay put unless you move.

  Naming
  - Palette label Portal → Warp (disambiguates from /portal.html URL)
  - Palette label Cube → Wall (since 🟦 already renders as a real 3D wall)
  - Internal data-type="warp" and new warp role

  Test

  1. Reload portal tab
  2. Place 🌀 🌀 🌀 at opposite corners + a 🟦 wall in the middle
  3. Walk into any 🌀 → whoosh, you're at a different one
  4. If you stay still on arrival → nothing happens (locked until you leave)
  5. Walk out and back in → teleport again

  Also: 🟦 cubes show in the palette as Wall now instead of Cube.

      34 +// designer's layout is authoritative across rounds.

      37  function endRound(reason) {
      38    if (!round.endsAt) return;
      39    if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }
      40    round.endsAt = 0;
      41    round.duration = 0;

      45 +    gridState.clear();
      46 +    for (const [k, v] of round.snapshot) gridState.set(k, v);

      728 +    // Round continues for everyone else. Local player becomes a
      729 +    // spectator: AI ignores them (see tickEnemyAI), they can't pick up
      730 +    // items (see handleCollisions), and the HUD shows GAME OVER.
      731 +    // Drops weapons since they're not participating anymore.
      732 +    this.isDead.value = true;

      736 +  }
      737 +
      738 +  // Teleport math — moves XROrigin so the player's head lands at `target`
      739 +  // on the XZ plane. Y is left alone so floor-level is preserved.
      740 +  private teleportPlayerTo(targetX: number, targetZ: number) {
      741 +    const headWorld = this.tempPos;
      742 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(headWorld);
      743 +    const originWorld = this.tempChase;
      744 +    this.player.getWorldPosition(originWorld);
      745 +    this.player.position.x = targetX - (headWorld.x - originWorld.x);
      746 +    this.player.position.z = targetZ - (headWorld.z - originWorld.z);
      747 +  }
      748 +
      749 +  // Pick a deterministic spawn: hash userId into the spawn list so two
      750 +  // players reliably end up at different chairs when there are enough.
      751 +  // Falls back to origin when no spawns are placed.
      752 +  private teleportToSpawn() {
      753 +    const spawns: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      754 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      755 +      if (item.role === 'spawn') {
      756 +        spawns.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      757 +      }
      758      }
      759 +    if (spawns.length === 0) {
      760 +      this.teleportPlayerTo(0, 0);
      761 +      return;
      762 +    }
      763 +    // Hash userId to a stable index. Portal observers have no userId
      764 +    // here — fallback to random for offline/dev.
      765 +    let h = 0;
      766 +    const id = this.userId ?? String(Math.random());
      767 +    for (let i = 0; i < id.length; i++) h = (h * 31 + id.charCodeAt(i)) | 0;
      768 +    const pick = spawns[Math.abs(h) % spawns.length];
      769 +    this.teleportPlayerTo(pick.x, pick.z);
      770    }
      771

      736      }

      738
      739 -  // Per-variant chase: speed + optional vertical bob for flying enemies.
      740 -  // Shared one-sin-per-frame phase keeps costs flat even with many ghosts.
      739 +  // Per-variant chase AI. Three behaviors stack on the same loop:
      740 +  //  - Robot: aggro only within radius, else walk home to origin.
      741 +  //  - Ghost: always chase (slow), passes through walls.
      742 +  //  - Skull: always chase, periodically switches target between
      743 +  //    live players (multiplayer flavor).
      744 +  //
      745 +  // Dead players are filtered out of the target list entirely so the
      746 +  // round can continue without the game turning into a corpse parade.
      747    private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      742 -    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      748 +    const now = performance.now();
      749 +
      750 +    // Gather live player positions. Own player excluded if dead.
      751 +    const targets: { id: string; x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      752 +    if (!this.isDead.peek()) {
      753 +      this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      754 +      targets.push({ id: this.userId ?? 'self', x: this.tempPos.x, z: this.tempPos.z });
      755 +    }
      756 +    for (const [uid, av] of this.avatars) {
      757 +      if (av.dead) continue;
      758 +      targets.push({ id: uid, x: av.root.position.x, z: av.root.position.z });
      759 +    }
      760 +    if (targets.length === 0) return; // nobody to chase — enemies idle
      761 +
      762 +    // Walls are static — gather once per tick.
      763 +    const walls: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      764      for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      765 +      if (item.kind === 'wall') walls.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      766 +    }
      767 +    const blockR = WALL_CELL_HALF + 0.18; // wall half + enemy half
      768 +    const hitsWall = (x: number, z: number): boolean => {
      769 +      for (const w of walls) {
      770 +        if (Math.abs(x - w.x) < blockR && Math.abs(z - w.z) < blockR) return true;
      771 +      }
      772 +      return false;
      773 +    };
      774 +
      775 +    // Skull target selection — rotate through live players periodically.
      776 +    // Pick once per tick; individual skulls share the selection so the
      777 +    // hunt feels coordinated.
      778 +    const skullRetarget = enemyBehavior('skull').retargetMs ?? 4000;
      779 +    let skullTarget = targets[0];
      780 +    if (this.skullTargetUserId) {
      781 +      const t = targets.find((p) => p.id === this.skullTargetUserId);
      782 +      if (t) skullTarget = t;
      783 +    }
      784 +    if (now >= this.skullRetargetAt) {
      785 +      skullTarget = targets[Math.floor(Math.random() * targets.length)];
      786 +      this.skullTargetUserId = skullTarget.id;
      787 +      this.skullRetargetAt = now + skullRetarget;
      788 +    }
      789 +
      790 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      791        if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      792        const stats = enemyStats(item.type);
      793 +      const behav = enemyBehavior(item.type);
      794 +      const pos = item.object3D.position;
      795
      747 -      // Horizontal chase
      748 -      this.tempChase.set(
      749 -        this.tempPos.x - item.object3D.position.x,
      750 -        0,
      751 -        this.tempPos.z - item.object3D.position.z,
      752 -      );
      753 -      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      754 -      if (dist > 0.1) {
      755 -        const step = stats.speed * deltaSeconds;
      756 -        this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      757 -        item.object3D.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      758 -        item.object3D.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      796 +      // Pick a target per variant:
      797 +      //   skull → shared skullTarget
      798 +      //   everyone else → closest live player
      799 +      let target = targets[0];
      800 +      if (item.type === 'skull') {
      801 +        target = skullTarget;
      802 +      } else {
      803 +        let bestD2 = Infinity;
      804 +        for (const p of targets) {
      805 +          const dx = p.x - pos.x;
      806 +          const dz = p.z - pos.z;
      807 +          const d2 = dx * dx + dz * dz;
      808 +          if (d2 < bestD2) { bestD2 = d2; target = p; }
      809 +        }
      810        }
      811
      812 +      // Aggro: robots only chase if target is within radius; otherwise
      813 +      // they return to origin. null radius = always aggroed.
      814 +      let moveToX: number, moveToZ: number;
      815 +      if (behav.aggroRadius === null) {
      816 +        moveToX = target.x;
      817 +        moveToZ = target.z;
      818 +      } else {
      819 +        const dx = target.x - pos.x;
      820 +        const dz = target.z - pos.z;
      821 +        if (dx * dx + dz * dz < behav.aggroRadius * behav.aggroRadius) {
      822 +          moveToX = target.x;
      823 +          moveToZ = target.z;
      824 +        } else {
      825 +          moveToX = item.origin[0];
      826 +          moveToZ = item.origin[2];
      827 +        }
      828 +      }
      829 +
      830 +      // Move toward destination, optionally blocked by walls.
      831 +      const dx = moveToX - pos.x;
      832 +      const dz = moveToZ - pos.z;
      833 +      const dist = Math.hypot(dx, dz);
      834 +      if (dist > 0.05) {
      835 +        const step = Math.min(dist, stats.speed * deltaSeconds);
      836 +        const nx = pos.x + (dx * step) / dist;
      837 +        const nz = pos.z + (dz * step) / dist;
      838 +        if (behav.wallPass || !hitsWall(nx, nz)) {
      839 +          pos.x = nx;
      840 +          pos.z = nz;
      841 +        } else {
      842 +          // Slide along a wall: try each axis independently so enemies
      843 +          // don't just stick helplessly on a corner.
      844 +          if (!hitsWall(nx, pos.z))      pos.x = nx;
      845 +          else if (!hitsWall(pos.x, nz)) pos.z = nz;
      846 +        }
      847 +      }
      848 +
      849        // Vertical bob — ghosts float and weave, ground units skip this.
      850        if (stats.bobAmp > 0) {
      763 -        item.object3D.position.y =
      764 -          item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      851 +        pos.y = item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      852        }
      853      }
      854    }

⏺ Shipped. All changes in one big pass:

  HUD

  - Now parented to player.head directly — true fixed HUD with zero smoothing lag
  - Smaller (0.44m × 0.11m) and positioned at bottom-center so it's out of your way

  Game-over (dead = spectator)

  - Health hits 0 → no longer ends the round. You see a big red GAME OVER / Spectating banner
  - You keep moving around; AI ignores you, you can't pick up items, your weapons drop
  - Other players see your avatar at 30% opacity
  - Round ends naturally (timer / all goals / everyone dead)

  Grid reset

  - Server snapshots the layout at ROUND_START, restores it at ROUND_END, broadcasts GRID_SYNC
  - Pickup/kills during a round are temporary — portal's design survives across rounds

  Spawn points

  - Palette: Chair → Spawn (🪑) with a green tint on the grid
  - On round start, each VR player teleports to a chair; hash(userId) % chairs.length so two players get different chairs when
   possible; falls back to world origin

  Enemy AI

  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 💀 Skull │ Normal speed, always chasing, retargets to random live player every 4s (multiplayer only) │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  - Non-phasing enemies slide along walls instead of sticking
  - Dead players filtered from target list

  Test

  2. Place 🪑 × 2, 🗡️ , 🔫, ⭐ × 3, 🟦 wall row, 🤖, 👻, 🔥
  3. Start Round → teleport to chair → play
  4. Try dying intentionally (stand in 🔥) → see GAME OVER, round keeps running
  5. Watch 🤖 only approach when you're close, return home when you leave
  6. Watch 👻 walk through a 🟦 wall; robot + skull slide along it
  7. End round → portal items are back

❯ OK, THE CHAIRS AND WARP AREN'T WORKING

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ just curious, maybe i need to rethink the way the chairs work. can you do some resaerch to see if human players will be
  able to sync to the same physical world layout so they are operating in the same space? if so maybe chiar spawning and warp
   will mess with this. what are your thoughts? is it even possible to sync views across mutliple players when we are doing
  htis on a website? maybe not huh

⏺ Good instinct to pause on this — it's a real design/tech question. Let me lay out what's possible and how it affects
  chairs/warps.

  Two very different multiplayer modes

  1. Remote players (each in their own room)

  - Each headset has its own physical space — rooms don't overlap
  - They share a virtual world (what we're doing now)
  - When Ted turns his body, his avatar in Rae's view turns; they aren't physically near each other
  - Chairs + warps make sense here — teleporting your virtual rig doesn't violate physics because your physical room is yours
  alone
  - ✅ Easy on the web. We already do this via WebSocket.

  2. Co-located players (same physical room)

  - Multiple Quest headsets in the same room need a shared coordinate origin so when Rae points at "the table," Ted sees her
  pointing at the same real spot
  - The hard part: calibrating each headset's tracking so (0, 0, 0) means the same physical point
  - Chairs + warps would break this — teleporting your rig decouples your virtual position from your actual body in the room,
  so you'd "appear" across the virtual room from where you're physically standing

  What's available on the web for co-located?

  Very limited:
  - Meta Quest Browser v39+ has an experimental XRSharedReferenceSpace with a shared UUID. Two Quests in the same physical
  room can request the same origin and their world coords match. Your existing server.js already forwards a spaceId for
  exactly this case.
  - No standard WebXR API for cross-browser co-location. Apple, Chrome on Android, etc. don't have an equivalent.
  - Everything else (QR-code calibration, ARKit WorldMap, ARCore shared anchors) is native only — not available to WebXR.

  So on the web today: co-located multiplayer = Meta Quest only, and experimental.

  My take on chairs + warps

  They're fine for the remote mode we're actually running. If you ever go co-located, you'd want to:
  - Disable spawn teleport when multiple players share a spaceId (detect via the field your server already forwards)
  - Disable warps — or re-imagine them as "doorway portals" between virtual rooms where everyone physically walks through
  - Put chairs in the virtual world as physical seating cues — "walk to your chair to start" — rather than teleport targets

  Practical recommendation

  Right now: keep chairs/warps, they're good for the remote / single-player case, which is where the vast majority of testing
  will happen. The code already has spaceId plumbing for later.

  If you want the game to be "same-room co-op" a la Beat Saber party: that's Quest-only and a separate design pass. You'd rip
  out teleports, make the grid represent a real physical table or room footprint, and rely on players walking.

  Web vs native reality check

  - Web / WebXR: fantastic for rapid iteration, zero install, cross-device — but colocation is a Meta-only experimental
  feature.
  - Native Unity/Unreal: full access to shared anchors, QR calibration, platform SDKs — but you lose the "just open the URL"
  magic.

  You're not wrong to doubt it. Colocation on the open web is genuinely bleeding-edge. For a class project / prototype,
  remote-sync (what we have) is almost certainly the right bet.

  Want me to (a) leave things as-is, (b) wire a spaceId-aware skip for teleports, or (c) rethink chairs as physical-walk-to
  points with no teleport?

      29
      30  // Round state. endsAt === 0 means no round in progress.
      31  // Duration is stored for reference (e.g. portals joining mid-round).
      32 -const round = { endsAt: 0, duration: 0, timeout: null };
      32 +// `snapshot` is a deep copy of gridState at ROUND_START — items the VR
      33 +// clients clear during the round are restored at ROUND_END so the
      34 +// designer's layout is authoritative across rounds.
      35 +const round = { endsAt: 0, duration: 0, timeout: null, snapshot: null };
      36
      37  function endRound(reason) {
      38    if (!round.endsAt) return;
      39    if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }
      40    round.endsAt = 0;
      41    round.duration = 0;
      42 +  // Restore the pre-round layout + push a full resync so every client
      43 +  // re-renders from the same authoritative snapshot.
      44 +  if (round.snapshot) {
      45 +    gridState.clear();
      46 +    for (const [k, v] of round.snapshot) gridState.set(k, v);
      47 +    round.snapshot = null;
      48 +  }
      49    console.log(`[⏱] Round ended (${reason})`);
      50    broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_END', reason });
      51 +  broadcast({ type: 'GRID_SYNC', grid: Object.fromEntries(gridState) });
      52  }

      724    }

      726    private playerDied() {

      727 +    console.log('[Round] Player died — entering spectator mode');
      728 +    // Round continues for everyone else. Local player becomes a
      729 +    // spectator: AI ignores them (see tickEnemyAI), they can't pick up
      730 +    // items (see handleCollisions), and the HUD shows GAME OVER.
      731 +    // Drops weapons since they're not participating anymore.
      732 +    this.isDead.value = true;
      733 +    this.equippedLeft.value = null;
      734 +    this.equippedRight.value = null;
      735 +    FX.roundLose(); // personal death cue — everyone else plays on
      736 +  }
      737 +
      738 +  // Teleport math — moves XROrigin so the player's head lands at `target`
      739 +  // on the XZ plane. Y is left alone so floor-level is preserved.
      740 +  private teleportPlayerTo(targetX: number, targetZ: number) {
      741 +    const headWorld = this.tempPos;
      742 +    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(headWorld);
      743 +    const originWorld = this.tempChase;
      744 +    this.player.getWorldPosition(originWorld);
      745 +    this.player.position.x = targetX - (headWorld.x - originWorld.x);
      746 +    this.player.position.z = targetZ - (headWorld.z - originWorld.z);
      747 +  }
      748 +
      749 +  // Pick a deterministic spawn: hash userId into the spawn list so two
      750 +  // players reliably end up at different chairs when there are enough.
      751 +  // Falls back to origin when no spawns are placed.
      752 +  private teleportToSpawn() {
      753 +    const spawns: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      754 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      755 +      if (item.role === 'spawn') {
      756 +        spawns.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      757 +      }
      758      }
      759 +    if (spawns.length === 0) {
      760 +      this.teleportPlayerTo(0, 0);
      761 +      return;
      762 +    }
      763 +    // Hash userId to a stable index. Portal observers have no userId
      764 +    // here — fallback to random for offline/dev.
      765 +    let h = 0;

⏺ Now rewrite tickEnemyAI with all the new logic:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 104 lines, removed 17 lines
      736      }
      737    }
      738
      739 -  // Per-variant chase: speed + optional vertical bob for flying enemies.
      740 -  // Shared one-sin-per-frame phase keeps costs flat even with many ghosts.
      739 +  // Per-variant chase AI. Three behaviors stack on the same loop:
      740 +  //  - Robot: aggro only within radius, else walk home to origin.
      741 +  //  - Ghost: always chase (slow), passes through walls.
      742 +  //  - Skull: always chase, periodically switches target between
      743 +  //    live players (multiplayer flavor).
      744 +  //
      745 +  // Dead players are filtered out of the target list entirely so the
      746 +  // round can continue without the game turning into a corpse parade.
      747    private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      742 -    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      748 +    const now = performance.now();
      749 +
      750 +    // Gather live player positions. Own player excluded if dead.
      751 +    const targets: { id: string; x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      752 +    if (!this.isDead.peek()) {
      753 +      this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      754 +      targets.push({ id: this.userId ?? 'self', x: this.tempPos.x, z: this.tempPos.z });
      755 +    }
      756 +    for (const [uid, av] of this.avatars) {
      757 +      if (av.dead) continue;
      758 +      targets.push({ id: uid, x: av.root.position.x, z: av.root.position.z });
      759 +    }
      760 +    if (targets.length === 0) return; // nobody to chase — enemies idle
      761 +
      762 +    // Walls are static — gather once per tick.
      763 +    const walls: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      764      for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      765 +      if (item.kind === 'wall') walls.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      766 +    }
      767 +    const blockR = WALL_CELL_HALF + 0.18; // wall half + enemy half
      768 +    const hitsWall = (x: number, z: number): boolean => {
      769 +      for (const w of walls) {
      770 +        if (Math.abs(x - w.x) < blockR && Math.abs(z - w.z) < blockR) return true;
      771 +      }
      772 +      return false;
      773 +    };
      774 +
      775 +    // Skull target selection — rotate through live players periodically.
      776 +    // Pick once per tick; individual skulls share the selection so the
      777 +    // hunt feels coordinated.
      778 +    const skullRetarget = enemyBehavior('skull').retargetMs ?? 4000;
      779 +    let skullTarget = targets[0];
      780 +    if (this.skullTargetUserId) {
      781 +      const t = targets.find((p) => p.id === this.skullTargetUserId);
      782 +      if (t) skullTarget = t;
      783 +    }
      784 +    if (now >= this.skullRetargetAt) {
      785 +      skullTarget = targets[Math.floor(Math.random() * targets.length)];
      786 +      this.skullTargetUserId = skullTarget.id;
      787 +      this.skullRetargetAt = now + skullRetarget;
      788 +    }
      789 +
      790 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      791        if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      792        const stats = enemyStats(item.type);
      793 +      const behav = enemyBehavior(item.type);
      794 +      const pos = item.object3D.position;
      795
      747 -      // Horizontal chase
      748 -      this.tempChase.set(
      749 -        this.tempPos.x - item.object3D.position.x,
      750 -        0,
      751 -        this.tempPos.z - item.object3D.position.z,
      752 -      );
      753 -      const dist = this.tempChase.length();
      754 -      if (dist > 0.1) {
      755 -        const step = stats.speed * deltaSeconds;
      756 -        this.tempChase.multiplyScalar(step / dist);
      757 -        item.object3D.position.x += this.tempChase.x;
      758 -        item.object3D.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      796 +      // Pick a target per variant:
      797 +      //   skull → shared skullTarget
      798 +      //   everyone else → closest live player
      799 +      let target = targets[0];
      800 +      if (item.type === 'skull') {
      801 +        target = skullTarget;
      802 +      } else {
      803 +        let bestD2 = Infinity;
      804 +        for (const p of targets) {
      805 +          const dx = p.x - pos.x;
      806 +          const dz = p.z - pos.z;
      807 +          const d2 = dx * dx + dz * dz;
      808 +          if (d2 < bestD2) { bestD2 = d2; target = p; }
      809 +        }
      810        }
      811
      812 +      // Aggro: robots only chase if target is within radius; otherwise
      813 +      // they return to origin. null radius = always aggroed.
      814 +      let moveToX: number, moveToZ: number;
      815 +      if (behav.aggroRadius === null) {
      816 +        moveToX = target.x;
      817 +        moveToZ = target.z;
      818 +      } else {
      819 +        const dx = target.x - pos.x;
      820 +        const dz = target.z - pos.z;
      821 +        if (dx * dx + dz * dz < behav.aggroRadius * behav.aggroRadius) {
      822 +          moveToX = target.x;
      823 +          moveToZ = target.z;
      824 +        } else {
      825 +          moveToX = item.origin[0];
      826 +          moveToZ = item.origin[2];
      827 +        }
      828 +      }
      829 +
      830 +      // Move toward destination, optionally blocked by walls.
      831 +      const dx = moveToX - pos.x;
      832 +      const dz = moveToZ - pos.z;
      833 +      const dist = Math.hypot(dx, dz);
      834 +      if (dist > 0.05) {
      835 +        const step = Math.min(dist, stats.speed * deltaSeconds);
      836 +        const nx = pos.x + (dx * step) / dist;
      837 +        const nz = pos.z + (dz * step) / dist;
      838 +        if (behav.wallPass || !hitsWall(nx, nz)) {
      839 +          pos.x = nx;
      840 +          pos.z = nz;
      841 +        } else {
      842 +          // Slide along a wall: try each axis independently so enemies
      843 +          // don't just stick helplessly on a corner.
      844 +          if (!hitsWall(nx, pos.z))      pos.x = nx;
      845 +          else if (!hitsWall(pos.x, nz)) pos.z = nz;
      846 +        }
      847 +      }
      848 +
      849        // Vertical bob — ghosts float and weave, ground units skip this.
      850        if (stats.bobAmp > 0) {
      763 -        item.object3D.position.y =
      764 -          item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      851 +        pos.y = item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      852        }
      853      }
      854    }

⏺ Shipped. All changes in one big pass:

  HUD

  - Now parented to player.head directly — true fixed HUD with zero smoothing lag
  - Smaller (0.44m × 0.11m) and positioned at bottom-center so it's out of your way

  Game-over (dead = spectator)

  - Health hits 0 → no longer ends the round. You see a big red GAME OVER / Spectating banner
  - You keep moving around; AI ignores you, you can't pick up items, your weapons drop
  - Other players see your avatar at 30% opacity
  - Round ends naturally (timer / all goals / everyone dead)

  Grid reset

  - Server snapshots the layout at ROUND_START, restores it at ROUND_END, broadcasts GRID_SYNC
  - Pickup/kills during a round are temporary — portal's design survives across rounds

  Spawn points

  - Palette: Chair → Spawn (🪑) with a green tint on the grid
  - On round start, each VR player teleports to a chair; hash(userId) % chairs.length so two players get different chairs when
   possible; falls back to world origin

  Enemy AI

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │          │                                         Behavior                                          │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 🤖 Robot │ Aggro radius 3m — chases if you're close, else walks home                                 │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 👻 Ghost │ Slow (0.45 m/s), always chasing, phases through walls                                     │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 💀 Skull │ Normal speed, always chasing, retargets to random live player every 4s (multiplayer only) │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  - Non-phasing enemies slide along walls instead of sticking
  - Dead players filtered from target list

  Test

  1. Reload portal (server + VR)
  2. Place 🪑 × 2, 🗡️ , 🔫, ⭐ × 3, 🟦 wall row, 🤖, 👻, 🔥
  3. Start Round → teleport to chair → play
  4. Try dying intentionally (stand in 🔥) → see GAME OVER, round keeps running
  5. Watch 🤖 only approach when you're close, return home when you leave
  6. Watch 👻 walk through a 🟦 wall; robot + skull slide along it
  7. End round → portal items are back

❯ OK, THE CHAIRS AND WARP AREN'T WORKING

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ just curious, maybe i need to rethink the way the chairs work. can you do some resaerch to see if human players will be
  able to sync to the same physical world layout so they are operating in the same space? if so maybe chiar spawning and warp
   will mess with this. what are your thoughts? is it even possible to sync views across mutliple players when we are doing
  htis on a website? maybe not huh

⏺ Good instinct to pause on this — it's a real design/tech question. Let me lay out what's possible and how it affects
  chairs/warps.

  Two very different multiplayer modes

  1. Remote players (each in their own room)

  - Each headset has its own physical space — rooms don't overlap
  - They share a virtual world (what we're doing now)
  - When Ted turns his body, his avatar in Rae's view turns; they aren't physically near each other
  - Chairs + warps make sense here — teleporting your virtual rig doesn't violate physics because your physical room is yours
  alone
  - ✅ Easy on the web. We already do this via WebSocket.

  2. Co-located players (same physical room)

  - Multiple Quest headsets in the same room need a shared coordinate origin so when Rae points at "the table," Ted sees her
  pointing at the same real spot
  - The hard part: calibrating each headset's tracking so (0, 0, 0) means the same physical point
  - Chairs + warps would break this — teleporting your rig decouples your virtual position from your actual body in the room,
  so you'd "appear" across the virtual room from where you're physically standing

  What's available on the web for co-located?

  Very limited:
  - Meta Quest Browser v39+ has an experimental XRSharedReferenceSpace with a shared UUID. Two Quests in the same physical
  room can request the same origin and their world coords match. Your existing server.js already forwards a spaceId for
  exactly this case.
  - No standard WebXR API for cross-browser co-location. Apple, Chrome on Android, etc. don't have an equivalent.
  - Everything else (QR-code calibration, ARKit WorldMap, ARCore shared anchors) is native only — not available to WebXR.

  So on the web today: co-located multiplayer = Meta Quest only, and experimental.

  My take on chairs + warps

  They're fine for the remote mode we're actually running. If you ever go co-located, you'd want to:
  - Disable spawn teleport when multiple players share a spaceId (detect via the field your server already forwards)
  - Disable warps — or re-imagine them as "doorway portals" between virtual rooms where everyone physically walks through
  - Put chairs in the virtual world as physical seating cues — "walk to your chair to start" — rather than teleport targets

  Practical recommendation

  Right now: keep chairs/warps, they're good for the remote / single-player case, which is where the vast majority of testing
  will happen. The code already has spaceId plumbing for later.

  If you want the game to be "same-room co-op" a la Beat Saber party: that's Quest-only and a separate design pass. You'd rip
  out teleports, make the grid represent a real physical table or room footprint, and rely on players walking.

  Web vs native reality check

  - Web / WebXR: fantastic for rapid iteration, zero install, cross-device — but colocation is a Meta-only experimental
  feature.
  - Native Unity/Unreal: full access to shared anchors, QR calibration, platform SDKs — but you lose the "just open the URL"
  magic.

  You're not wrong to doubt it. Colocation on the open web is genuinely bleeding-edge. For a class project / prototype,
  remote-sync (what we have) is almost certainly the right bet.

  Want me to (a) leave things as-is, (b) wire a spaceId-aware skip for teleports, or (c) rethink chairs as physical-walk-to
  points with no teleport?

  1. Remove warp entirely
  2. Enforce single chair placement
  3. "Chair" → "Starting Point"
  4. Start Round requires chair; shows popup if missing
  5. VR players see "Go to 🪑 and press SELECT" → confirm → round actually starts

⏺ Update(portal.html)
  ⎿  Added 18 lines
      1265          cell.addEventListener('dragend', cell._dragEnd);
      1266        }
      1267
      1268 +      // Only one starting-point chair allowed on the grid. Placing a
      1269 +      // new one clears the existing one first so the designer never has
      1270 +      // to hunt down a duplicate.
      1271 +      function findExistingChairKey() {
      1272 +        for (const [k, v] of state) {
      1273 +          if (v.type === 'chair') return k;
      1274 +        }
      1275 +        return null;
      1276 +      }
      1277 +
      1278        // ── State helpers (optimistic + server sync) ─────────────
      1279        function placeItem(key, item) {
      1280          // Always attach a role so VR can branch behavior without parsing icons.
      1281          const withRole = item.role ? item : { ...item, role: roleFor(item.icon) };
      1282 +
      1283 +        // Single-chair rule: if the new item is a chair and one already
      1284 +        // exists somewhere else, clear the old one first.
      1285 +        if (withRole.type === 'chair') {
      1286 +          const existing = findExistingChairKey();
      1287 +          if (existing && existing !== key) clearCell(existing);
      1288 +        }
      1289 +
      1290          state.set(key, withRole);
      1291          syncGrids();
      1292          wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', key, item: withRole });

      45 +function cancelPending() {
      46 +  if (!round.pending) return;
      47 +  round.pending = false;
      48 +  round.pendingDuration = 0;
      49 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_CANCEL' });
      50 +  console.log('[⏱] Round request cancelled');
      51 +}
      52 +
      53  function endRound(reason) {
      54    if (!round.endsAt) return;
      55    if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }

⏺ Now handle ROUND_REQUEST / ROUND_READY / ROUND_CANCEL:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 37 lines
      146          break;
      147
      148        case 'ROUND_START':
      149 +        // Legacy — straight-to-start path (kept for any client that
      150 +        // hasn't migrated to the pending flow).
      151          startRound(msg.duration);
      152          break;
      153
      154 +      case 'ROUND_REQUEST': {
      155 +        // Portal asked to start; enter pending state. VR players must
      156 +        // walk to the chair + press SELECT to confirm → actual start.
      157 +        if (round.endsAt || round.pending) break;
      158 +        const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(msg.duration || 30)));
      159 +        round.pending = true;
      160 +        round.pendingDuration = d;
      161 +        console.log(`[⏱] Round requested (${d}s) — waiting for VR ready`);
      162 +        broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_PENDING', duration: d });
      163 +        // If there are no VR players at all, auto-start so the portal
      164 +        // operator can still run solo tests.
      165 +        let vrPlayers = 0;
      166 +        for (const u of users.values()) vrPlayers++;
      167 +        if (vrPlayers === 0) {
      168 +          console.log('[⏱] No VR players connected — auto-starting');
      169 +          round.pending = false;
      170 +          startRound(d);
      171 +        }
      172 +        break;
      173 +      }
      174 +
      175 +      case 'ROUND_READY':
      176 +        // First VR player to confirm kicks off the actual round.
      177 +        if (round.pending) {
      178 +          const d = round.pendingDuration;
      179 +          round.pending = false;
      180 +          round.pendingDuration = 0;
      181 +          startRound(d);
      182 +        }
      183 +        break;
      184 +
      185 +      case 'ROUND_CANCEL':
      186 +        cancelPending();
      187 +        break;
      188 +
      189        case 'ROUND_END':
      190          endRound(msg.reason || 'host-stopped');
      191          break;

      740 -  // Shared one-sin-per-frame phase keeps costs flat even with many ghosts.
      739 +  // Per-variant chase AI. Three behaviors stack on the same loop:
      740 +  //  - Robot: aggro only within radius, else walk home to origin.
      741 +  //  - Ghost: always chase (slow), passes through walls.
      742 +  //  - Skull: always chase, periodically switches target between
      743 +  //    live players (multiplayer flavor).
      744 +  //
      745 +  // Dead players are filtered out of the target list entirely so the
      746 +  // round can continue without the game turning into a corpse parade.
      747    private tickEnemyAI(deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      742 -    this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      748 +    const now = performance.now();
      749 +
      750 +    // Gather live player positions. Own player excluded if dead.
      751 +    const targets: { id: string; x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      752 +    if (!this.isDead.peek()) {
      753 +      this.player.head.getWorldPosition(this.tempPos);
      754 +      targets.push({ id: this.userId ?? 'self', x: this.tempPos.x, z: this.tempPos.z });
      755 +    }
      756 +    for (const [uid, av] of this.avatars) {
      757 +      if (av.dead) continue;
      758 +      targets.push({ id: uid, x: av.root.position.x, z: av.root.position.z });
      759 +    }
      760 +    if (targets.length === 0) return; // nobody to chase — enemies idle
      761 +
      762 +    // Walls are static — gather once per tick.
      763 +    const walls: { x: number; z: number }[] = [];
      764      for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      765 +      if (item.kind === 'wall') walls.push({ x: item.origin[0], z: item.origin[2] });
      766 +    }
      767 +    const blockR = WALL_CELL_HALF + 0.18; // wall half + enemy half
      768 +    const hitsWall = (x: number, z: number): boolean => {
      769 +      for (const w of walls) {
      770 +        if (Math.abs(x - w.x) < blockR && Math.abs(z - w.z) < blockR) return true;
      771 +      }
      772 +      return false;
      773 +    };
      774 +
      775 +    // Skull target selection — rotate through live players periodically.
      776 +    // Pick once per tick; individual skulls share the selection so the
      777 +    // hunt feels coordinated.
      778 +    const skullRetarget = enemyBehavior('skull').retargetMs ?? 4000;
      779 +    let skullTarget = targets[0];
      780 +    if (this.skullTargetUserId) {
      781 +      const t = targets.find((p) => p.id === this.skullTargetUserId);
      782 +      if (t) skullTarget = t;
      783 +    }
      784 +    if (now >= this.skullRetargetAt) {
      785 +      skullTarget = targets[Math.floor(Math.random() * targets.length)];
      786 +      this.skullTargetUserId = skullTarget.id;
      787 +      this.skullRetargetAt = now + skullRetarget;
      788 +    }
      789 +
      790 +    for (const item of this.spawnedEntities.values()) {
      791        if (item.role !== 'enemy') continue;
      792        const stats = enemyStats(item.type);
      793 +      const behav = enemyBehavior(item.type);
      794 +      const pos = item.object3D.position;
      795
      747 -      // Horizontal chase
      748 -      this.tempChase.set(
      749 -        this.tempPos.x - item.object3D.position.x,
      750 -        0,
      751 -        this.tempPos.z - item.object3D.position.z,
      752 -      );
      753 -      const dist = this.tempChase.length();

      755 -        const step = stats.speed * deltaSeconds;

      758 -        item.object3D.position.z += this.tempChase.z;
      796 +      // Pick a target per variant:
      797 +      //   skull → shared skullTarget
      798 +      //   everyone else → closest live player
      799 +      let target = targets[0];
      800 +      if (item.type === 'skull') {
      801 +        target = skullTarget;
      802 +      } else {
      803 +        let bestD2 = Infinity;
      804 +        for (const p of targets) {
      805 +          const dx = p.x - pos.x;
      806 +          const dz = p.z - pos.z;
      807 +          const d2 = dx * dx + dz * dz;
      808 +          if (d2 < bestD2) { bestD2 = d2; target = p; }
      809 +        }
      810        }
      811
      812 +      // Aggro: robots only chase if target is within radius; otherwise
      813 +      // they return to origin. null radius = always aggroed.
      814 +      let moveToX: number, moveToZ: number;
      815 +      if (behav.aggroRadius === null) {
      816 +        moveToX = target.x;
      817 +        moveToZ = target.z;
      818 +      } else {
      819 +        const dx = target.x - pos.x;
      820 +        const dz = target.z - pos.z;
      821 +        if (dx * dx + dz * dz < behav.aggroRadius * behav.aggroRadius) {
      822 +          moveToX = target.x;
      823 +          moveToZ = target.z;
      824 +        } else {
      825 +          moveToX = item.origin[0];
      826 +          moveToZ = item.origin[2];
      827 +        }
      828 +      }
      829 +
      830 +      // Move toward destination, optionally blocked by walls.
      831 +      const dx = moveToX - pos.x;
      832 +      const dz = moveToZ - pos.z;
      833 +      const dist = Math.hypot(dx, dz);
      834 +      if (dist > 0.05) {
      835 +        const step = Math.min(dist, stats.speed * deltaSeconds);
      836 +        const nx = pos.x + (dx * step) / dist;
      837 +        const nz = pos.z + (dz * step) / dist;
      838 +        if (behav.wallPass || !hitsWall(nx, nz)) {
      839 +          pos.x = nx;
      840 +          pos.z = nz;
      841 +        } else {
      842 +          // Slide along a wall: try each axis independently so enemies
      843 +          // don't just stick helplessly on a corner.
      844 +          if (!hitsWall(nx, pos.z))      pos.x = nx;
      845 +          else if (!hitsWall(pos.x, nz)) pos.z = nz;
      846 +        }
      847 +      }
      848 +
      849        // Vertical bob — ghosts float and weave, ground units skip this.
      850        if (stats.bobAmp > 0) {
      763 -        item.object3D.position.y =
      764 -          item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      851 +        pos.y = item.origin[1] + 0.5 + Math.sin(time * stats.bobSpeed) * stats.bobAmp;
      852        }
      853      }
      854    }

⏺ Shipped. All changes in one big pass:

  HUD

  - Now parented to player.head directly — true fixed HUD with zero smoothing lag
  - Smaller (0.44m × 0.11m) and positioned at bottom-center so it's out of your way

  Game-over (dead = spectator)

  - Health hits 0 → no longer ends the round. You see a big red GAME OVER / Spectating banner
  - You keep moving around; AI ignores you, you can't pick up items, your weapons drop
  - Other players see your avatar at 30% opacity
  - Round ends naturally (timer / all goals / everyone dead)

  Grid reset

  - Server snapshots the layout at ROUND_START, restores it at ROUND_END, broadcasts GRID_SYNC
  - Pickup/kills during a round are temporary — portal's design survives across rounds

  Spawn points

  - Palette: Chair → Spawn (🪑) with a green tint on the grid
  - On round start, each VR player teleports to a chair; hash(userId) % chairs.length so two players get different chairs when
   possible; falls back to world origin

  Enemy AI

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │          │                                         Behavior                                          │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 🤖 Robot │ Aggro radius 3m — chases if you're close, else walks home                                 │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 👻 Ghost │ Slow (0.45 m/s), always chasing, phases through walls                                     │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 💀 Skull │ Normal speed, always chasing, retargets to random live player every 4s (multiplayer only) │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  - Non-phasing enemies slide along walls instead of sticking
  - Dead players filtered from target list

  Test

  1. Reload portal (server + VR)
  2. Place 🪑 × 2, 🗡️ , 🔫, ⭐ × 3, 🟦 wall row, 🤖, 👻, 🔥
  3. Start Round → teleport to chair → play
  4. Try dying intentionally (stand in 🔥) → see GAME OVER, round keeps running
  5. Watch 🤖 only approach when you're close, return home when you leave
  6. Watch 👻 walk through a 🟦 wall; robot + skull slide along it
  7. End round → portal items are back
