❯ OK, THE CHAIRS AND WARP AREN'T WORKING

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ just curious, maybe i need to rethink the way the chairs work. can you do some resaerch to see if human players will be
  able to sync to the same physical world layout so they are operating in the same space? if so maybe chiar spawning and warp
   will mess with this. what are your thoughts? is it even possible to sync views across mutliple players when we are doing
  htis on a website? maybe not huh

⏺ Good instinct to pause on this — it's a real design/tech question. Let me lay out what's possible and how it affects
  chairs/warps.

  Two very different multiplayer modes

  1. Remote players (each in their own room)

  - Each headset has its own physical space — rooms don't overlap
  - They share a virtual world (what we're doing now)
  - When Ted turns his body, his avatar in Rae's view turns; they aren't physically near each other
  - Chairs + warps make sense here — teleporting your virtual rig doesn't violate physics because your physical room is yours
  alone
  - ✅ Easy on the web. We already do this via WebSocket.

  2. Co-located players (same physical room)

  - Multiple Quest headsets in the same room need a shared coordinate origin so when Rae points at "the table," Ted sees her
  pointing at the same real spot
  - The hard part: calibrating each headset's tracking so (0, 0, 0) means the same physical point
  - Chairs + warps would break this — teleporting your rig decouples your virtual position from your actual body in the room,
  so you'd "appear" across the virtual room from where you're physically standing

  What's available on the web for co-located?

  Very limited:
  - Meta Quest Browser v39+ has an experimental XRSharedReferenceSpace with a shared UUID. Two Quests in the same physical
  room can request the same origin and their world coords match. Your existing server.js already forwards a spaceId for
  exactly this case.
  - No standard WebXR API for cross-browser co-location. Apple, Chrome on Android, etc. don't have an equivalent.
  - Everything else (QR-code calibration, ARKit WorldMap, ARCore shared anchors) is native only — not available to WebXR.

  So on the web today: co-located multiplayer = Meta Quest only, and experimental.

  My take on chairs + warps

  They're fine for the remote mode we're actually running. If you ever go co-located, you'd want to:
  - Disable spawn teleport when multiple players share a spaceId (detect via the field your server already forwards)
  - Disable warps — or re-imagine them as "doorway portals" between virtual rooms where everyone physically walks through
  - Put chairs in the virtual world as physical seating cues — "walk to your chair to start" — rather than teleport targets

  Practical recommendation

  Right now: keep chairs/warps, they're good for the remote / single-player case, which is where the vast majority of testing
  will happen. The code already has spaceId plumbing for later.

  If you want the game to be "same-room co-op" a la Beat Saber party: that's Quest-only and a separate design pass. You'd rip
  out teleports, make the grid represent a real physical table or room footprint, and rely on players walking.

  Web vs native reality check

  - Web / WebXR: fantastic for rapid iteration, zero install, cross-device — but colocation is a Meta-only experimental
  feature.
  - Native Unity/Unreal: full access to shared anchors, QR calibration, platform SDKs — but you lose the "just open the URL"
  magic.

  You're not wrong to doubt it. Colocation on the open web is genuinely bleeding-edge. For a class project / prototype,
  remote-sync (what we have) is almost certainly the right bet.

  Want me to (a) leave things as-is, (b) wire a spaceId-aware skip for teleports, or (c) rethink chairs as physical-walk-to
  points with no teleport?

      1269 +      // new one clears the existing one first so the designer never has
      1270 +      // to hunt down a duplicate.
      1271 +      function findExistingChairKey() {
      1272 +        for (const [k, v] of state) {
      1273 +          if (v.type === 'chair') return k;
      1274 +        }
      1275 +        return null;

      1279        function placeItem(key, item) {
      1280          // Always attach a role so VR can branch behavior without parsing icons.
      1281          const withRole = item.role ? item : { ...item, role: roleFor(item.icon) };

      153
      154 +      case 'ROUND_REQUEST': {
      155 +        // Portal asked to start; enter pending state. VR players must
      156 +        // walk to the chair + press SELECT to confirm → actual start.
      157 +        if (round.endsAt || round.pending) break;
      158 +        const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(msg.duration || 30)));
      159 +        round.pending = true;
      160 +        round.pendingDuration = d;
      161 +        console.log(`[⏱] Round requested (${d}s) — waiting for VR ready`);
      162 +        broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_PENDING', duration: d });
      163 +        // If there are no VR players at all, auto-start so the portal
      164 +        // operator can still run solo tests.
      165 +        let vrPlayers = 0;
      166 +        for (const u of users.values()) vrPlayers++;
      167 +        if (vrPlayers === 0) {
      168 +          console.log('[⏱] No VR players connected — auto-starting');
      169 +          round.pending = false;
      170 +          startRound(d);
      171 +        }
      172 +        break;
      173 +      }
      174 +
      175 +      case 'ROUND_READY':
      176 +        // First VR player to confirm kicks off the actual round.
      177 +        if (round.pending) {
      178 +          const d = round.pendingDuration;
      179 +          round.pending = false;
      180 +          round.pendingDuration = 0;
      181 +          startRound(d);
      182 +        }
      183 +        break;
      184 +
      185 +      case 'ROUND_CANCEL':
      186 +        cancelPending();
      187 +        break;
      188 +
      189        case 'ROUND_END':
      190          endRound(msg.reason || 'host-stopped');
      191          break;

      Chair ready-check flow; drop warps; one chair only…)
  ⎿  [game-idea acdccf5] Chair ready-check flow; drop warps; one chair only

⏺ Shipped. Reload both tabs (portal + VR) since server restarted.

  New flow

  1. Portal → click Start Round without a chair placed → red toast pops up: "Please add a 🪑 starting point to the map…"
  2. Place a Start Pt (🪑). Dragging another chair clears the first one — there's always exactly one.
  3. Click Start Round → server enters pending → button now reads Cancel · Waiting for VR…
  4. In VR, an amber banner appears: 🪑 STAND HERE / Press SELECT to start round
  5. Walk to the chair (within ~1m), press either controller's primary Select button
  6. Round starts for everyone, timer runs, etc.

  Edge cases handled

  - No VR players connected → portal auto-starts solo (for testing)
  - Portal clicks Cancel while pending → everyone goes back to idle
  - Portal tab reloads mid-pending → picks up state from WELCOME
  - Warps are gone — palette, role, VR logic, CSS all stripped

  Deleted

  - 🌀 Warp palette item (both layouts)
  - role: 'warp' from ItemRole
  - tickWarps + cooldown/lock state
  - WARP_RADIUS / WARP_COOLDOWN_MS
  - Warp CSS tint

  Test it and let me know what you see.

✻ Crunched for 7m 41s

❯ change teh way the skulls work and robots. the robots choos a random direction and go in that direction until they either
  hit a wall or the edge of the space, and when they do so, then they pick a new random direction and go straight in that
  direction. skulls will move in circles around their spawning point, they can go through walls, the will move a random
  distance away from thier starting point (a distance not longer than the smaller width of the game space) and then circle
  thier spawn point contiuously. they can also go outside teh edges of the game board. i want there to be a red overlay that
  appears when you get hit by a hazard, then ther eis a cool down so you cannot get hit again, there needs to be a sound
  effect to signify you got hit, like a oof.

❯ OK, THE CHAIRS AND WARP AREN'T WORKING

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ just curious, maybe i need to rethink the way the chairs work. can you do some resaerch to see if human players will be
  able to sync to the same physical world layout so they are operating in the same space? if so maybe chiar spawning and warp
   will mess with this. what are your thoughts? is it even possible to sync views across mutliple players when we are doing
  htis on a website? maybe not huh

⏺ Good instinct to pause on this — it's a real design/tech question. Let me lay out what's possible and how it affects
  chairs/warps.

  Two very different multiplayer modes

  1. Remote players (each in their own room)

  - Each headset has its own physical space — rooms don't overlap
  - They share a virtual world (what we're doing now)
  - When Ted turns his body, his avatar in Rae's view turns; they aren't physically near each other
  - Chairs + warps make sense here — teleporting your virtual rig doesn't violate physics because your physical room is yours
  alone
  - ✅ Easy on the web. We already do this via WebSocket.

  2. Co-located players (same physical room)

  - Multiple Quest headsets in the same room need a shared coordinate origin so when Rae points at "the table," Ted sees her
  pointing at the same real spot
  - The hard part: calibrating each headset's tracking so (0, 0, 0) means the same physical point
  - Chairs + warps would break this — teleporting your rig decouples your virtual position from your actual body in the room,
  so you'd "appear" across the virtual room from where you're physically standing

  What's available on the web for co-located?

  Very limited:
  - Meta Quest Browser v39+ has an experimental XRSharedReferenceSpace with a shared UUID. Two Quests in the same physical
  room can request the same origin and their world coords match. Your existing server.js already forwards a spaceId for
  exactly this case.
  - No standard WebXR API for cross-browser co-location. Apple, Chrome on Android, etc. don't have an equivalent.
  - Everything else (QR-code calibration, ARKit WorldMap, ARCore shared anchors) is native only — not available to WebXR.

  So on the web today: co-located multiplayer = Meta Quest only, and experimental.

  My take on chairs + warps

  They're fine for the remote mode we're actually running. If you ever go co-located, you'd want to:
  - Disable spawn teleport when multiple players share a spaceId (detect via the field your server already forwards)
  - Disable warps — or re-imagine them as "doorway portals" between virtual rooms where everyone physically walks through
  - Put chairs in the virtual world as physical seating cues — "walk to your chair to start" — rather than teleport targets

  Practical recommendation

  Right now: keep chairs/warps, they're good for the remote / single-player case, which is where the vast majority of testing
  will happen. The code already has spaceId plumbing for later.

  If you want the game to be "same-room co-op" a la Beat Saber party: that's Quest-only and a separate design pass. You'd rip
  out teleports, make the grid represent a real physical table or room footprint, and rely on players walking.

  Web vs native reality check

  - Web / WebXR: fantastic for rapid iteration, zero install, cross-device — but colocation is a Meta-only experimental
  feature.
  - Native Unity/Unreal: full access to shared anchors, QR calibration, platform SDKs — but you lose the "just open the URL"
  magic.

  You're not wrong to doubt it. Colocation on the open web is genuinely bleeding-edge. For a class project / prototype,
  remote-sync (what we have) is almost certainly the right bet.

  Want me to (a) leave things as-is, (b) wire a spaceId-aware skip for teleports, or (c) rethink chairs as physical-walk-to
  points with no teleport?

      1268 +      // Only one starting-point chair allowed on the grid. Placing a
      1269 +      // new one clears the existing one first so the designer never has
      1270 +      // to hunt down a duplicate.
      1271 +      function findExistingChairKey() {
      1272 +        for (const [k, v] of state) {
      1273 +          if (v.type === 'chair') return k;
      1274 +        }
      1275 +        return null;
      1276 +      }
      1277 +
      1278        // ── State helpers (optimistic + server sync) ─────────────
      1279        function placeItem(key, item) {
      1280          // Always attach a role so VR can branch behavior without parsing icons.
      1281          const withRole = item.role ? item : { ...item, role: roleFor(item.icon) };
      1282 +
      1283 +        // Single-chair rule: if the new item is a chair and one already
      1284 +        // exists somewhere else, clear the old one first.
      1285 +        if (withRole.type === 'chair') {
      1286 +          const existing = findExistingChairKey();
      1287 +          if (existing && existing !== key) clearCell(existing);
      1288 +        }
      1289 +
      1290          state.set(key, withRole);
      1291          syncGrids();

      47 +  round.pending = false;

      54    if (!round.endsAt) return;

⏺ Now handle ROUND_REQUEST / ROUND_READY / ROUND_CANCEL:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 37 lines
      146          break;
      147
      148        case 'ROUND_START':
      149 +        // Legacy — straight-to-start path (kept for any client that
      150 +        // hasn't migrated to the pending flow).
      151          startRound(msg.duration);
      152          break;
      153
      154 +      case 'ROUND_REQUEST': {
      155 +        // Portal asked to start; enter pending state. VR players must
      156 +        // walk to the chair + press SELECT to confirm → actual start.
      157 +        if (round.endsAt || round.pending) break;
      158 +        const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(msg.duration || 30)));
      159 +        round.pending = true;
      160 +        round.pendingDuration = d;
      161 +        console.log(`[⏱] Round requested (${d}s) — waiting for VR ready`);
      162 +        broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_PENDING', duration: d });
      163 +        // If there are no VR players at all, auto-start so the portal
      164 +        // operator can still run solo tests.
      165 +        let vrPlayers = 0;
      166 +        for (const u of users.values()) vrPlayers++;
      167 +        if (vrPlayers === 0) {
      168 +          console.log('[⏱] No VR players connected — auto-starting');
      169 +          round.pending = false;
      170 +          startRound(d);
      171 +        }
      172 +        break;
      173 +      }
      174 +
      175 +      case 'ROUND_READY':
      176 +        // First VR player to confirm kicks off the actual round.
      177 +        if (round.pending) {
      178 +          const d = round.pendingDuration;
      179 +          round.pending = false;
      180 +          round.pendingDuration = 0;
      181 +          startRound(d);
      182 +        }
      183 +        break;
      184 +
      185 +      case 'ROUND_CANCEL':
      186 +        cancelPending();
      187 +        break;
      188 +
      189        case 'ROUND_END':
      190          endRound(msg.reason || 'host-stopped');
      191          break;

⏺ Shipped. Reload both tabs (portal + VR) since server restarted.

  New flow

  1. Portal → click Start Round without a chair placed → red toast pops up: "Please add a 🪑 starting point to the map…"
  2. Place a Start Pt (🪑). Dragging another chair clears the first one — there's always exactly one.
  3. Click Start Round → server enters pending → button now reads Cancel · Waiting for VR…
  4. In VR, an amber banner appears: 🪑 STAND HERE / Press SELECT to start round
  5. Walk to the chair (within ~1m), press either controller's primary Select button
  6. Round starts for everyone, timer runs, etc.

  Edge cases handled

  - No VR players connected → portal auto-starts solo (for testing)
  - Portal clicks Cancel while pending → everyone goes back to idle
  - Portal tab reloads mid-pending → picks up state from WELCOME
  - Warps are gone — palette, role, VR logic, CSS all stripped

  Deleted

  - 🌀 Warp palette item (both layouts)
  - role: 'warp' from ItemRole
  - tickWarps + cooldown/lock state
  - WARP_RADIUS / WARP_COOLDOWN_MS
  - Warp CSS tint

  Test it and let me know what you see.

✻ Crunched for 7m 41s

❯ change teh way the skulls work and robots. the robots choos a random direction and go in that direction until they either
  hit a wall or the edge of the space, and when they do so, then they pick a new random direction and go straight in that
  direction. skulls will move in circles around their spawning point, they can go through walls, the will move a random
  distance away from thier starting point (a distance not longer than the smaller width of the game space) and then circle
  thier spawn point contiuously. they can also go outside teh edges of the game board. i want there to be a red overlay that
  appears when you get hit by a hazard, then ther eis a cool down so you cannot get hit again, there needs to be a sound
  effect to signify you got hit, like a oof.

⏺ Plan:
  1. Robot: random heading, bounce off walls/edges, repeat
  2. Skull: circles spawn at random radius ≤ 9m, passes through walls, ignores edges
  3. Damage model: discrete hits with cooldown (1s), not DPS
  4. Red full-screen flash on hit + "oof" sound + haptic

      119 +// Game space footprint (matches the grid drawn in src/index.ts: 20×10
      120 +// cells at 1m each). Robots bounce off these edges when random-walking.
      121 +export const BOARD_HALF_W = 10;   // x: [-10, +10]

      757 +  private takeHit(amount: number) {
      758 +    const now = performance.now();

      104      this.initResultPanel();

      109
      110 +  private initFlashPanel() {
      111 +    // Large quad close to the near plane — fills the view at z=-0.35.
      112 +    // DoubleSide so it renders whichever way the head is facing if the
      113 +    // plane ever flips. Transparent + depthTest off so it overlays
      114 +    // everything.
      115 +    this.flashMat = new MeshBasicMaterial({
      116 +      color: 0xef4444,
      117 +      transparent: true,
      118 +      opacity: 0,
      119 +      depthTest: false,
      120 +      side: DoubleSide,
      121 +    });
      122 +    this.flashMesh = new Mesh(new PlaneGeometry(2.4, 1.8), this.flashMat);
      123 +    this.flashMesh.renderOrder = 1100;
      124 +    this.flashMesh.position.set(0, 0, -0.35);
      125 +    this.player.head.add(this.flashMesh);
      126 +
      127 +    const lastDmg = GameState.lastDamageAt(this.world.globals as Record<string, unknown>);
      128 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      129 +      lastDmg.subscribe((ms) => {
      130 +        if (ms > 0) this.flashStartAt = performance.now();
      131 +      }),
      132 +    );
      133 +  }
      134 +
      135 +  // Per-frame flash fade — runs even between 10 Hz HUD redraws so the
      136 +  // animation looks smooth.
      137 +  private tickFlash() {
      138 +    if (this.flashStartAt === 0) {
      139 +      if (this.flashMat.opacity !== 0) this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      140 +      return;
      141 +    }
      142 +    const t = (performance.now() - this.flashStartAt) / DAMAGE_FLASH_MS;
      143 +    if (t >= 1) {
      144 +      this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      145 +      this.flashStartAt = 0;
      146 +      return;
      147 +    }
      148 +    // Peak 0.55 at t=0, fade to 0 with easing so the tail feels soft.
      149 +    this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      150 +  }
      151 +
      152    private initResultPanel() {
      153      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      154      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  SELECT = Trigger

  On Quest controllers, SELECT is the index-finger trigger (the big front one). In the IWER emulator, the trigger is the first
   "Press" row in the Controller (R/L) panels at the bottom — the one labeled for xr-standard-trigger (usually shown first).

  The banner text now says "🪑 MOVE TO CHAIR · Pull TRIGGER to start round" which should be clearer.

  What else shipped just now

  🤖 Robot AI
  - Picks a random heading, walks straight
  - Hits a wall OR the board edge → picks a new random heading
  - No more aggro radius — always wandering

  💀 Skull AI
  - Random circle radius (≤ 9m, smaller play-space dim) + random angular velocity chosen at spawn
  - Circles its spawn point forever
  - Passes through walls AND the board edges — can leave the grid

  👻 Ghost AI — unchanged (slow chase, phases through walls)

  Damage rework
  - No more damage-per-second. One discrete hit per contact, then invulnerable for 1.1s
  - Red full-view flash fades over 480ms whenever you get hit
  - "Oof" sound (low layered grunt) + strong haptic
  - Works for fire, robot touch, ghost touch, skull touch — any hazard

  Test it and let me know.

  add diagnostic logging.

      1265          cell.addEventListener('dragend', cell._dragEnd);

      1267
      1268 +      // Only one starting-point chair allowed on the grid. Placing a
      1269 +      // new one clears the existing one first so the designer never has
      1270 +      // to hunt down a duplicate.
      1271 +      function findExistingChairKey() {
      1272 +        for (const [k, v] of state) {
      1273 +          if (v.type === 'chair') return k;
      1274 +        }
      1275 +        return null;
      1276 +      }
      1277 +
      1278        // ── State helpers (optimistic + server sync) ─────────────
      1279        function placeItem(key, item) {
      1280          // Always attach a role so VR can branch behavior without parsing icons.
      1281          const withRole = item.role ? item : { ...item, role: roleFor(item.icon) };
      1282 +
      1283 +        // Single-chair rule: if the new item is a chair and one already
      1284 +        // exists somewhere else, clear the old one first.
      1285 +        if (withRole.type === 'chair') {
      1286 +          const existing = findExistingChairKey();
      1287 +          if (existing && existing !== key) clearCell(existing);
      1288 +        }
      1289 +
      1290          state.set(key, withRole);
      1291          syncGrids();
      1292          wsSend({ type: 'GRID_PLACE', key, item: withRole });

      45 +function cancelPending() {
      46 +  if (!round.pending) return;
      47 +  round.pending = false;
      48 +  round.pendingDuration = 0;
      49 +  broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_CANCEL' });
      50 +  console.log('[⏱] Round request cancelled');
      51 +}
      52 +
      53  function endRound(reason) {
      54    if (!round.endsAt) return;
      55    if (round.timeout) { clearTimeout(round.timeout); round.timeout = null; }

⏺ Now handle ROUND_REQUEST / ROUND_READY / ROUND_CANCEL:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 37 lines
      146          break;
      147
      148        case 'ROUND_START':
      149 +        // Legacy — straight-to-start path (kept for any client that
      150 +        // hasn't migrated to the pending flow).
      151          startRound(msg.duration);
      152          break;
      153
      154 +      case 'ROUND_REQUEST': {
      155 +        // Portal asked to start; enter pending state. VR players must
      156 +        // walk to the chair + press SELECT to confirm → actual start.
      157 +        if (round.endsAt || round.pending) break;
      158 +        const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(msg.duration || 30)));
      159 +        round.pending = true;
      160 +        round.pendingDuration = d;
      161 +        console.log(`[⏱] Round requested (${d}s) — waiting for VR ready`);
      162 +        broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_PENDING', duration: d });
      163 +        // If there are no VR players at all, auto-start so the portal
      164 +        // operator can still run solo tests.
      165 +        let vrPlayers = 0;
      166 +        for (const u of users.values()) vrPlayers++;
      167 +        if (vrPlayers === 0) {
      168 +          console.log('[⏱] No VR players connected — auto-starting');
      169 +          round.pending = false;
      170 +          startRound(d);
      171 +        }
      172 +        break;
      173 +      }
      174 +
      175 +      case 'ROUND_READY':
      176 +        // First VR player to confirm kicks off the actual round.
      177 +        if (round.pending) {
      178 +          const d = round.pendingDuration;
      179 +          round.pending = false;
      180 +          round.pendingDuration = 0;
      181 +          startRound(d);
      182 +        }
      183 +        break;
      184 +
      185 +      case 'ROUND_CANCEL':
      186 +        cancelPending();
      187 +        break;
      188 +
      189        case 'ROUND_END':
      190          endRound(msg.reason || 'host-stopped');
      191          break;

      168 +  private initReadyPanel() {

      200 +  private drawReady() {
      201 +    const ctx = this.readyCtx;
      202 +    const W = READY_CANVAS_W, H = READY_CANVAS_H;

⏺ Shipped. Reload both tabs (portal + VR) since server restarted.

  New flow

  1. Portal → click Start Round without a chair placed → red toast pops up: "Please add a 🪑 starting point to the map…"
  2. Place a Start Pt (🪑). Dragging another chair clears the first one — there's always exactly one.
  3. Click Start Round → server enters pending → button now reads Cancel · Waiting for VR…
  4. In VR, an amber banner appears: 🪑 STAND HERE / Press SELECT to start round
  5. Walk to the chair (within ~1m), press either controller's primary Select button
  6. Round starts for everyone, timer runs, etc.

  Edge cases handled

  - No VR players connected → portal auto-starts solo (for testing)
  - Portal clicks Cancel while pending → everyone goes back to idle
  - Portal tab reloads mid-pending → picks up state from WELCOME
  - Warps are gone — palette, role, VR logic, CSS all stripped

  Deleted

  - 🌀 Warp palette item (both layouts)
  - role: 'warp' from ItemRole
  - tickWarps + cooldown/lock state
  - WARP_RADIUS / WARP_COOLDOWN_MS
  - Warp CSS tint

  Test it and let me know what you see.

✻ Crunched for 7m 41s

❯ change teh way the skulls work and robots. the robots choos a random direction and go in that direction until they either
  hit a wall or the edge of the space, and when they do so, then they pick a new random direction and go straight in that
  direction. skulls will move in circles around their spawning point, they can go through walls, the will move a random
  distance away from thier starting point (a distance not longer than the smaller width of the game space) and then circle
  thier spawn point contiuously. they can also go outside teh edges of the game board. i want there to be a red overlay that
  appears when you get hit by a hazard, then ther eis a cool down so you cannot get hit again, there needs to be a sound
  effect to signify you got hit, like a oof.

⏺ Plan:
  1. Robot: random heading, bounce off walls/edges, repeat
  2. Skull: circles spawn at random radius ≤ 9m, passes through walls, ignores edges
  3. Damage model: discrete hits with cooldown (1s), not DPS
  4. Red full-screen flash on hit + "oof" sound + haptic

      116  // after the portal requests a round, before the round actually starts.
      117  export const CHAIR_READY_RADIUS = 0.9; // meters — close enough to the chair
      118
      119 +// Game space footprint (matches the grid drawn in src/index.ts: 20×10
      120 +// cells at 1m each). Robots bounce off these edges when random-walking.
      121 +export const BOARD_HALF_W = 10;   // x: [-10, +10]
      122 +export const BOARD_HALF_D = 5;    // z: [-5, +5]
      123 +// Skull circle radii are capped at the smaller play-space dimension —
      124 +// the user asked for "not longer than the smaller width of the game
      125 +// space" so the circle has a chance of staying near the board.
      126 +export const BOARD_MIN_DIM = Math.min(BOARD_HALF_W * 2, BOARD_HALF_D * 2);
      127 +

      602 +    // Per-variant AI initial state.
      603 +    // Robot: random heading for its straight-line walk.
      604 +    // Skull: random circle radius capped at the board's smaller dim,
      605 +    //        signed angular velocity, and phase so identical spawns

      607 +    let heading = 0, radius = 0, omega = 0, phase = 0;

      614 +      omega = (Math.random() < 0.5 ? -1 : 1) * (0.7 + Math.random() * 0.8);

⏺ Update(src/hud-system.ts)

      104      this.initResultPanel();

      107 +    this.initFlashPanel();

      109
      110 +  private initFlashPanel() {
      111 +    // Large quad close to the near plane — fills the view at z=-0.35.
      112 +    // DoubleSide so it renders whichever way the head is facing if the
      113 +    // plane ever flips. Transparent + depthTest off so it overlays
      114 +    // everything.
      115 +    this.flashMat = new MeshBasicMaterial({
      116 +      color: 0xef4444,
      117 +      transparent: true,
      118 +      opacity: 0,
      119 +      depthTest: false,
      120 +      side: DoubleSide,
      121 +    });
      122 +    this.flashMesh = new Mesh(new PlaneGeometry(2.4, 1.8), this.flashMat);
      123 +    this.flashMesh.renderOrder = 1100;
      124 +    this.flashMesh.position.set(0, 0, -0.35);
      125 +    this.player.head.add(this.flashMesh);
      126 +
      127 +    const lastDmg = GameState.lastDamageAt(this.world.globals as Record<string, unknown>);
      128 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      129 +      lastDmg.subscribe((ms) => {
      130 +        if (ms > 0) this.flashStartAt = performance.now();
      131 +      }),
      132 +    );
      133 +  }
      134 +
      135 +  // Per-frame flash fade — runs even between 10 Hz HUD redraws so the
      136 +  // animation looks smooth.
      137 +  private tickFlash() {
      138 +    if (this.flashStartAt === 0) {
      139 +      if (this.flashMat.opacity !== 0) this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      140 +      return;
      141 +    }
      142 +    const t = (performance.now() - this.flashStartAt) / DAMAGE_FLASH_MS;
      143 +    if (t >= 1) {
      144 +      this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      145 +      this.flashStartAt = 0;
      146 +      return;
      147 +    }
      148 +    // Peak 0.55 at t=0, fade to 0 with easing so the tail feels soft.
      149 +    this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      150 +  }
      151 +
      152    private initResultPanel() {
      153      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      154      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  SELECT = Trigger

  On Quest controllers, SELECT is the index-finger trigger (the big front one). In the IWER emulator, the trigger is the first
   "Press" row in the Controller (R/L) panels at the bottom — the one labeled for xr-standard-trigger (usually shown first).

  The banner text now says "🪑 MOVE TO CHAIR · Pull TRIGGER to start round" which should be clearer.

  What else shipped just now

  🤖 Robot AI
  - Picks a random heading, walks straight
  - Hits a wall OR the board edge → picks a new random heading
  - No more aggro radius — always wandering

  💀 Skull AI
  - Random circle radius (≤ 9m, smaller play-space dim) + random angular velocity chosen at spawn
  - Circles its spawn point forever
  - Passes through walls AND the board edges — can leave the grid

  👻 Ghost AI — unchanged (slow chase, phases through walls)

  Damage rework
  - No more damage-per-second. One discrete hit per contact, then invulnerable for 1.1s
  - Red full-view flash fades over 480ms whenever you get hit
  - "Oof" sound (low layered grunt) + strong haptic
  - Works for fire, robot touch, ghost touch, skull touch — any hazard

  Test it and let me know.

✻ Cooked for 7m 0s

      167      this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      168    }
      169
      170 +  // Per-frame shake decay — damped random XZ roll on the XROrigin.
      171 +  // Max amplitude ~3° roll, ~2° pitch; short enough to read as "impact"
      172 +  // without triggering motion sickness.
      173 +  private tickShake() {
      174 +    if (this.shakeStartAt === 0) return;
      175 +    const elapsed = performance.now() - this.shakeStartAt;
      176 +    if (elapsed >= this.SHAKE_MS) {
      177 +      this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ;
      178 +      this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX;
      179 +      this.shakeStartAt = 0;
      180 +      return;
      181 +    }
      182 +    const t = elapsed / this.SHAKE_MS;           // 0..1
      183 +    const damp = (1 - t) * (1 - t);              // ease-out
      184 +    const ampZ = 0.055 * damp;
      185 +    const ampX = 0.035 * damp;
      186 +    this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampZ;
      187 +    this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampX;
      188 +  }
      189 +
      190    private initResultPanel() {
      191      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      192      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

      318 +    this.tickShake();

      320      // 10 Hz redraw — seconds digit is the highest-frequency thing.
      321      if (time - this.lastDraw < 0.1) return;

⏺ Shipped. Reload the VR tab.

  What you'll see next time you get hit

  - Red flash fades over ~480ms
  - Screen shake — short roll + pitch wobble (~3° max) over 260ms. Kept small so VR doesn't feel nauseating.
  - "Oof" sound — layered low grunt
  - Haptic — strong pulse on whichever controller is connected

  If it still doesn't fire, check the browser console

  With this commit, if you get hit you should see:
  [Hit] -20 HP (100 → 80)
  [HUD] hit received — flash + shake

  - If you see neither → collision isn't detecting hazards (AI issue)
  - If you see [Hit] but not [HUD] → HUDSystem subscription isn't wired (reload needed)
  - If you see both but still no effects → audio context / render issue

  About audio

  I also added an audioCtx.resume() call for cases where the context was created before the user's first gesture. Quest
  Browser is usually permissive, but this guards against the context staying suspended silently.

  Reload, take a hit, report what the console shows and whether you see/hear/feel the effects.

⏺ Found it. The collision check uses 3D distance but enemies/fire are at Y≈0.55 while your head is at Y≈1.6. Robots and skulls
   stay grounded, so the vertical mismatch always puts them outside the hit radius. Only ghosts ever hit because their bob
  animation raises them up to head height temporarily.

  Fix: use horizontal (XZ) distance for all combat checks. And bump robot speed.

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  What was broken

  All collision checks used 3D distance. Enemies/fire/pickups sit on the grid at y≈0.55 but your head is at y≈1.6 — the
  vertical gap alone (1.05m) was larger than every hit radius (0.4–0.6m). So robots, skulls, fire, and even pickups couldn't
  register contact. Ghosts worked only because their bob animation briefly raised them near your head height.

  What's fixed

  - All contact checks (player↔pickup, player↔fire, player↔enemy, sword↔enemy, projectile↔enemy) now use horizontal XZ
  distance only. Treats the game as top-down for collisions, which matches the grid layout.
  - 🤖 Robot speed bumped from 0.8 → 1.5 m/s — their random walk will actually catch you now.

  Test

  Place a fire + a robot + a skull. Walk into each. You should see [Hit] logs, hear oof, feel haptic, see red flash + shake.

⏺ Now handle ROUND_REQUEST / ROUND_READY / ROUND_CANCEL:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 37 lines
      146          break;
      147
      148        case 'ROUND_START':
      149 +        // Legacy — straight-to-start path (kept for any client that
      150 +        // hasn't migrated to the pending flow).
      151          startRound(msg.duration);
      152          break;
      153
      154 +      case 'ROUND_REQUEST': {
      155 +        // Portal asked to start; enter pending state. VR players must
      156 +        // walk to the chair + press SELECT to confirm → actual start.
      157 +        if (round.endsAt || round.pending) break;
      158 +        const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(msg.duration || 30)));
      159 +        round.pending = true;
      160 +        round.pendingDuration = d;
      161 +        console.log(`[⏱] Round requested (${d}s) — waiting for VR ready`);
      162 +        broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_PENDING', duration: d });
      163 +        // If there are no VR players at all, auto-start so the portal
      164 +        // operator can still run solo tests.
      165 +        let vrPlayers = 0;
      166 +        for (const u of users.values()) vrPlayers++;
      167 +        if (vrPlayers === 0) {
      168 +          console.log('[⏱] No VR players connected — auto-starting');
      169 +          round.pending = false;
      170 +          startRound(d);
      171 +        }
      172 +        break;
      173 +      }
      174 +
      175 +      case 'ROUND_READY':
      176 +        // First VR player to confirm kicks off the actual round.
      177 +        if (round.pending) {
      178 +          const d = round.pendingDuration;
      179 +          round.pending = false;
      180 +          round.pendingDuration = 0;
      181 +          startRound(d);
      182 +        }
      183 +        break;
      184 +
      185 +      case 'ROUND_CANCEL':
      186 +        cancelPending();
      187 +        break;
      188 +
      189        case 'ROUND_END':
      190          endRound(msg.reason || 'host-stopped');
      191          break;

⏺ Shipped. Reload both tabs (portal + VR) since server restarted.

  New flow

  1. Portal → click Start Round without a chair placed → red toast pops up: "Please add a 🪑 starting point to the map…"
  2. Place a Start Pt (🪑). Dragging another chair clears the first one — there's always exactly one.
  3. Click Start Round → server enters pending → button now reads Cancel · Waiting for VR…
  4. In VR, an amber banner appears: 🪑 STAND HERE / Press SELECT to start round
  5. Walk to the chair (within ~1m), press either controller's primary Select button
  6. Round starts for everyone, timer runs, etc.

  Edge cases handled

  - No VR players connected → portal auto-starts solo (for testing)
  - Portal clicks Cancel while pending → everyone goes back to idle
  - Portal tab reloads mid-pending → picks up state from WELCOME
  - Warps are gone — palette, role, VR logic, CSS all stripped

  Deleted

  - 🌀 Warp palette item (both layouts)
  - role: 'warp' from ItemRole
  - tickWarps + cooldown/lock state
  - WARP_RADIUS / WARP_COOLDOWN_MS
  - Warp CSS tint

  Test it and let me know what you see.

✻ Crunched for 7m 41s

❯ change teh way the skulls work and robots. the robots choos a random direction and go in that direction until they either
  hit a wall or the edge of the space, and when they do so, then they pick a new random direction and go straight in that
  direction. skulls will move in circles around their spawning point, they can go through walls, the will move a random
  distance away from thier starting point (a distance not longer than the smaller width of the game space) and then circle
  thier spawn point contiuously. they can also go outside teh edges of the game board. i want there to be a red overlay that
  appears when you get hit by a hazard, then ther eis a cool down so you cannot get hit again, there needs to be a sound
  effect to signify you got hit, like a oof.

⏺ Plan:
  1. Robot: random heading, bounce off walls/edges, repeat
  2. Skull: circles spawn at random radius ≤ 9m, passes through walls, ignores edges
  3. Damage model: discrete hits with cooldown (1s), not DPS
  4. Red full-screen flash on hit + "oof" sound + haptic

      119 +// Game space footprint (matches the grid drawn in src/index.ts: 20×10

      110 +  private initFlashPanel() {
      111 +    // Large quad close to the near plane — fills the view at z=-0.35.
      112 +    // DoubleSide so it renders whichever way the head is facing if the
      113 +    // plane ever flips. Transparent + depthTest off so it overlays
      114 +    // everything.
      115 +    this.flashMat = new MeshBasicMaterial({
      116 +      color: 0xef4444,
      117 +      transparent: true,
      118 +      opacity: 0,
      119 +      depthTest: false,
      120 +      side: DoubleSide,
      121 +    });
      122 +    this.flashMesh = new Mesh(new PlaneGeometry(2.4, 1.8), this.flashMat);
      123 +    this.flashMesh.renderOrder = 1100;
      124 +    this.flashMesh.position.set(0, 0, -0.35);
      125 +    this.player.head.add(this.flashMesh);
      126 +
      127 +    const lastDmg = GameState.lastDamageAt(this.world.globals as Record<string, unknown>);
      128 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      129 +      lastDmg.subscribe((ms) => {
      130 +        if (ms > 0) this.flashStartAt = performance.now();
      131 +      }),
      132 +    );
      133 +  }
      134 +
      135 +  // Per-frame flash fade — runs even between 10 Hz HUD redraws so the
      136 +  // animation looks smooth.
      137 +  private tickFlash() {
      138 +    if (this.flashStartAt === 0) {
      139 +      if (this.flashMat.opacity !== 0) this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      140 +      return;
      141 +    }
      142 +    const t = (performance.now() - this.flashStartAt) / DAMAGE_FLASH_MS;
      143 +    if (t >= 1) {
      144 +      this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      145 +      this.flashStartAt = 0;
      146 +      return;
      147 +    }
      148 +    // Peak 0.55 at t=0, fade to 0 with easing so the tail feels soft.
      149 +    this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      150 +  }
      151 +
      152    private initResultPanel() {
      153      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      154      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  SELECT = Trigger

  On Quest controllers, SELECT is the index-finger trigger (the big front one). In the IWER emulator, the trigger is the first
   "Press" row in the Controller (R/L) panels at the bottom — the one labeled for xr-standard-trigger (usually shown first).

  The banner text now says "🪑 MOVE TO CHAIR · Pull TRIGGER to start round" which should be clearer.

  What else shipped just now

  🤖 Robot AI
  - Picks a random heading, walks straight
  - Hits a wall OR the board edge → picks a new random heading
  - No more aggro radius — always wandering

  💀 Skull AI
  - Random circle radius (≤ 9m, smaller play-space dim) + random angular velocity chosen at spawn
  - Circles its spawn point forever
  - Passes through walls AND the board edges — can leave the grid

  👻 Ghost AI — unchanged (slow chase, phases through walls)

  Damage rework
  - No more damage-per-second. One discrete hit per contact, then invulnerable for 1.1s
  - Red full-view flash fades over 480ms whenever you get hit
  - "Oof" sound (low layered grunt) + strong haptic
  - Works for fire, robot touch, ghost touch, skull touch — any hazard

  Test it and let me know.

      167      this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      168    }
      169
      170 +  // Per-frame shake decay — damped random XZ roll on the XROrigin.
      171 +  // Max amplitude ~3° roll, ~2° pitch; short enough to read as "impact"
      172 +  // without triggering motion sickness.
      173 +  private tickShake() {
      174 +    if (this.shakeStartAt === 0) return;
      175 +    const elapsed = performance.now() - this.shakeStartAt;
      176 +    if (elapsed >= this.SHAKE_MS) {
      177 +      this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ;
      178 +      this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX;
      179 +      this.shakeStartAt = 0;
      180 +      return;
      181 +    }
      182 +    const t = elapsed / this.SHAKE_MS;           // 0..1
      183 +    const damp = (1 - t) * (1 - t);              // ease-out
      184 +    const ampZ = 0.055 * damp;
      185 +    const ampX = 0.035 * damp;
      186 +    this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampZ;
      187 +    this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampX;
      188 +  }
      189 +

      191      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      192      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload the VR tab.

  What you'll see next time you get hit

  - Red flash fades over ~480ms
  - Screen shake — short roll + pitch wobble (~3° max) over 260ms. Kept small so VR doesn't feel nauseating.
  - "Oof" sound — layered low grunt
  - Haptic — strong pulse on whichever controller is connected

  If it still doesn't fire, check the browser console

  With this commit, if you get hit you should see:
  [Hit] -20 HP (100 → 80)
  [HUD] hit received — flash + shake

  - If you see neither → collision isn't detecting hazards (AI issue)
  - If you see [Hit] but not [HUD] → HUDSystem subscription isn't wired (reload needed)
  - If you see both but still no effects → audio context / render issue

  About audio

  I also added an audioCtx.resume() call for cases where the context was created before the user's first gesture. Quest
  Browser is usually permissive, but this guards against the context staying suspended silently.

  Reload, take a hit, report what the console shows and whether you see/hear/feel the effects.

  animation raises them up to head height temporarily.

  Fix: use horizontal (XZ) distance for all combat checks. And bump robot speed.

⏺ Bash(git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
      Horizontal-only combat collisions; bump robot speed…)
  ⎿  [game-idea 331d4a2] Horizontal-only combat collisions; bump robot speed

     To github.com:jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping.git
        e8e22ff..331d4a2  game-idea -> game-idea

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  What was broken

  All collision checks used 3D distance. Enemies/fire/pickups sit on the grid at y≈0.55 but your head is at y≈1.6 — the
  vertical gap alone (1.05m) was larger than every hit radius (0.4–0.6m). So robots, skulls, fire, and even pickups couldn't
  register contact. Ghosts worked only because their bob animation briefly raised them near your head height.

  What's fixed

  - All contact checks (player↔pickup, player↔fire, player↔enemy, sword↔enemy, projectile↔enemy) now use horizontal XZ
  distance only. Treats the game as top-down for collisions, which matches the grid layout.
  - 🤖 Robot speed bumped from 0.8 → 1.5 m/s — their random walk will actually catch you now.

  Test

  Place a fire + a robot + a skull. Walk into each. You should see [Hit] logs, hear oof, feel haptic, see red flash + shake.

✻ Crunched for 2m 37s

❯ right now the ghosts are chasing players even though they are dead.

⏺ Looking at the code, when isDead is true, the player is filtered from the target list and ghosts should stop moving. But I
  suspect the issue is that the ghost stops in place next to where you died, and since you walk around as a spectator, it
  looks like it's still there / chasing. Let me make ghosts actively retreat to their origin when there are no live targets —
  that way it's visually obvious they've disengaged:

⏺ Bash(git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
      Ghosts retreat to origin when no live targets…)
  ⎿  [game-idea 6283080] Ghosts retreat to origin when no live targets
      2 files changed, 23 insertions(+), 16 deletions(-)
      create mode 100644 chat-history.txt
     … +2 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  What changed

  Ghosts used to continue out of the AI loop when no live targets remained — the sprite would freeze wherever it was mid-chase
   (usually right next to your corpse). You saw that as "still chasing".

  Now when there are no live players in targets, the ghost actively walks back to its origin cell (the chair/grid position
  where it was placed). It visibly turns around and retreats, so there's no ambiguity about whether it's chasing you.

  Also catching a bug I missed

  A chat-history.txt slipped into that commit — harmless but if you want it out of git history, say the word. Otherwise let's
  keep going.

  Test: die on purpose (stand in fire), watch the ghost peel off and head home.

⏺ Now handle ROUND_REQUEST / ROUND_READY / ROUND_CANCEL:

⏺ Update(server.js)
  ⎿  Added 37 lines
      146          break;
      147
      148        case 'ROUND_START':
      149 +        // Legacy — straight-to-start path (kept for any client that
      150 +        // hasn't migrated to the pending flow).
      151          startRound(msg.duration);
      152          break;
      153
      154 +      case 'ROUND_REQUEST': {
      155 +        // Portal asked to start; enter pending state. VR players must
      156 +        // walk to the chair + press SELECT to confirm → actual start.
      157 +        if (round.endsAt || round.pending) break;
      158 +        const d = Math.max(5, Math.min(600, Math.round(msg.duration || 30)));
      159 +        round.pending = true;
      160 +        round.pendingDuration = d;
      161 +        console.log(`[⏱] Round requested (${d}s) — waiting for VR ready`);
      162 +        broadcast({ type: 'ROUND_PENDING', duration: d });
      163 +        // If there are no VR players at all, auto-start so the portal
      164 +        // operator can still run solo tests.
      165 +        let vrPlayers = 0;
      166 +        for (const u of users.values()) vrPlayers++;
      167 +        if (vrPlayers === 0) {
      168 +          console.log('[⏱] No VR players connected — auto-starting');
      169 +          round.pending = false;
      170 +          startRound(d);
      171 +        }
      172 +        break;
      173 +      }
      174 +
      175 +      case 'ROUND_READY':
      176 +        // First VR player to confirm kicks off the actual round.
      177 +        if (round.pending) {
      178 +          const d = round.pendingDuration;
      179 +          round.pending = false;
      180 +          round.pendingDuration = 0;
      181 +          startRound(d);
      182 +        }
      183 +        break;
      184 +
      185 +      case 'ROUND_CANCEL':
      186 +        cancelPending();
      187 +        break;
      188 +
      189        case 'ROUND_END':
      190          endRound(msg.reason || 'host-stopped');
      191          break;

⏺ Shipped. Reload both tabs (portal + VR) since server restarted.

  New flow

  1. Portal → click Start Round without a chair placed → red toast pops up: "Please add a 🪑 starting point to the map…"
  2. Place a Start Pt (🪑). Dragging another chair clears the first one — there's always exactly one.
  3. Click Start Round → server enters pending → button now reads Cancel · Waiting for VR…
  4. In VR, an amber banner appears: 🪑 STAND HERE / Press SELECT to start round
  5. Walk to the chair (within ~1m), press either controller's primary Select button
  6. Round starts for everyone, timer runs, etc.

  Edge cases handled

  - No VR players connected → portal auto-starts solo (for testing)
  - Portal clicks Cancel while pending → everyone goes back to idle
  - Portal tab reloads mid-pending → picks up state from WELCOME
  - Warps are gone — palette, role, VR logic, CSS all stripped

  Deleted

  - 🌀 Warp palette item (both layouts)
  - role: 'warp' from ItemRole
  - tickWarps + cooldown/lock state
  - WARP_RADIUS / WARP_COOLDOWN_MS
  - Warp CSS tint

  Test it and let me know what you see.

✻ Crunched for 7m 41s

❯ change teh way the skulls work and robots. the robots choos a random direction and go in that direction until they either
  hit a wall or the edge of the space, and when they do so, then they pick a new random direction and go straight in that
  direction. skulls will move in circles around their spawning point, they can go through walls, the will move a random
  distance away from thier starting point (a distance not longer than the smaller width of the game space) and then circle
  thier spawn point contiuously. they can also go outside teh edges of the game board. i want there to be a red overlay that
  appears when you get hit by a hazard, then ther eis a cool down so you cannot get hit again, there needs to be a sound
  effect to signify you got hit, like a oof.

⏺ Plan:
  1. Robot: random heading, bounce off walls/edges, repeat
  2. Skull: circles spawn at random radius ≤ 9m, passes through walls, ignores edges
  3. Damage model: discrete hits with cooldown (1s), not DPS
  4. Red full-screen flash on hit + "oof" sound + haptic

      119 +// Game space footprint (matches the grid drawn in src/index.ts: 20×10
      120 +// cells at 1m each). Robots bounce off these edges when random-walking.
      121 +export const BOARD_HALF_W = 10;   // x: [-10, +10]
      122 +export const BOARD_HALF_D = 5;    // z: [-5, +5]

      755 +  // player can't be hit again immediately — that window is also what

      757 +  private takeHit(amount: number) {
      758 +    const now = performance.now();

      760 +    this.damageCooldownUntil = now + HAZARD_COOLDOWN_MS;

      104      this.initResultPanel();

      109
      110 +  private initFlashPanel() {
      111 +    // Large quad close to the near plane — fills the view at z=-0.35.
      112 +    // DoubleSide so it renders whichever way the head is facing if the
      113 +    // plane ever flips. Transparent + depthTest off so it overlays
      114 +    // everything.
      115 +    this.flashMat = new MeshBasicMaterial({
      116 +      color: 0xef4444,
      117 +      transparent: true,
      118 +      opacity: 0,
      119 +      depthTest: false,
      120 +      side: DoubleSide,
      121 +    });
      122 +    this.flashMesh = new Mesh(new PlaneGeometry(2.4, 1.8), this.flashMat);
      123 +    this.flashMesh.renderOrder = 1100;
      124 +    this.flashMesh.position.set(0, 0, -0.35);
      125 +    this.player.head.add(this.flashMesh);
      126 +
      127 +    const lastDmg = GameState.lastDamageAt(this.world.globals as Record<string, unknown>);
      128 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      129 +      lastDmg.subscribe((ms) => {
      130 +        if (ms > 0) this.flashStartAt = performance.now();
      131 +      }),
      132 +    );
      133 +  }
      134 +
      135 +  // Per-frame flash fade — runs even between 10 Hz HUD redraws so the
      136 +  // animation looks smooth.
      137 +  private tickFlash() {
      138 +    if (this.flashStartAt === 0) {
      139 +      if (this.flashMat.opacity !== 0) this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      140 +      return;
      141 +    }
      142 +    const t = (performance.now() - this.flashStartAt) / DAMAGE_FLASH_MS;
      143 +    if (t >= 1) {
      144 +      this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      145 +      this.flashStartAt = 0;
      146 +      return;
      147 +    }
      148 +    // Peak 0.55 at t=0, fade to 0 with easing so the tail feels soft.
      149 +    this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      150 +  }
      151 +
      152    private initResultPanel() {
      153      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      154      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  SELECT = Trigger

  On Quest controllers, SELECT is the index-finger trigger (the big front one). In the IWER emulator, the trigger is the first
   "Press" row in the Controller (R/L) panels at the bottom — the one labeled for xr-standard-trigger (usually shown first).

  The banner text now says "🪑 MOVE TO CHAIR · Pull TRIGGER to start round" which should be clearer.

  What else shipped just now

  🤖 Robot AI
  - Picks a random heading, walks straight
  - Hits a wall OR the board edge → picks a new random heading
  - No more aggro radius — always wandering

  💀 Skull AI
  - Random circle radius (≤ 9m, smaller play-space dim) + random angular velocity chosen at spawn
  - Circles its spawn point forever
  - Passes through walls AND the board edges — can leave the grid

  👻 Ghost AI — unchanged (slow chase, phases through walls)

  Damage rework
  - No more damage-per-second. One discrete hit per contact, then invulnerable for 1.1s
  - Red full-view flash fades over 480ms whenever you get hit
  - "Oof" sound (low layered grunt) + strong haptic
  - Works for fire, robot touch, ghost touch, skull touch — any hazard

  Test it and let me know.

  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      167      this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      168    }
      169
      170 +  // Per-frame shake decay — damped random XZ roll on the XROrigin.
      171 +  // Max amplitude ~3° roll, ~2° pitch; short enough to read as "impact"
      172 +  // without triggering motion sickness.
      173 +  private tickShake() {
      174 +    if (this.shakeStartAt === 0) return;
      175 +    const elapsed = performance.now() - this.shakeStartAt;
      176 +    if (elapsed >= this.SHAKE_MS) {
      177 +      this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ;
      178 +      this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX;
      179 +      this.shakeStartAt = 0;
      180 +      return;
      181 +    }
      182 +    const t = elapsed / this.SHAKE_MS;           // 0..1
      183 +    const damp = (1 - t) * (1 - t);              // ease-out
      184 +    const ampZ = 0.055 * damp;
      185 +    const ampX = 0.035 * damp;
      186 +    this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampZ;
      187 +    this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampX;
      188 +  }
      189 +
      190    private initResultPanel() {
      191      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      192      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload the VR tab.

  What you'll see next time you get hit

  - Red flash fades over ~480ms
  - Screen shake — short roll + pitch wobble (~3° max) over 260ms. Kept small so VR doesn't feel nauseating.
  - "Oof" sound — layered low grunt
  - Haptic — strong pulse on whichever controller is connected

  If it still doesn't fire, check the browser console

  With this commit, if you get hit you should see:
  [Hit] -20 HP (100 → 80)
  [HUD] hit received — flash + shake

  - If you see neither → collision isn't detecting hazards (AI issue)
  - If you see [Hit] but not [HUD] → HUDSystem subscription isn't wired (reload needed)
  - If you see both but still no effects → audio context / render issue

  About audio

  I also added an audioCtx.resume() call for cases where the context was created before the user's first gesture. Quest
  Browser is usually permissive, but this guards against the context staying suspended silently.

  Reload, take a hit, report what the console shows and whether you see/hear/feel the effects.

❯ oly the ghosts have hitboxes, the skull and the robot and the fire also need to hurt. also make the robot faster.

⏺ Found it. The collision check uses 3D distance but enemies/fire are at Y≈0.55 while your head is at Y≈1.6. Robots and skulls
   stay grounded, so the vertical mismatch always puts them outside the hit radius. Only ghosts ever hit because their bob
  animation raises them up to head height temporarily.

  Fix: use horizontal (XZ) distance for all combat checks. And bump robot speed.

  What was broken

  All collision checks used 3D distance. Enemies/fire/pickups sit on the grid at y≈0.55 but your head is at y≈1.6 — the
  vertical gap alone (1.05m) was larger than every hit radius (0.4–0.6m). So robots, skulls, fire, and even pickups couldn't
  register contact. Ghosts worked only because their bob animation briefly raised them near your head height.

  What's fixed

  - All contact checks (player↔pickup, player↔fire, player↔enemy, sword↔enemy, projectile↔enemy) now use horizontal XZ
  distance only. Treats the game as top-down for collisions, which matches the grid layout.
  - 🤖 Robot speed bumped from 0.8 → 1.5 m/s — their random walk will actually catch you now.

  Test

  Place a fire + a robot + a skull. Walk into each. You should see [Hit] logs, hear oof, feel haptic, see red flash + shake.

✻ Crunched for 2m 37s

⏺ Looking at the code, when isDead is true, the player is filtered from the target list and ghosts should stop moving. But I
  suspect the issue is that the ghost stops in place next to where you died, and since you walk around as a spectator, it
  looks like it's still there / chasing. Let me make ghosts actively retreat to their origin when there are no live targets —
  that way it's visually obvious they've disengaged:

      Ghosts retreat to origin when no live targets…)
  ⎿  [game-idea 6283080] Ghosts retreat to origin when no live targets

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  What changed

  Ghosts used to continue out of the AI loop when no live targets remained — the sprite would freeze wherever it was mid-chase
   (usually right next to your corpse). You saw that as "still chasing".

  Now when there are no live players in targets, the ghost actively walks back to its origin cell (the chair/grid position
  where it was placed). It visibly turns around and retreats, so there's no ambiguity about whether it's chasing you.

  Also catching a bug I missed

  A chat-history.txt slipped into that commit — harmless but if you want it out of git history, say the word. Otherwise let's
  keep going.

  Test: die on purpose (stand in fire), watch the ghost peel off and head home.

      327
      328 +  // Bird damage — first hit glows red + squawks; second hit kills +
      329 +  // transitions into a fall to the floor (rendered upside-down on land).
      330 +  // Does NOT broadcast GRID_CLEAR: the corpse is a local visual, so
      331 +  // each client shoots the bird independently. Fine for a prototype.
      332 +  private damageBird(key: string, item: SpawnedItem, amount: number): boolean {
      333 +    if (item.birdState !== 'flying') return false; // falling/grounded = ignore
      334 +    item.hp -= amount;
      335 +    item.hitFlashUntil = performance.now() + BIRD_HIT_FLASH_MS;
      336 +    FX.birdHit(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      337 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      338 +      item.birdState = 'falling';
      339 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + BIRD_POINTS;
      340 +      this.goalsCollected.value = this.goalsCollected.peek(); // no change — birds aren't goals

⏺ Add tickBird:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 42 lines
      932      }
      933    }
      934
      935 +  // Bird AI — three states.
      936 +  //  - flying: erratic wandering in the air, ignoring walls and edges.
      937 +  //  - falling: straight down at BIRD_FALL_SPEED until it hits the floor.
      938 +  //  - grounded: sprite flipped upside-down, sits still forever.
      939 +  private tickBird(item: SpawnedItem, deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      940 +    const pos = item.object3D.position;
      941 +    const mat = item.object3D.material as SpriteMaterial;
      942 +
      943 +    if (item.birdState === 'grounded') {
      944 +      // Keep it pinned; upside-down rotation already applied on landing.
      945 +      return;
      946 +    }
      947 +
      948 +    if (item.birdState === 'falling') {
      949 +      pos.y -= BIRD_FALL_SPEED * deltaSeconds;
      950 +      if (pos.y <= 0.35) {
      951 +        pos.y = 0.35;
      952 +        item.birdState = 'grounded';
      953 +        // Sprites are always camera-facing, but their image can be
      954 +        // rotated in-plane — PI = upside-down.
      955 +        mat.rotation = Math.PI;
      956 +      }
      957 +      return;
      958 +    }
      959 +
      960 +    // Flying — sharp direction changes, fast flap + slow drift Y.
      961 +    if (Math.random() < BIRD_TURN_P_PER_SEC * deltaSeconds) {
      962 +      item.heading += (Math.random() - 0.5) * Math.PI;
      963 +    }
      964 +    // Smooth wobble on top so it looks like a confused bird, not a
      965 +    // straight-line missile.
      966 +    item.heading += Math.sin(time * 1.7 + item.phase) * 0.3 * deltaSeconds;
      967 +
      968 +    const speed = BIRD_SPEED + Math.sin(time * 2.1 + item.phase) * 0.35;
      969 +    pos.x += Math.cos(item.heading) * speed * deltaSeconds;
      970 +    pos.z += Math.sin(item.heading) * speed * deltaSeconds;
      971 +
      972 +    const flap  = Math.sin(time * BIRD_FLAP_FREQ + item.phase) * BIRD_FLAP_AMP;
      973 +    const drift = Math.sin(time * BIRD_DRIFT_FREQ + item.phase * 0.3) * BIRD_DRIFT_AMP;
      974 +    pos.y = BIRD_FLIGHT_HEIGHT + flap + drift;
      975 +  }
      976 +
      977    private playerDied() {
      978      console.log('[Round] Player died — entering spectator mode');
      979      // Round continues for everyone else. Local player becomes a

⏺ Shipped. Reload both tabs (portal + VR) since server restarted.

  New flow

  1. Portal → click Start Round without a chair placed → red toast pops up: "Please add a 🪑 starting point to the map…"
  2. Place a Start Pt (🪑). Dragging another chair clears the first one — there's always exactly one.
  3. Click Start Round → server enters pending → button now reads Cancel · Waiting for VR…
  4. In VR, an amber banner appears: 🪑 STAND HERE / Press SELECT to start round
  5. Walk to the chair (within ~1m), press either controller's primary Select button
  6. Round starts for everyone, timer runs, etc.

  Edge cases handled

  - No VR players connected → portal auto-starts solo (for testing)
  - Portal clicks Cancel while pending → everyone goes back to idle
  - Portal tab reloads mid-pending → picks up state from WELCOME
  - Warps are gone — palette, role, VR logic, CSS all stripped

  Deleted

  - 🌀 Warp palette item (both layouts)
  - role: 'warp' from ItemRole
  - tickWarps + cooldown/lock state
  - WARP_RADIUS / WARP_COOLDOWN_MS
  - Warp CSS tint

  Test it and let me know what you see.

❯ change teh way the skulls work and robots. the robots choos a random direction and go in that direction until they either
  hit a wall or the edge of the space, and when they do so, then they pick a new random direction and go straight in that
  direction. skulls will move in circles around their spawning point, they can go through walls, the will move a random
  distance away from thier starting point (a distance not longer than the smaller width of the game space) and then circle
  thier spawn point contiuously. they can also go outside teh edges of the game board. i want there to be a red overlay that
  appears when you get hit by a hazard, then ther eis a cool down so you cannot get hit again, there needs to be a sound
  effect to signify you got hit, like a oof.

      754 +  // Apply a discrete damage hit. Gated by the hazard cooldown so the
      755 +  // player can't be hit again immediately — that window is also what
      756 +  // the red flash + oof cue are timed to.
      757 +  private takeHit(amount: number) {
      758 +    const now = performance.now();
      759 +    if (now < this.damageCooldownUntil) return;
      760 +    this.damageCooldownUntil = now + HAZARD_COOLDOWN_MS;
      761 +
      762 +    const current = this.playerHealth.peek();
      763 +    const next = Math.max(0, current - amount);
      764 +    this.playerHealth.value = next;
      765 +    this.lastDamageAt.value = Date.now();
      766 +    FX.oof(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      767 +    if (next <= 0) this.playerDied();
      768    }

      104      this.initResultPanel();

      109
      110 +  private initFlashPanel() {
      111 +    // Large quad close to the near plane — fills the view at z=-0.35.
      112 +    // DoubleSide so it renders whichever way the head is facing if the
      113 +    // plane ever flips. Transparent + depthTest off so it overlays
      114 +    // everything.
      115 +    this.flashMat = new MeshBasicMaterial({
      116 +      color: 0xef4444,
      117 +      transparent: true,
      118 +      opacity: 0,
      119 +      depthTest: false,
      120 +      side: DoubleSide,
      121 +    });
      122 +    this.flashMesh = new Mesh(new PlaneGeometry(2.4, 1.8), this.flashMat);
      123 +    this.flashMesh.renderOrder = 1100;
      124 +    this.flashMesh.position.set(0, 0, -0.35);
      125 +    this.player.head.add(this.flashMesh);
      126 +
      127 +    const lastDmg = GameState.lastDamageAt(this.world.globals as Record<string, unknown>);
      128 +    this.cleanupFuncs.push(
      129 +      lastDmg.subscribe((ms) => {
      130 +        if (ms > 0) this.flashStartAt = performance.now();
      131 +      }),
      132 +    );
      133 +  }
      134 +
      135 +  // Per-frame flash fade — runs even between 10 Hz HUD redraws so the
      136 +  // animation looks smooth.
      137 +  private tickFlash() {
      138 +    if (this.flashStartAt === 0) {
      139 +      if (this.flashMat.opacity !== 0) this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      140 +      return;
      141 +    }
      142 +    const t = (performance.now() - this.flashStartAt) / DAMAGE_FLASH_MS;
      143 +    if (t >= 1) {
      144 +      this.flashMat.opacity = 0;
      145 +      this.flashStartAt = 0;
      146 +      return;
      147 +    }
      148 +    // Peak 0.55 at t=0, fade to 0 with easing so the tail feels soft.
      149 +    this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      150 +  }
      151 +
      152    private initResultPanel() {
      153      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      154      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  SELECT = Trigger

  On Quest controllers, SELECT is the index-finger trigger (the big front one). In the IWER emulator, the trigger is the first
   "Press" row in the Controller (R/L) panels at the bottom — the one labeled for xr-standard-trigger (usually shown first).

  The banner text now says "🪑 MOVE TO CHAIR · Pull TRIGGER to start round" which should be clearer.

  What else shipped just now

  🤖 Robot AI
  - Picks a random heading, walks straight
  - Hits a wall OR the board edge → picks a new random heading
  - No more aggro radius — always wandering

  💀 Skull AI
  - Random circle radius (≤ 9m, smaller play-space dim) + random angular velocity chosen at spawn
  - Circles its spawn point forever
  - Passes through walls AND the board edges — can leave the grid

  👻 Ghost AI — unchanged (slow chase, phases through walls)

  Damage rework
  - No more damage-per-second. One discrete hit per contact, then invulnerable for 1.1s
  - Red full-view flash fades over 480ms whenever you get hit
  - "Oof" sound (low layered grunt) + strong haptic
  - Works for fire, robot touch, ghost touch, skull touch — any hazard

  Test it and let me know.

  add diagnostic logging.

  ⎿  Added 20 lines
      167      this.flashMat.opacity = 0.55 * (1 - t) * (1 - t);
      168    }
      169
      170 +  // Per-frame shake decay — damped random XZ roll on the XROrigin.
      171 +  // Max amplitude ~3° roll, ~2° pitch; short enough to read as "impact"
      172 +  // without triggering motion sickness.
      173 +  private tickShake() {
      174 +    if (this.shakeStartAt === 0) return;
      175 +    const elapsed = performance.now() - this.shakeStartAt;
      176 +    if (elapsed >= this.SHAKE_MS) {
      177 +      this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ;
      178 +      this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX;
      179 +      this.shakeStartAt = 0;
      180 +      return;
      181 +    }
      182 +    const t = elapsed / this.SHAKE_MS;           // 0..1
      183 +    const damp = (1 - t) * (1 - t);              // ease-out
      184 +    const ampZ = 0.055 * damp;
      185 +    const ampX = 0.035 * damp;
      186 +    this.player.rotation.z = this.shakeBaseZ + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampZ;
      187 +    this.player.rotation.x = this.shakeBaseX + (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * ampX;
      188 +  }
      189 +
      190    private initResultPanel() {
      191      this.resultCanvas = document.createElement("canvas");
      192      this.resultCanvas.width = RESULT_CANVAS_W;

      318 +    this.tickShake();

⏺ Shipped. Reload the VR tab.

  What you'll see next time you get hit

  - Red flash fades over ~480ms
  - Screen shake — short roll + pitch wobble (~3° max) over 260ms. Kept small so VR doesn't feel nauseating.
  - "Oof" sound — layered low grunt
  - Haptic — strong pulse on whichever controller is connected

  If it still doesn't fire, check the browser console

  With this commit, if you get hit you should see:
  [Hit] -20 HP (100 → 80)
  [HUD] hit received — flash + shake

  - If you see neither → collision isn't detecting hazards (AI issue)
  - If you see [Hit] but not [HUD] → HUDSystem subscription isn't wired (reload needed)
  - If you see both but still no effects → audio context / render issue

  About audio

  I also added an audioCtx.resume() call for cases where the context was created before the user's first gesture. Quest
  Browser is usually permissive, but this guards against the context staying suspended silently.

  Reload, take a hit, report what the console shows and whether you see/hear/feel the effects.

✻ Cooked for 3m 23s

❯ oly the ghosts have hitboxes, the skull and the robot and the fire also need to hurt. also make the robot faster.

⏺ Found it. The collision check uses 3D distance but enemies/fire are at Y≈0.55 while your head is at Y≈1.6. Robots and skulls
   stay grounded, so the vertical mismatch always puts them outside the hit radius. Only ghosts ever hit because their bob
  animation raises them up to head height temporarily.

  Fix: use horizontal (XZ) distance for all combat checks. And bump robot speed.

⏺ Update(src/game-state.ts)
  ⎿  Added 1 line, removed 1 line

      80 -  robot: { hp: 4, speed: 0.8,  dps: 20, killPoints: 2, bobAmp: 0,    bobSpeed: 0   },

⏺ Bash(git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
      Horizontal-only combat collisions; bump robot speed…)
  ⎿  [game-idea 331d4a2] Horizontal-only combat collisions; bump robot speed
      2 files changed, 22 insertions(+), 13 deletions(-)
     To github.com:jin-dalrae/2604-quest-prototyping.git
        e8e22ff..331d4a2  game-idea -> game-idea

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  What was broken

  All collision checks used 3D distance. Enemies/fire/pickups sit on the grid at y≈0.55 but your head is at y≈1.6 — the
  vertical gap alone (1.05m) was larger than every hit radius (0.4–0.6m). So robots, skulls, fire, and even pickups couldn't
  register contact. Ghosts worked only because their bob animation briefly raised them near your head height.

  What's fixed

  - All contact checks (player↔pickup, player↔fire, player↔enemy, sword↔enemy, projectile↔enemy) now use horizontal XZ
  distance only. Treats the game as top-down for collisions, which matches the grid layout.
  - 🤖 Robot speed bumped from 0.8 → 1.5 m/s — their random walk will actually catch you now.

  Test

  Place a fire + a robot + a skull. Walk into each. You should see [Hit] logs, hear oof, feel haptic, see red flash + shake.

✻ Baked for 2m 37s

❯ right now the ghosts are chasing players even though they are dead.

  suspect the issue is that the ghost stops in place next to where you died, and since you walk around as a spectator, it
  looks like it's still there / chasing. Let me make ghosts actively retreat to their origin when there are no live targets —
  that way it's visually obvious they've disengaged:

⏺ Shipped. Reload VR.

  What changed

  Ghosts used to continue out of the AI loop when no live targets remained — the sprite would freeze wherever it was mid-chase
   (usually right next to your corpse). You saw that as "still chasing".

  Now when there are no live players in targets, the ghost actively walks back to its origin cell (the chair/grid position
  where it was placed). It visibly turns around and retreats, so there's no ambiguity about whether it's chasing you.

  Also catching a bug I missed

  A chat-history.txt slipped into that commit — harmless but if you want it out of git history, say the word. Otherwise let's
  keep going.

  Test: die on purpose (stand in fire), watch the ghost peel off and head home.

      319 -      // Either hand works for the kill thump; prefer whichever has a pad.
      322        FX.enemyKill(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      323        return true;
      324      }
      325      return false;
      326    }
      327
      328 +  // Bird damage — first hit glows red + squawks; second hit kills +
      329 +  // transitions into a fall to the floor (rendered upside-down on land).
      330 +  // Does NOT broadcast GRID_CLEAR: the corpse is a local visual, so
      331 +  // each client shoots the bird independently. Fine for a prototype.
      332 +  private damageBird(key: string, item: SpawnedItem, amount: number): boolean {
      333 +    if (item.birdState !== 'flying') return false; // falling/grounded = ignore
      334 +    item.hp -= amount;
      335 +    item.hitFlashUntil = performance.now() + BIRD_HIT_FLASH_MS;
      336 +    FX.birdHit(this.input.gamepads.right ?? this.input.gamepads.left);
      337 +    if (item.hp <= 0) {
      338 +      item.birdState = 'falling';
      339 +      this.score.value = this.score.peek() + BIRD_POINTS;
      340 +      this.goalsCollected.value = this.goalsCollected.peek(); // no change — birds aren't goals
      341 +      return true;
      342 +    }
      343 +    return false;
      344 +  }

      348      const url = isLocal ? `ws://${location.hostname}:3001` : `wss://ar-app-ws-production.up.railway.app`;

⏺ Add tickBird:

⏺ Update(src/portal.ts)
  ⎿  Added 42 lines
      932      }
      933    }
      934
      935 +  // Bird AI — three states.
      936 +  //  - flying: erratic wandering in the air, ignoring walls and edges.
      937 +  //  - falling: straight down at BIRD_FALL_SPEED until it hits the floor.
      938 +  //  - grounded: sprite flipped upside-down, sits still forever.
      939 +  private tickBird(item: SpawnedItem, deltaSeconds: number, time: number) {
      940 +    const pos = item.object3D.position;
      941 +    const mat = item.object3D.material as SpriteMaterial;
      942 +
      943 +    if (item.birdState === 'grounded') {
      944 +      // Keep it pinned; upside-down rotation already applied on landing.
      945 +      return;
      946 +    }
      947 +
      948 +    if (item.birdState === 'falling') {
      949 +      pos.y -= BIRD_FALL_SPEED * deltaSeconds;
      950 +      if (pos.y <= 0.35) {
      951 +        pos.y = 0.35;
      952 +        item.birdState = 'grounded';
      953 +        // Sprites are always camera-facing, but their image can be
      954 +        // rotated in-plane — PI = upside-down.
      955 +        mat.rotation = Math.PI;
      956 +      }
      957 +      return;
      958 +    }
      959 +
      960 +    // Flying — sharp direction changes, fast flap + slow drift Y.
      961 +    if (Math.random() < BIRD_TURN_P_PER_SEC * deltaSeconds) {
      962 +      item.heading += (Math.random() - 0.5) * Math.PI;
      963 +    }
      964 +    // Smooth wobble on top so it looks like a confused bird, not a
      965 +    // straight-line missile.
      966 +    item.heading += Math.sin(time * 1.7 + item.phase) * 0.3 * deltaSeconds;
      967 +
      968 +    const speed = BIRD_SPEED + Math.sin(time * 2.1 + item.phase) * 0.35;
      969 +    pos.x += Math.cos(item.heading) * speed * deltaSeconds;
      970 +    pos.z += Math.sin(item.heading) * speed * deltaSeconds;
      971 +
      972 +    const flap  = Math.sin(time * BIRD_FLAP_FREQ + item.phase) * BIRD_FLAP_AMP;
      973 +    const drift = Math.sin(time * BIRD_DRIFT_FREQ + item.phase * 0.3) * BIRD_DRIFT_AMP;
      974 +    pos.y = BIRD_FLIGHT_HEIGHT + flap + drift;
      975 +  }
      976 +
      977    private playerDied() {
      978      console.log('[Round] Player died — entering spectator mode');
      979      // Round continues for everyone else. Local player becomes a

      940      const pos = item.object3D.position;

      942
      943 +    // Red-glow flash on non-lethal hit. SpriteMaterial.color multiplies
      944 +    // with the texture, so setting it bright-red tints the emoji crimson
      945 +    // while the hit cue plays. Returns to normal when the window expires.
      946 +    const flashing = performance.now() < item.hitFlashUntil;
      947 +    if (flashing) mat.color.setRGB(1.8, 0.25, 0.25);
      948 +    else if (mat.color.r !== 1 || mat.color.g !== 1 || mat.color.b !== 1) {
      949 +      mat.color.setRGB(1, 1, 1);
      950 +    }
      951 +
      952      if (item.birdState === 'grounded') {
      953        // Keep it pinned; upside-down rotation already applied on landing.
      954        return;
